entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Feathren Ancestry
entries: Your unique mix of avian and feline ancestry grants you supernatural abilities. Use the Feathren Ancestry tables to determine your combination of physical features, choosing one Avian option and one Feline option.

You know the cantrip corresponding to your avian ancestry and the {@spell Identify|XPHB} spell. At character level 3, you learn the spell corresponding to your feline ancestry. At character level 5, you learn the {@spell Enhance Ability|XPHB} spell. You always have these spells prepared. You can cast each spell once without a spell slot or Material component, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast the spells using any spell slots you have of an appropriate level.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for the spells you cast with this trait (choose the ability when you select the ancestries).

entries.name: Kindred Speech
entries: You can communicate simple ideas to birds and cats that are Large or smaller, including griffons. They can understand your words, though you have no special ability to understand them in return. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Animal Handling|XPHB}) checks and Charisma checks you make to influence them.

entries.name: Natural Creator
entries: You are imbued with a mote of the astral griffon's creative essence. You have proficiency with two {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice, and you can gain proficiency with a new tool in half the normal amount of time.

entries.name: Talons
entries: When you use your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} to deal damage with your talons, it deals {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage instead of the usual Bludgeoning damage.

Reil's talons moved quickly across the surface, tearing through concepts with her charcoal on the paper as easily as flesh. Her piercing eyes darted about the page in search of ideas to explore. Finding none, she moved to a new sheet and put the old one atop the stack to the side.

She stared at the now empty parchment in front of her and twirled the stick of charcoal in her hand. Her feathers were ruffled and the rings on her swinging tail clinked softly as they moved back and forth in {@status Concentration|XPHB}.

The sound of hammers against metal could be heard from the workshop below, resonating with a familiar rhythm and ring that matched the pace of her thought, charcoal, and tail. She was a pendulum in motion: a clock that counted down to the stroke of brilliance, and when it rang out, the satisfied trills of victory would echo throughout the chamber.

Feathren are the representation of something new from something old. A step beyond their ancestral griffon heritage, these bipedal creatures celebrate their individuality amidst the cries and jeers from common folk. Originally born from the Astral Plane, where all things can be lost, found, and remade, feathren now walk the Material Plane in search of new ideas, materials, and direction. Filled with endless curiosity, many make their way through the world as adventurers and artisans, taking care to learn what they can from their experiences before moving on to the next one.

entries.name: Varied Heritage
entries: Feathren unite remarkable aspects of two otherwise disparate creatures. Their strong legs are feline, while their chest, arms, and head are avian. Despite their distant relation to griffons, feathren stand upright and can use their sharp talons to manipulate objects as deftly as any other humanoid. Their voices often bear some birdlike resemblance to their ancestry and contain the occasional trill, coo, or caw.

Few feathren look the same. Their fur, plumage, and build can vary widely, forming unique combinations of origin and aptitude. While some feathren are born with the body of an eagle and lion, others combine the likes of owls and crows with tigers and leopards. Feathren find particular pleasure in jewelry and decoration—an inescapable trait from their heritage. Without wings to embellish, most turn to adorning themselves with baubles and rings on their talons, feathered ears, and tail. Others enjoy body art, such as tattoos, paints, and various unique means of self-expression. Most feathren struggle to resist the urge to preen or adjust their worn finery whenever they catch a glimpse of their reflection.

entries.name: Confident and Curious
entries: Born with the innate gifts of two naturally skilled creatures, feathren have a shared sense of confidence in their abilities. Feathren spend their lives learning and mastering new trades to sate their curiosity, preferring to answer a puzzle or question on their own rather than ask for help. Many feathren find camaraderie among those that share a common passion, and while they're remiss to turn to peers for help, they enjoy the sharing of stories and conversation about their interests.

entries.name: Feathren Names
entries: As is their nature, feathren enjoy experimenting with names and playing with the combinations they can make with them. Feathren are given a name at birth that's used as a first name, but give themselves new surnames as they grow.

Throughout a feathren's lifespan, they may go through as many as twenty surnames: some may last for years, while others are kept for mere weeks. These names are typically combinations of words that describe them and act as either loose titles or personal mantras, and can come from a variety of languages.

items.name: Male Names:
items.entry: Acero, Astraios, Caicias, Cleve, Fhyriad, Gyrin, Iapet, Ipsideo, Jurat, Klost, Lostro, Quyll, Ronta, Strey, Untreyo, Yulla, Zwayne

items.name: Female Names:
items.entry: Accra, Axellia, Coora, Ereya, Finneli, Fuli, Herata, Hyla, Juip, Leies, Lucine, Lustra, Monit, Omi, Parra, Reyda, Velrin

items.name: Example Surnames:
items.entry: Birthwoven, Crownfeather, Downy, Gemcrest, Ghostwing, Goldtail, Mistwalker, Ricket, Rightedge, Starborn, Wavecrushed

entries.name: Friends, Foes, and Feathren
entries: Despite their intelligence, many people see feathren as little more than walking, talking griffons. As a result, they're often treated with the same caution their large ancestors receive, but unless given a reason to be afraid, most people will simply steal glances at them from across the way or avoid them all together. Someone familiar with griffons can be as likely to belittle a feathren as they are to befriend them, depending on that person's history.

Those that see past a feathren's ancestry find themselves with a lasting companion. Dragonborn, dwarves, gnomes, and half-elves in particular are likely to be befriended by feathren, who see them as either natural creative peers or kindred spirits caught between worlds. Half-orcs and tieflings can spark this same sense of camaraderie, but often lack the creative drive that can cement a lifelong friendship.

item.name: Abjurer's Gilder
entries: This golden badge is usually given to Wizards who either excel at or have mastered Abjuration magic as a symbol of their accomplishments. While wearing this badge, you always have the {@spell Shield|XPHB} spell prepared if it's in your spellbook, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can have prepared. In addition, when you are hit by an attack roll or targeted by the {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB} spell, you can take a special {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, instead of your normal one, to cast {@spell Shield|XPHB}. This special {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} can be used before or after your normal {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} for the round, but it can't be used on the same turn as your normal {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. Once you take this special {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} you can't do so again until the start of your next turn.

item.name: Aegis of Radiance
entries: This Shield emits a faint, holy glow when wielded and recognizes your devotion to a higher power. When you attune to the Shield, the emblem adorning its face changes to one that represents your chosen deity, allowing you to use the Shield as a {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB}. You gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} while holding this Shield, in addition to the Shield's normal bonus to AC.

While holding the Shield, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and use a command word to cause it to magically shatter into countless fragments of pure light. The pieces of light protect you as if you were wielding the Shield, leaving your hands free. The light surrounds you for 1 minute, until you dismiss the effect as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, or until you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition or die, at which point the Shield rematerializes on the ground at your feet or strapped to your arm if you have a hand free. If your {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB} is emblazoned upon or attached to the Shield, it floats prominently amidst the shards of light and continues to function as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for you. Undead creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls made against you while you're surrounded by the light in this way. The surrounding shards of light shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Amulet of Equilibrium
entries: This spiraling pendant is made of three magic-sensitive panes of colorful crystal. Each piece of crystal is attuned to a different arcane element. If you can cast spells, you can use the amulet as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} while you're attuned to it.

The amulet has 4 charges and regains 2 expended charges daily at dawn. When you deal Cold, Fire, or Lightning damage to only one creature while wearing the amulet, you can expend any number of charges to reroll the same number of damage dice. You must use the new rolls.
Whenever you take Cold, Fire, or Lightning damage from an enemy, the amulet regains 1 expended charge.

item.name: Arcane Mirror
entries: This mirror-like Shield is enchanted with Abjuration magic that can reflect spells back at their source. While holding the Shield, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you're hit by an attack to cast the {@spell Shield|XPHB} spell from it. You can't use this property again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

The Shield has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. When you succeed on a saving throw against a spell that targets only you or a spell attack roll misses you, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to learn the level of the triggering spell. As part of this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you can expend a number of the Shield's charges equal to the spell's level to reflect that spell back at its source; you can't reflect a spell of level 7 or higher in this way. When you do, the caster of the spell must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the caster takes {@damage 1d8} Radiant damage for each charge you expended as a bolt of reflected arcane energy hits it. On a successful save, the caster takes half as much damage. If you reflect a spell in this way that would cause you to take half damage on a successful save, you take no damage instead.

item.name: Arcanist's Bowstaff
item.reqAttune: by a Creature with an Intelligence of 17+
entries: When you attune to this {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}, it remains solid in your grip but feels pliable within your mind. Whenever you make an attack with this magic weapon, you can choose to make the attack at range. When you do, you use your mind to telekinetically curve the {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} into the form of a {@item Longbow|XPHB}. While using it in this form, you're proficient with the {@item Longbow|XPHB} and it doesn't have the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|Heavy} property. The bow is strung with an arcane energy that arcs between the rings on either end of the weapon. A glowing, magical arrow forms whenever the arcing energy is drawn back and fired. After the arrow hits or misses its target, it disappears and the {@item Longbow|XPHB} reverts to its {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} form. This {@item Longbow|XPHB} can only use the ammunition that it generates.
You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and you can use your Intelligence modifier instead of Strength or Dexterity for those rolls.

In addition, this {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. Immediately after you make an attack roll with this weapon as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB} spell from it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. When you do, the darts are fired from either the {@item Longbow|XPHB} or the {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}'s embossed faces.

item.name: Architect's Disdain
entries: This {@item adamantine War Pick|XDMG} is specially designed to tear down walls. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Whenever the {@item War Pick|XPHB} hits an object or structure, the hit is a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. In addition, if you hit a magically created object or structure, such as a {@spell Wall of Ice|XPHB}, with the {@item War Pick|XPHB}, you can choose to dispel that object or structure as if by the {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} spell (level 6 version). This effect fails if the spell or magical effect that created the object or structure was cast with a level 7+ spell slot. Once the {@item War Pick|XPHB} has dispelled an object or structure in this way, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Astral Sea Piercer
entries: This golden {@item War Pick|XPHB} was forged in the depths of the Astral Plane and leaves a faint trail of extraplanar energy behind it when swung. You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and targets hit by it take an extra {@damage 1d4} Force damage. In addition, while you're in the Astral Plane, you always know the direction to the nearest portal to another plane of existence.

entries.name: Portal
entries: This weapon has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d3 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend 1 charge to slam the {@item War Pick|XPHB} into any flat, solid surface at least 5 feet in each dimension. When you do, you magically create a small planar rift, which fades after 1 minute. If you expend another charge in this way while there's another such rift within 300 feet of you, the two rifts tear open to form a portal between them. The portal lasts until the end of your next turn or until you close it early (no action required). When the portal closes, the rifts fade. Medium and smaller creatures and objects can move or interact freely through the portal while it's open, carrying with them any momentum they had through to the other side.

Both rifts must be placed on the same plane of existence, and if you create a second rift more than 300 feet away from the first, the first one fades. Rifts can be entered from any direction to travel through the portal. When you enter a rift in this way, you reappear 5 feet in front of the other rift.

In addition, you can expend 1 of the {@item War Pick|XPHB}'s charges when you hit a Small or larger creature with it. When you do, that creature takes an extra {@damage 1d8} Force damage as a rift appears on its body. Rifts that appear on a creature in this way are attached to the creature and move with it until a portal is opened. When a portal opens between two rifts and at least one of the rifts is attached to a creature, the attached creature or creatures must succeed on a DC 17 Charisma saving throw or be immediately shunted through the portal to the other rift's location. Large and larger creatures automatically succeed on this saving throw.

item.name: Aurum and Argentum
entries: This gold and silver set of Daggers seem ordinary when used on their own but become magical when used together. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with these magic weapons while you hold one of them in each hand. When you make an extra attack as a result of using the Daggers' {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack if you aren't already adding it to the damage.

In addition, if you hit the same target with both Daggers in the same round, it takes an extra {@damage 1d4} Piercing damage from the second attack and can't make {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} until the end of your turn.

item.name: Avian Circlet
entries: This ornate silver tiara grants you power over certain flying creatures. While wearing the circlet, you can cast {@spell Animal Messenger|XPHB} or Speak with Animals from it. A target isn't affected by these spells if it doesn't have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}.

In addition, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to touch a willing Beast with a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}. For 1 minute, you can see through the target's eyes as well as your own. While seeing through the target's eyes, you benefit from any special visual senses it has. You can end this effect early (no action required). Once this property is used, it can't be used again until this next dawn.

item.name: Axe Beak Tomahawk
entries: This magic {@item Handaxe|XPHB} is made from the remains of a slain axe beak. The first time on each of your turns that you hit a creature that has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} with the axe, that creature must make a DC 13 Strength saving throw. Each time that a target fails this save, its {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 15 feet for 1 minute.

item.name: Bag of Sundrops
entries: This small, colorful bag is filled with {@dice 1d6 + 4} golden, marble-like orbs, called sundrops, that glow softly when held. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can throw a sundrop at a point you can see within 30 feet of you. When you do, the orb explodes into a dazzling pillar of light at that point, which lasts until the start of your next turn. The pillar is a 5-foot-radius, 40-foot-high {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} of {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} centered on the point where the sundrop shattered. The light is sunlight.
A {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} spell targeting the light ends the effect early.

item.name: Band of Mirrored Essence
entries: This unassuming platinum ring is polished to a mirror finish. When you reduce a Small or larger enemy to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} while wearing the ring, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cause the ring to capture a portion of that creature's essence. Looking into the ring shows the reflection of the creature whose essence is held inside. Once you capture an essence with the ring, it can't do so again for {@dice 1d8} hours. The ring can only have one essence at a time. If you capture an essence when the ring already has one, you replace the original.
As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can release the captured essence and assume either the creature's appearance or one of its features, as detailed below.

entries.name: Assume Appearance
entries: The creature must be no more than one size larger or smaller than you for you to take on its appearance. When you do, you don't physically change in size or shape, but your body is shrouded in a convincing illusion. Your voice changes to match the creature's, and you can understand any of its known languages but can neither read nor speak any language that you don't already know. Another creature can see through the illusion by succeeding on a DC 16 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check.

Physical interaction with this illusory form reveals it to be an illusion, because things can pass through it. The illusion lasts for 8 hours. It ends early if you dismiss it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} or if you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition or die.

entries.name: Assume Feature
entries: If you assume one of the creature's features, your body or mind temporarily changes to accommodate the new ability. You can choose to gain one skill or saving throw proficiency, {@variantrule Damage|XPHB} {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}, {@variantrule Condition|XPHB} {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}, special sense, or language known that the creature had. If the creature could breathe underwater, you can choose to gain that feature instead. You retain this feature for 8 hours. You lose it early if you dismiss it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} or if you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition or die.

item.name: Bands of the Found and Lost
entries: The compass-like mechanisms on top of this pair of matching leather bracelets remain motionless until each bracelet is worn by a different creature. While they're worn, each compass points in the direction of the other bracelet. It doesn't indicate height or depth. The compasses spin wildly if the two bands are worn on different planes of existence.

item.name: Bard-in-a-Box
entries: This small music box can play songs upon request. While holding it, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to command the box to magically play a song for which it has music, causing the contraption to emit a slightly tinny rendition of the musical number. The box can know the music for up to five songs at a time but can't recreate lyrics or other creature sounds. The box has a knob on its side to adjust its volume, allowing it to be as quiet as a whisper or as loud as a scream.

Sheet music can be fed into the box through a narrow slot along the bottom of the device. Sheets must be labeled with a song title, page number, and instrument in order for the box to learn the music. Sheets failing to meet these requirements cause the box to make a sad, discordant noise before being spat back out. Once a sheet is successfully fed into the box, it is consumed and can't be retrieved.
If the box already knows five songs and a new sheet of music is successfully fed into it, it quickly plays several bars of the oldest song it knows before it slows to a stop and forgets the music.

item.name: Barge Helm
entries: This flat, rugged helmet lets you run headlong into targets and bowl them over with abandon. If you move at least 20 feet in a straight line toward a target immediately before successfully shoving it, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Bludgeoning damage from being barreled into by the helmet.

item.name: Barricade Shield
entries: While holding this sturdy iron Shield, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to plant it on the ground and expand its height and width to stretch up to 15 feet in either direction, potentially becoming a defensive wall or similar platform. The edges of the Shield magically conform to fit the shape of an enclosed space within its expanded size, such as the walls of a tunnel or hallway. While holding the expanded Shield in place, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} becomes 0, and you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity checks and Dexterity saving throws. Regardless of the Shield's size, its weight always remains the same.

The expanded Shield is a nonmagical object that has AC 14 and HP 40. The Shield can remain expanded for up to 1 minute before it reverts back to its normal size. The expanded Shield reverts early (but is not destroyed) if it is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or moved. If you're holding the expanded Shield, you can choose to revert it early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Basco's Handy Bangle
entries: This shiny platinum bangle is inlaid with arcane runes that seem to be written by different hands. Labels can be read behind each of the adorning runes on the inside of the bangle. There are ten runes on the bangle, each corresponding to one of the following tools: {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}, {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB}, {@item Carpenter's Tools|XPHB}, {@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB}, {@item Leatherworker's Tools|XPHB}, {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}, {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB}, Musical Instruments ({@item drum|XPHB}, {@item flute|XPHB}, {@item lute|XPHB}, and {@item lyre|XPHB}), {@item Navigator's Tools|XPHB}, and {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch one of the runes and use the command words, "I can do this," to gain proficiency with the rune's associated tool for the next 24 hours; if you're already proficient with the associated tool, you instead gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} with it for the duration. The rune glows with arcane energy while in effect. Once a rune is activated in this way, it can't be activated again until {@dice 1d4} days have passed.

item.name: Basilisk Dagger
entries: This magic {@item Dagger|XPHB} was made from pieces of a slain basilisk and still retains some of its properties. The {@item Dagger|XPHB} deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Poison damage on a hit.

In addition, when you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll with this weapon against a Large or smaller creature, the target must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.

item.name: Bath Potion
entries: This bubbly potion is frothy and smells like lavender. When you drink this potion, any dirt and grime on your body is magically rinsed away. Further, your breath, hair, and nails are suddenly cleaned, either looking polished and pristine or smelling of daisies and honey for the next 24 hours. Bubbles occasionally escape from your mouth while the potion's effects are active.

item.name: Baton of Many Sizes
entries: While holding this segmented, 1-foot-long rod, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and use a command word to cause it to extend in either direction. The rod can extend up to be up to 50 feet long or as far as the surrounding space allows. If the rod touches another creature or surface before reaching its full length, the rod stops moving in that direction. If it stops moving against a solid, unmoving surface, the rod fills the gaps to hold firm against it.

The rod is unwieldy to hold once it's longer than 10 feet, forcing you to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check at the start of each of your turns to maintain your hold of it while it's extended beyond that range. The DC for this check is equal to 8 plus 1 for every 5 feet beyond 10 that the rod is extended. For example, the DC is 9 while the rod is 15 feet long, but 16 while it's a full 50 feet. If you drop the rod or use it to make an attack while it's extended more than 10 feet, it immediately retracts back to its shortened length. You can also cause it to retract by taking a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and repeating the command word.

item.name: Battle Tax
entries: This golden {@item Battleaxe|XPHB} is the chosen weapon of many merchants and followers of mercantile deities. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

When you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll with this weapon against a Small or larger enemy, you magically earn a number of Gold Pieces equal to the attack's damage. When you do, the axe glimmers like a sparkling coin, and you can sense the weight and clink of coins magically falling into your purse, pocket, or bag (your choice). If you have no room for the coins or don't want them to be immediately added to your storage, the coins erupt from where you hit the target instead. Once the axe generates coins in this way, it can't create any more for 12 hours.

item.name: Battlebrew Maul
entries: The head of this {@item Maul|XPHB} is an alchemical keg that generates battlebrew liquor, a favorite among Dwarves and Orcs. The keg can hold up to 4 gallons of the liquor. It creates 1 gallon each day at dawn and another gallon each time you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} while holding it. A creature can take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to remove or reattach the handle of the {@item Maul|XPHB} to the keg and another {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to pour out or stopper it.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While held in combat, the hammer heats the liquor inside it. When you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll with this weapon, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} Acid damage as the hammer buckles from the impact and releases a spray of the volatile liquor. The next time the target takes Fire damage in the next minute after being sprayed with liquor in this way, it takes an extra {@damage 2d6} Fire damage as the liquor ignites and burns away.

item.name: Battlechef Buckler
entries: This small Shield is the repurposed pot lid of a famous adventuring chef. While wielding the Shield, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against any effect that deals Fire damage. In addition, if you place the Shield face down over a pot or similar vessel that's holding no more than 2 gallons of a water-based liquid inside, the liquid comes to a rolling boil after 10 minutes.

item.name: Battlechef's Splendid Saucepan
entries: This magic saucepan can be used to turn nearly anything into a delicious and filling meal. You can boil up to 1 pound of any organic, nonmagical materials in the saucepan for 10 minutes or longer to magically transform them into a stew that provides enough nourishment to sustain four creatures for one day.

The saucepan also functions as a {@item Mace|XPHB}, granting you a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with it. The saucepan deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Thunder damage to any target it hits that's either wearing metal armor or is made of metal or stone.

If you're not wearing Heavy armor, you can take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to either wear the saucepan as a helmet or remove it. While wearing the saucepan in this way, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}.

item.name: Belt of the Raid Leader
entries: This thick leather belt is reinforced with large rivets and secured with a heavy iron skull buckle. While wearing the belt, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to let loose a powerful war cry that inspires you and up to five allies of your choice within 60 feet of you that can hear you. An inspired creature has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls until the start of your next turn. When you use this property of the belt, you can make one attack using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Blizzard Sphere
entries: This glass sphere contains a raging blizzard inside. It's cold to the touch and vibrates with the gusts of wind trapped within. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can throw the orb at a creature or object you can see within 30 feet of you, shattering it on impact. When the orb shatters, the blizzard erupts from the glass in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} of swirling ice and snow originating from the target. When a creature enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 4d8} Cold damage, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.

The blizzard remains for {@dice 1d4} rounds or until targeted by {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} (as if the effect were a level 5 spell). Any surface within the area is covered in slick ice and sleet, turning the ground within the sphere into {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} that remains for the effect's duration. While the blizzard persists, the area is {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB}.

item.name: Blood Pact Pendants
entries: These iron pendants come in pairs and are connected by a powerful bond of self-sacrifice. In order to create this bond, you and an ally with whom you are close perform a brief, 1-minute ritual to connect your destinies and forge a blood pact. When you create this blood pact, you must each agree on a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to permanently sacrifice, reducing your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum by that amount (minimum 1). Once a pact has been forged, trickles of the bound creatures' blood are absorbed into the normally clear gems adorning the faces of these pendants. While the gem is filled in this way, it seems to gently swirl and pulse in time with your heartbeat.

While the bound creatures are wearing their respective pendants and are on the same plane of existence, they're protected by this pact as long as one of the creatures is still conscious. When a bound creature dies, it's immediately revived with {@dice 2d10} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} plus twice the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} it had sacrificed. When a creature is revived in this way, the other bound creature immediately drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition, ending the blood pact and restoring both creatures' {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximums to normal; this property ignores any effect that would normally prevent a creature from being reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Once the pact is ended in this way, the gems and pendants crack and are no longer magical.

item.name: Bloodmage Dagger
entries: This roughly hewn obsidian {@item Dagger|XPHB} is razor sharp and can be used as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB}. Its handle is covered in small holes that drink in whatever blood runs down the blade. There's a small reservoir for blood at the {@item Dagger|XPHB}'s pommel that seems to never completely fill. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

The {@item Dagger|XPHB} has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d3 + 2} expended charges daily at dusk. While holding the {@item Dagger|XPHB}, you can expend 1 charge as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to make a melee spell attack, using your spell attack bonus, against a living creature you can see within 5 feet of you. When you do, an illusory crimson dagger sweeps out toward the creature. On a hit, the target takes Necrotic damage equal to {@dice 3d6} plus your spellcasting ability modifier, and you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Necrotic damage dealt. If the target is a Construct, Plant, or Undead creature, you don't regain any {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} in this way.

Alternatively, while holding the {@item Dagger|XPHB}, you can expend 1 or more charges when you cast a spell using a level 1, 2, or 3 spell slot that targets either yourself or a single creature. The spell slot you use to cast this spell counts as one level higher for each charge you expend in this way. You can't increase a spell slot level beyond level 5 in this way. When you use this property of the {@item Dagger|XPHB}, you also expend 1 {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} for each charge you expend in this way. Roll a number of {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} equal to the number of expended charges and take Necrotic damage equal to the total. This damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}. If expending charges in this way causes you to drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the spell fails and you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition.

item.name: Bloodscryer Oculus
entries: These red goggles have a second set of lenses that magically adjust their distance from your eyes to magnify and focus on targets. While you're attuned to the goggles, you are unaffected by visual obscurity, such as fog or smoke, and can see normally in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} and {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}—both magical and nonmagical—within 60 feet of yourself. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, wearing the goggles increases its range by 60 feet. Additionally, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks that rely on sight.

These lenses are also imbued with several innate spells. While wearing the goggles, you can cast one of the following spells from them, using your spell save DC, but can only target creatures whose blood you can see: {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} (level 3 version), {@spell Locate Creature|XPHB}, or {@spell Scrying|XPHB}. Casting a spell in this way doesn't require your {@status Concentration|XPHB}. Once the goggles are used to cast a spell in this way, they can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn.

item.name: Bolt
entries: This {@item Heavy Crossbow|XPHB} is made of metal and fires small bolts of electricity instead of normal ammunition. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you pull the string back to load the crossbow, the string crackles with energy and loads a small bolt of lightning ammunition instead. Attacks made with this crossbow deal {@damage 1d12} Lightning damage instead of {@dice 1d10} Piercing. In addition, whenever you make a ranged attack with this weapon against a metal target or a creature wearing metal armor, you make the attack roll with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

This crossbow acts as a small lightning rod. If another creature within 10 feet of you takes Lightning damage while you're holding the crossbow, you take {@damage 1d6} Lightning damage for every 10 Lightning damage the other creature took.

item.name: Boots of Dendallen
entries: These dark, fleshy boots grant marvelous powers and enhance your normal movement. While wearing the boots, you gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can hover. You can hover up to 20 feet off the ground in this way, leaving behind a thin trail of shadow when you move. In addition, when you take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action while wearing the boots, you can move an extra 10 feet.

The boots have 4 charges and regain {@dice 1d3 + 1} expended charges daily at dusk. While wearing the boots, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to cast either the Expeditious Retreat or {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} spell from them. When you cast {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} in this way, the silvery mist that surrounds you is instead dark and ominous.

entries.name: Curse
entries: When you pull these cursed boots over your feet for the first time, make a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw. On a successful save, you are aware of the curse's effects and can immediately remove the boots before they extend the curse to you. On a failed save, you become cursed, and the boots' muscly sinews dig into your legs and feet, interlocking with your own flesh; you take {@damage 4d8} Necrotic damage, and you can't remove the boots unless you are targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic.

While wearing the boots, your feet sometimes move in unexpected ways. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) checks and Dexterity saving throws, and when you have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, it costs you an extra 10 feet of movement to right yourself. When you remove the boots after being cursed by them, you take another {@damage 4d8} Necrotic damage as they painfully tear themselves from your body.

item.name: Boots of the Cloud Jumper
entries: While wearing these boots, you can jump while in the air as if you'd leapt from solid ground. You can't jump farther than your remaining movement would allow, and you treat all jumps made in this way as if they were standing jumps. When you make a midair jump, faint clouds fly out from under your feet. The boots allow you to jump in this way once before they need to make contact with a solid surface again. {@variantrule Jumping|XPHB} in this way while falling before landing on solid ground allows you to subtract up to 20 feet when calculating damage from the fall.

item.name: Borboss' Joyous Wand of Color
entries: This crystalline, prismatic wand can channel the illustrative intent of its wielder. While holding the wand, you can magically create a stroke of color in the air, allowing you to draw in midair or on any surface the wand touches. The effect is illusory, visibly magical, radiates {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} in a 5-foot radius, and dissipates after 1 hour.
If you attempt to draw on an object that is being worn or carried by an unwilling creature, that creature must succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity saving throw in order to avoid it.

item.name: Boreal Pendant
entries: This enlarged, floating snowflake is magically held in place and changes its shape as temperatures fluctuate. While attuned to the pendant you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage and give off an aura of cheeriness. The snowflake has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing it, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 15) from it: {@spell Charm Person|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Color Spray|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Hypnotic Pattern|XPHB} (3 charges), or {@spell Ice Storm|XPHB} (4 charges).
If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the snowflake melts into a puddle of water. After 1 year, the pendant reforms around the magical golden brackets that hold it in place.

item.name: Bottled Abyss
entries: This small vial has a single dose of a fuming, pitch-black poison inside it. When a creature consumes the poison, it takes {@damage 4d6} Psychic damage and must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition and becomes unable to benefit from a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. While the affected creature sleeps, its dreams are plagued with terrible nightmares. The creature repeats the save every 24 hours. The poison lasts until the creature succeeds on a save against it or is cured by the {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB}, Heal, or {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell.

item.name: Bounty Hunter Enforcer
entries: This {@item Spear|XPHB} has a hollow metal head filled with a spring-loaded {@item Net|PHB}. Attacks made with this weapon deal Bludgeoning damage instead of Piercing. When you hit a target with a melee attack using the weapon, you can press a button on the handle as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to deploy the spring-loaded {@item Net|PHB}. When you do, the target of the attack has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition if it's Large or smaller. Once the {@item Net|PHB} is deployed, you can retract and reset it as a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action. When you do, anything held by the {@item Net|PHB} is released. Attacks with the {@item Spear|XPHB} are made with {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} while the {@item Net|PHB} is deployed. If something is {@condition Restrained|XPHB} by the {@item Net|PHB}, you can't use the {@item Spear|XPHB} to make weapon attacks.

You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to prime the {@item Net|PHB} to trigger upon impact, allowing it to deploy the next time the {@item Spear|XPHB} hits a target when it's thrown or swung as part of an attack.

This {@item Net|PHB} follows the {@item Net|PHB}'s normal rules but is destroyed if it's detached from the {@item Spear|XPHB}. You can replace the {@item Net|PHB} with a new one by spending 1 minute attaching it to the {@item Spear|XPHB}.

item.name: Bow of Mind Thievery
entries: Arrows fired from this magic bow deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Psychic damage to any creature that they hit.

When you hit a Humanoid with an Arrow fired from the bow, you can force it to make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target is subjected to the effects of a {@spell Dominate Person|XPHB} spell for up to 1 minute, as if it were cast by you. This effect doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}. In addition, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically see through the target's eyes and hear what it hears until the start of your next turn, gaining the benefits of any special senses the target has. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Braided Quarterstaff
entries: This magic {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} is made of a tightly knit length of braided rope. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can use a command word to magically release its knotted form and loosen it into a 50-foot length of Rope. Repeating the command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} causes the Rope to tighten back up into a {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}. If a creature has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition from the Rope when you use the command word, the Rope tightens around the creature but doesn't crush it. To escape once tightened in this way, the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature or another creature within 5 feet of it must take an action to make a DC 15 Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check, freeing the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature on a success.

Tying an object no larger than 6 inches in any dimension to the end of the Rope doesn't prevent the Rope from reforming into the {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}. The Rope can only be broken by magical means. If broken, it becomes nonmagical and can't be transformed back into a {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}.

item.name: Brambleheart Quiver
entries: This wooden Quiver can carry your Arrows as well as provide some of its own. The Quiver has 4 magic Arrows grown on it and regrows {@dice 1d3 + 1} picked Arrows daily at dawn. These magic Arrows are thorned and remain attached to the Quiver until picked for use (no action required). While you're attuned to the Quiver, you gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with these magic Arrows. An Arrow grown in this way is no longer magical after 1 minute has passed since it was first picked from the Quiver.

When you hit a target with one of these magic Arrows, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to use a command word. When you do, the target takes {@damage 2d4} Piercing damage and the Arrow transforms into a thick field of thorns, duplicating the effect of a {@spell Spike Growth|XPHB} spell centered on a point within 5 feet of the target. This effect doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB} and lasts until the start of your next turn.

item.name: Breastplate of the Morning Light
entries: While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}. You can also take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cause the armor to emit a flash of blinding, radiant light. When you do, all other creatures within 30 feet of you must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn. Undead creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this save and take {@damage 2d8} Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Brooch of Many Sizes
entries: This metal brooch features a rotatable dial and can be used to secure a cape or adorn a piece of clothing. While wearing the brooch, you can cast the {@spell Enlarge/Reduce|XPHB} spell on yourself by turning the dial toward either the larger or smaller figure adorning the sides of the brooch. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

While under the effects of this spell, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to overload the brooch by forcibly twisting the dial beyond its normal limits. When you do, your size magically changes by an additional category: you either grow a second size larger if you have the "enlarge" effect or shrink a second size smaller if you have the "reduce" effect. Your size can't be larger than Huge or smaller than Tiny by overloading the brooch, and your damage and weight are increased or decreased as if you were affected by a second instance of the {@spell Enlarge/Reduce|XPHB} spell.

When you return to your original size after overloading the brooch, you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition and a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0 until the end of your next turn. Once the brooch has been overloaded, its properties can't be used again until 7 days have passed.

item.name: Bug Smashers
entries: This grotesque pair of gloves are made using the barbed exoskeleton and harvested remains of a giant centipede. Immediately after you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll made using an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} with the gloves, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Poison damage and must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn. {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} using these gloves deal either Bludgeoning or Piercing damage (your choice).

item.name: Cactus Mace
entries: This magic weapon's head is an enchanted cactus that rapidly regrows its missing needles. The first target hit by the weapon on each of your turns takes an extra {@damage 1d4} Piercing damage from the attack.

item.name: Candle of Anti-Light
entries: While burning, this dark candle sheds magical {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius. It can be lit for up to 1 hour before it's consumed and can be snuffed out or relit like a normal candle as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Completely covering the candle with something opaque blocks the {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}. {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} can't see through it, and nonmagical light can't illuminate it.

item.name: Catnip Amulet
entries: This gold and jade amulet seems to draw domestic cats to it. While wearing the necklace, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks made to find or call Tiny domestic cats (if there are any) and on Wisdom ({@skill Animal Handling|XPHB}) checks you make to interact with them.

item.name: Celestial Circlet
item.reqAttune: by a Cleric of Good Alignment
entries: This golden circlet radiates holy energy. A thin halo hovers above your head while you wear it, emitting {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. Small motes of holy light swirl around the apex of the circlet.

While you wear the circlet, you are always under the effect of the {@spell Protection from Evil and Good|XPHB} spell. This effect only applies to one of the spell's creature types: you can choose a new creature type (other than Celestials) to be protected against daily at dawn.

The circlet has 7 charges. While wearing it, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast either Cure Wounds (1 charge per spell level, up to level 4) or Spirit Guardians (3 charges) from it, using your spell save DC and spellcasting ability modifier. Immediately before or after you cast a spell in this way, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically fly up to 10 feet without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. When you do, you are carried away on a pair of angelic wings.
The circlet regains {@dice 1d4 + 3} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the halo vanishes and the circlet becomes nonmagical.

item.name: Ceratopper
entries: This adamantine helm is fashioned in the likeness of a triceratops and decorated with painted designs. You gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} while you wear this helmet. If you move at least 20 feet in a straight line toward a target immediately before hitting it with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} using the helmet, the target takes Piercing damage equal to {@dice 3d4} plus your Strength modifier instead of the normal damage for your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. If you hit and the target is no more than one size larger than you, it must also succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

In addition, while wearing the helmet, you can cast the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell from it, transforming yourself into a Triceratops. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until 2 days have passed.

item.name: Charlatan's Wardrobe
entries: This set of plain clothes is exceptionally comfortable and easy to wear. They are always clean and smell freshly washed and lightly perfumed. You can change the appearance of the clothes as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action by twirling in place and using a command word. The clothes' changed appearance is illusory. Physical interaction with the clothes reveals their changed appearance to be an illusion, since things can pass through it. A creature that takes a {@action Study|XPHB} action to examine the clothes can determine that their changed appearance is an illusion with a successful DC 13 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check. Regardless of their appearance, the clothes offer no additional protection from the elements, and can only appear to be clothes.

item.name: Chillspike
entries: This cold steel weapon from the Everglacier is topped with a menacing ice spike that never melts. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra {@damage 1d10} Cold damage on a hit. In addition, this weapon's properties and attacks with it ignore {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage.

When you reduce a Large or smaller creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} using this weapon, you can choose to encase that creature in ice. An encased creature can't gain the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and is held in place by a shell of ice for 1 minute. The creature doesn't make {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} while it's encased in the ice. The ice has AC 12, HP 20, {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison and Psychic damage, and {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Fire damage. If a spell or other magical effect restores {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to the encased creature or the ice is destroyed, the ice crumbles and releases the creature.

entries.name: Everglacier's Fury
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can plunge the {@item Pike|XPHB} into the ground at your feet. When you do, the blade of the {@item Pike|XPHB} magically vanishes into the earth and summons up to three icy spikes from the ground nearby. Choose up to three different targets on the ground you can see within 20 feet of you. An affected target must succeed on a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d8} Cold damage as a frozen spike erupts from the ground beneath it. The spikes immediately recede into the ground after the effect, and the icy blade of the {@item Pike|XPHB} reappears.

item.name: Cinnabar Rapier
entries: This magic {@item Rapier|XPHB}'s blade is alchemically coated in a thin but toxic layer of cinnabar. The weapon has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d8 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend 1 or more charges to cause its poison to leech into the target, dealing an extra {@damage 1d4} Poison damage for each charge expended in this way.

item.name: Circlet of the Huntsman's Third Eye
entries: This barbed metal circlet features a grotesque yet ornately preserved eye hanging from its center. The eye rests on the forehead of the creature wearing it. While wearing the circlet, its latent cunning guides your hand, granting you {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) checks.

The circlet has 5 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dusk. While wearing the circlet, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} spell from it. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws made to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} on this spell. In addition, you can expend 1 charge as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, or 1 additional charge when casting Hunter's Mark in this way, to magically gain {@sense Truesight|XPHB} with a range of 120 feet for 1 minute.

entries.name: Curse
entries: Attuning to the circlet curses you until you are targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic; removing the circlet fails to end the curse. While cursed, whenever you roll a 1 on an attack roll or ability check, you take {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage as the eye's intense frustration and scorn pierce your psyche.

item.name: Cloak of the Boundless Spirit
entries: This swirling, translucent cloak bolsters the fighting spirits of nearby allies. The cloak has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. When an ally you can see within 15 feet of you takes damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to reduce that damage by {@dice 2d6}.

In addition, when an ally within 15 feet of you rolls a 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20} for a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB}, it regains 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}, and you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to grant them {@dice 2d6} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. Once an ally regains 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} by rolling a 19 on a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB} in this way, they can't do so again until they finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Clockwork Faefly
entries: This brass clockwork mechanism is 2 inches long and whirs with the hum of tiny gears inside. You can tap the head of the faefly twice as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. When you do, it magically begins to flutter 5 feet in the air until a creature grasps it or it is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The faefly has AC 10, HP 10, {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison and Psychic damage, and {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all other damage. If you move more than 60 away feet from the hovering faefly, it follows you until it is within 60 feet of you. It takes the shortest route to do so. If prevented from moving, the faefly sinks gently to the ground and becomes inactive, and its light fades out. While it's flying, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and use a command word to cause it to move up to 20 feet.

If you are within 60 feet of the faefly, you can use another command word as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cause it to emit light as if targeted by the {@spell Light|XPHB} spell or cast {@spell Faerie Fire|XPHB} (save DC 13), affecting a 20-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} originating from the faefly. Faerie Fire doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB} when cast in this way, but it ends early if the faefly drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Once the {@spell Faerie Fire|XPHB} spell is cast from the faefly, it can't be cast in this way again until the next dawn.

The faefly is a magical object, not a creature. If the {@spell Mending|XPHB} spell is cast on it while it has at least 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}, it regains {@dice 2d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. If the faefly drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it can't be used again until 2 days have passed, at which point it regains all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

item.name: Clockwork Healerbee
entries: This pill-shaped magical mechanism is 2 inches long and made of brass and glass. It has a pair of tiny wings that silently keep it aloft. It hovers up to 5 feet in the air and does its best to stay at your side within your space. The bee can pass through another creature's space and doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

The bee has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. While the bee is within your space, you can use a command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend up to 3 charges to send the bee to magically heal a creature you can see within 20 feet of you. The bee flies out and touches its stinger to the target, causing the target to regain {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} for each expended charge and emitting an instantaneous burst of {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} that fills the target's space. The bee then returns to your space.

The bee is a magical object, not a creature. It has AC 15, HP 10, {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison and Psychic damage, and {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all other damage. If the {@spell Mending|XPHB} spell is cast on the bee while it has at least 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}, it regains {@dice 2d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. If the bee drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it can't be used again for 24 hours, at which point it regains all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

item.name: Clockwork Inkbeetle
entries: This minuscule, mechanical beetle is 1 inch long and quietly whirs with a mix of magical and mechanical forces. Placing the beetle on a piece of parchment you're holding or carrying causes it to spring to life and begin tracking your travels. As you move, so does the beetle, leaving behind a small line of ink. The ink immediately dries and doesn't dissolve in water. In addition, the beetle's small frame allows it to move even while enclosed in a book or similar enclosure without losing its sense of movement or direction.

The beetle mirrors your movement to create a to-scale map of your journey, drawing 1 inch of ink in your choice of the following scales: 20 feet per inch, 200 feet per inch, 2 miles per inch, or 20 miles per inch. You can rotate the dial on the beetle's underside to change the scale as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. The beetle is immune to magical effects that would otherwise alter its sense of direction.

The beetle always knows which way is magnetic north. When placed on a piece of parchment for the first time, it crouches down to stamp a small compass rose on the sheet before it begins to move. If the beetle runs out of space on the parchment to travel, it stops moving in that direction and emits a clicking sound that can be heard by creatures within 5 feet of it.

Attaching one Band of the Found and Lost to the beetle while you wear the other allows the beetle to move and trace your movements in this way from afar as long as you are both on the same plane of existence.

item.name: Coil Crook
entries: This smooth, red staff is actually made from a rigid length of decorative silken rope. You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and use a command word to cause it to magically loosen and turn into a 10-foot length of Rope. You can repeat the command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to cause the Rope to untangle itself and become the solid staff once more. It can be used as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} in either of these forms.
While holding the staff, you can cast the {@spell Rope Trick|XPHB} spell from it, using it as the rope required for the spell. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Coldsnap
entries: You have a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Cold damage on a hit.

The {@item Whip|XPHB} has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a creature that doesn't have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Cold damage with this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to force the target to make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} becomes 0 until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is instead halved for the same duration.
Once you attune to this weapon, its normally frigid handle feels warm to the touch.

item.name: Companion's Band
entries: This woven band can be worn as a necklace or bracelet by resizing its strap. While wearing the band, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to summon a spectral companion that appears in a space you can see within 5 feet of you. The companion has the appearance of a dog, cat, bird, or similar Small or smaller Beast of your choice. Its form is translucent and immaterial but feels warm to the touch. It emits {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} in a 5-foot radius. The companion remains until dismissed (no action required) or until you remove the band. It is a creation of magical force, not a creature; it doesn't occupy space and doesn't require air, food, or drink. It is {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} to you and other creatures you choose. It stays within 30 feet of you at all times and moves through objects as necessary in order to do so. If it's unable to reach you, it disappears until summoned again. The companion wears a spectral copy of the band on its neck or limb (your choice).

The companion understands the emotion and intent of your commands but can't speak or understand language. Attempting to read the companion's thoughts only reveals an overwhelming sense of love and affection for you.

Once the same spectral companion has remained or has been summoned at least once a day for 7 consecutive days, the band and companion become permanently linked to you. When this happens, the band no longer requires you to be attuned to it in order to use it, but no other creature can attune to it. Once the band is linked to you in this way, you can summon your linked companion as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} instead of a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. In addition, the companion becomes partially physical, allowing you and creatures you choose to be able to gently pet and hold its otherwise spectral form.
If you die, the band can be attuned to by a different creature. The linked companion follows you to whatever afterlife awaits you or reappears if you're resurrected and summon it once again.

item.name: Container of Heat and Frost
entries: This heavy iron container is adorned with three arcane symbols along the edge of its removable lid. The container can hold up to 1 gallon of liquid. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can press one of the container's arcane glyphs to heat the liquid inside to a boil (212 degrees Fahrenheit), chill it (33 degrees Fahrenheit), or maintain its current temperature (keeping it between 33 and 212 degrees Fahrenheit). Once pressed, the selected glyph glows either red, blue, or yellow, respectively.

It takes 1 minute to boil or chill a liquid. Pressing the glyph again as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action ends the heating or cooling process early. After boiling or chilling the liquid, the container maintains its hot or cool temperature, respectively.

item.name: Coralshield Golem
entries: This crablike golem is made of a light but resilient magical coral. While wearing it on your back, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to command it to magically move along your arm and don itself upon you. You can command the golem to return to your back as another {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. If you don't have a free hand or refuse to drop what you're holding when the golem attempts to don itself, it returns and reattaches to your back.

item.name: Couatl Herald's Guard
item.reqAttune: by a Creature of Good Alignment
entries: This magic Shield is made from yew and covered in durable couatl feathers. When a creature you can see within 10 feet of you takes damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to magically take that damage instead of the target. When you do so, the damage type changes to Force. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Flight of the Couatls
entries: While you're attuned to three items with this property, you gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet. If you already have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}, it increases by 30 feet instead.

item.name: Couatl Herald's Lash
item.reqAttune: by a Creature of Good Alignment
entries: This slender {@item Whip|XPHB}'s grip is feathered and comfortable to wield. Its weight has been magically reduced and it gently sways on its own. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and it has the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property.

Once on each of your turns when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can forgo one of your attacks, instead using the {@item Whip|XPHB} to attempt to bind a Medium or smaller creature within the {@item Whip|XPHB}'s reach. The target must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition and takes {@damage 1d4} Bludgeoning damage as it's constricted. If a target that's already {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the {@item Whip|XPHB} fails another save against you using the {@item Whip|XPHB} in this way, it has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition instead.

A target that is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} or {@condition Restrained|XPHB} by the {@item Whip|XPHB} can use its action to make a DC 15 Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) check, ending the condition on itself on a success. Moving more than 10 feet away from the target or dropping the {@item Whip|XPHB} also ends the condition on the target. Until the condition ends, you can't use the {@item Whip|XPHB} against a different target.

entries.name: Flight of the Couatls
entries: While you're attuned to three items with this property, you gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet. If you already have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}, it increases by 30 feet instead.

item.name: Couatl Herald's Mantle
item.reqAttune: by a Creature of Good Alignment
entries: This magic cloak is given to those deemed kindhearted and responsible by a Celestial. While wearing the mantle, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition and the effects of magical contagions. In addition, you are immune to any effect that would sense your emotions.

entries.name: Flight of the Couatls
entries: While you're attuned to 3 items with this property, you gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet. If you already have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}, it increases by 30 feet instead.

item.name: Couatl Herald's Radiance
item.reqAttune: by a Creature of Good Alignment
entries: This magic {@item wooden staff|XPHB} was enchanted by a powerful couatl. The staff has 5 charges. While holding the staff, you can expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells, using your spellcasting ability modifier and spell save DC: {@spell Create Food and Water|XPHB}, {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB} (level 2 version), {@spell Lesser Restoration|XPHB}, {@spell Protection from Poison|XPHB}, {@spell Sanctuary|XPHB}, or {@spell Shield|XPHB}.
The staff regains {@dice 1d3 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the staff vanishes in a flash of light and is lost forever.

entries.name: Flight of the Couatls
entries: While you're attuned to three items with this property, you gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet. If you already have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}, it increases by 30 feet instead.

item.name: Couatl Herald's Scales
item.reqAttune: by a Creature of Good Alignment
entries: This enchanted armor is made from couatl scales that were collected by a generous Celestial. While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} and it doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks.

entries.name: Tooth and Scale
entries: When you take damage from a creature within 10 feet of you, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to channel the good spirit within the armor and cause a bright, spectral couatl to appear above you. The spectral form strikes at the creature who damaged you, forcing it to make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. The creature takes {@damage 4d10} Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Flight of the Couatls
entries: While you're attuned to three items with this property, you gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet. If you already have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}, it increases by 30 feet instead.

item.name: Cratering Quarterstaff
entries: This thick, stony {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} requires a Strength score of 17 or higher in order to adequately wield it. Despite glowing with embers, it feels comfortably warm to the touch. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

This {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. Once on each of your turns immediately after you make an attack roll with the {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}, you can expend 1 charge (no action required) to slam the weapon into the ground and create a 10-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} of intense tremors and flame in the direction of the attack's target. Each creature in the {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw; the attack's target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this save. A creature takes {@damage 1d8} Bludgeoning damage and {@damage 1d8} Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Crosier of Divine Power
entries: This staff can be wielded as a magic {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} that grants a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with it.

The staff is enchanted with holy magic that manifests as radiant motes of power during times of need. When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} against an enemy, the staff creates 2 motes. At the start of each of your turns in combat, roll {@dice 1d6}. On a 6, a new mote appears above the head of the staff. When you hit a target with the staff or heal a creature with a spell or magical effect while holding it, you can expend 1 of the motes to add {@dice 2d6} to the amount of damage dealt or {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} healed. The extra damage is Radiant.
Each mote lasts for 1 minute or until it's expended.

item.name: Crown of Deep Winter
entries: This icy crown is made using ice from the Everglacier. While wearing the crown, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage. In addition, you can cast the {@spell Fire Shield|XPHB} spell from it, using the chill shield option. Once the crown has been used to cast this spell, it can't cast it again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Frozen Burst
entries: While wearing this crown, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when a creature within 5 feet of you that you can see hits you with an attack roll, causing the area around you to explode in a burst of razor-sharp icicles. When you do, all other creatures within 5 feet of you must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes {@damage 2d8} Cold damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. You can't use this property again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Crypt Keeper Glaive
entries: You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Whenever you reduce a Small or larger creature that is {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} toward you to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} with the {@item Glaive|XPHB}, you gain {@dice 1d10} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

In addition, the {@item Glaive|XPHB}'s blade begins to glow with a sickly green haze whenever you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} in this way. While glowing, the next time you hit a target with a melee attack using the {@item Glaive|XPHB}, the target takes extra Necrotic damage equal to the number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} you gained. Once you deal this extra damage, the haze fades. The haze and any remaining {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} are lost after 1 minute.
Undead creatures killed by this weapon can't be raised from the dead again.

entries.name: Six Feet Under
entries: The first time on each of your turns that you hit a target with the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition using this weapon, the weapon deals maximum damage.

item.name: Dagger of the Ogre Mage
entries: This blade was once used as a {@item dagger|XPHB} by an ogre, but is nevertheless large enough to be a {@item Shortsword|XPHB}. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

The sword's hilt is hollow and can hold up to three Spell Scrolls. If the {@item Spell Scroll|XDMG} is a cantrip and is left within the compartment for 1 minute, one of the three clear gems adorning the sword's grip is activated. Once activated, you can cast the cantrip at will using your spell save DC and spell attack modifier.

Cantrips cast in this way use the weapon as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} and allow you to perform any Somatic components with the weapon instead of requiring a free hand. {@item Spell Scroll|XDMG} contained within the hilt don't crumble to dust after using them in this way.
When you cast a cantrip in this way that deals damage, the sword's damage type changes to match the cantrip's for 1 minute or until you end it early (no action required).

item.name: Dark Fathom Armor
entries: This dark, sea-soaked suit of armor smells of brine and is covered in eldritch runes. While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} and can breathe underwater.

In addition, while wearing the armor, you can cast the {@spell Evard's Black Tentacles|XPHB} spell (save DC 15) from it. When cast in this way, the spell targets a 15-foot square centered on you; it doesn't follow you when you move. This version of the spell doesn't require your {@status Concentration|XPHB}, and you are immune to its effects. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dusk.

item.name: Dead Ringer
entries: This bronze mask lets out a deep, sonorous ring when struck. When you're reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or fail a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB} while wearing the mask, the three enemies nearest to you within 30 feet of you must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take {@damage 2d12} Necrotic damage. Targets automatically succeed on this save if they aren't missing any {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or have the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition. If you're healed or stabilized after dropping to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, this property of the mask can't be used again until the next dusk.

item.name: Death Knell
entries: This dark metal weapon is grimly ornate. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Once per turn when you reduce a Small or larger creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} with this weapon, a foreboding, gonglike sound emanates from the blade. Each enemy within 15 feet of the target that's missing any of its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw, taking {@damage 1d8} Psychic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Devil's Detail Eyepatch
entries: This well-worn leather eyepatch is enchanted with the illusion of an embroidered eye on the front. While wearing the eyepatch over an eye, whether you're missing one or not, you can magically see from the patch as if it were a working eye.
