The embroidered eye adorning the front of the patch swivels and moves as you look around while wearing it.

In addition, while wearing the eyepatch over an eye, you can use a command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet for 1 hour. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, this effect increases its range by 60 feet. For the duration, you can see through magical {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} as if it were nonmagical {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Direstone Dwarven Pick
entries: This old, weathered {@item War Pick|XPHB} is able to effortlessly break and mold stone. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Creatures and objects made of stone that are hit by the {@item War Pick|XPHB} take an extra {@damage 1d8} Piercing damage from the attack. In addition, the weapon has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. You can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Meld into Stone|XPHB} or {@spell Stone Shape|XPHB} spell from the weapon.

item.name: Discordant Thunderstave
entries: This thin, hollow {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} reverberates when it strikes a target. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which has the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} property. When you hit a target with the weapon and roll the maximum number on its damage die, the hollow staff shudders and emits a deep, thunderous sound. Roll the weapon's damage die again and add it to the total damage of the attack. This extra damage is Thunder damage.

item.name: Doodle Ring
entries: This magic wooden ring is always covered in splotches of charcoal, ink, or paint, regardless of how well or often it's cleaned. While wearing the ring on a finger, you can touch that finger to a solid surface and begin to draw. The drawing uses your finger to make marks as if it were a piece of charcoal, an ink quill, or a paintbrush (your choice). The marks you make can be in any color. Any drawing made by the ring can be easily smudged or washed away without leaving any marks behind. The ring can have up to a total of 5 square feet covered with drawings in this way at any time. Drawing more than that begins to erase the previous marks, and any mark it makes is magically erased after 24 hours.

item.name: Dragon Tamer Lance
entries: This slender, iridescent {@item Lance|XPHB} is covered in an enchanted finish that resembles the hardened scales of many dragons. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While attuned to the weapon, you know {@language Draconic|XPHB}.

This {@item Lance|XPHB} has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a target with the {@item Lance|XPHB}, you can expend 1 charge to deal an extra {@damage 3d6} Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison damage (your choice) to the target. If you expend a charge in this way when you hit a Dragon, it must also succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.

Whenever you expend a charge in this way, you can expend a second charge to deal an additional {@damage 1d6} damage of the same type to the target, and the target can't take {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} until the start of its next turn.

entries.name: Wyrmling Steed
entries: In addition, while holding the {@item Lance|XPHB}, you can cast the {@spell Find Steed|XPHB} spell from it. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn. When you cast the spell in this way, you can choose to summon a dragon wyrmling instead of a normal mount by expending a number of charges equal to its {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} (maximum 2). You determine the kind of dragon summoned, but its alignment toward Good or Evil must be the same as your own; if your alignment is Neutral, you can summon a wyrmling of either alignment. The wyrmling counts as one size larger when used as a mount and can't use its breath weapons.

entries.name: Forceful Impact
entries: If you move at least 20 feet in a straight line toward a Medium or smaller target immediately before making a melee attack roll with the {@item Lance|XPHB} while within 5 feet of the target, you make the attack roll with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. On a hit, you can immediately make an additional melee attack roll with the {@item Lance|XPHB} as part of the initial attack. This additional attack roll must be made against a different target directly behind the first that is within 5 feet of it and isn't behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}.

item.name: Dragon Turtle Shield
entries: This Shield is made from a piece of reclaimed dragon turtle shell. The dragon turtle head sculpture adorning its front is made of brass, and it magically creates and heats a small reservoir of water that replenishes daily at dawn. When you take Fire damage while holding the Shield, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to use a command word. When you do, the water inside it is superheated, reducing the Fire damage you take by half. The superheated water is released as a dense cloud of steam, causing the area in a 45-foot-diameter {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you to be {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB}. The cloud remains stationary and lasts for 1 minute or until a strong wind (like that created by {@spell Gust of Wind|XPHB}) disperses it.
Once this property is used, the Shield's reservoir is empty and can't be used again in this way until the next dawn when it replenishes or until it's submerged in nonmagical salt water for 1 hour.

item.name: Dragon-Kindled Edge
entries: This magnificent blade is crafted from the remains of an ancient dragon. You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The weapon deals an extra {@damage 2d6} damage on a hit if the target is a Dragon. In addition, while you're attuned to this weapon, you know {@language Draconic|XPHB}.

entries.name: Kindled Cleave
entries: When you're affected by a {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} dragon's breath weapon, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to hold the blade in front of you. When you do, the sword splits the breath weapon's area of effect, leaving you and any creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} behind you unaffected by it. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

When you use this property to split a breath weapon that deals damage, the sword gives off an elemental haze corresponding to the dragon's breath weapon. The haze lasts for 1 minute, and it sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet. The first target you hit with an attack roll using the sword while it has this haze takes an extra {@damage 6d6} damage of the breath weapon's damage type. This extra damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}.

entries.name: Lesser Draconic Resistance
entries: If you fail a saving throw by 4 or less, you can choose to succeed instead. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Dragon's Call
entries: This ear cuff imparts a deeper understanding of draconic history and language upon its wearer. While wearing the ear cuff, you know Draconic. In addition, you gain a +1 bonus to any Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma check you make that involves dragon-related lore.

item.name: Dream Mantle
entries: This short purple cape is vaguely iridescent and is difficult to look at from afar. The cape creates illusions of faraway places that are visible only to creatures that are at least 30 feet away from it. While wearing the mantle, creatures more than 30 feet away from you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks that rely on sight to perceive you, and they make ranged attack rolls against you with {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}. This property is suppressed while you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} or {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition or are otherwise unable to move.

In addition, you can cast the {@spell Dream|XPHB} spell (save DC 16) from the mantle over the course of 1 minute, using yourself as the messenger within the dream. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until 7 days have passed.

item.name: Druidic Throwing Club
entries: This magic wooden {@item Club|XPHB} has the {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} property with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While attuned to the {@item Club|XPHB}, you know the {@spell Shillelagh|XPHB} cantrip. If the {@item Club|XPHB} is affected by {@spell Shillelagh|XPHB}, the spell doesn't end when the weapon is thrown. The {@item Club|XPHB} flies back to your hand immediately after making a ranged attack roll with it.

item.name: Dryadleaf
entries: This magic plant only grows at the base of trees that are often stepped through by dryads. Its leaves continuously shift as if by a breeze. Touching the plant, even gently, causes its leaves to stop their gentle swaying for 1 minute.

Dryadleaf plants only grow up to four leaves at a time and are found in the wild with {@dice 1d4} matured leaves. Leaves are fully matured once they have a thin yellow rim. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to eat a mature Dryadleaf. When you do, you can speak to Beasts, Plant creatures, and nonmagical plants for 1 minute as if you shared a language. Once it's been picked, a Dryadleaf retains this property for 7 days before it wilts.

You can move the plant to a pot over the course of 1 minute. When you do, make a DC 15 Intelligence ({@skill Nature|XPHB}) or Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) check. On a success, the delicate plant survives the transfer to a new pot. On a failure, the plant is irreversibly damaged and dies.

The plant can survive with surprisingly little air, allowing it to survive in an extradimensional space such as a {@item Bag of Holding|XDMG} or {@item Portable Hole|XDMG}. As long as it's watered lightly each day and given a moderate amount of sunlight (weather and environment permitting), the plant grows {@dice 1d4} missing mature leaves every 7 days.

item.name: Eaglereign
entries: This thick glove is ornamented with eagle feathers and fitted with an enchanted silk lining. You can use the glove to cast the {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB} spell as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action instead of its normal casting time. When you do, the familiar takes the form of an Eagle. The familiar disappears if you are no longer attuned to the glove. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Summon Giant Eagle
entries: You can focus on the glove for 1 hour to magically summon a Giant Eagle that appears in an unoccupied space that you can see within 30 feet of you. It disappears when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, after 12 hours, or if you dismiss it (no action required). Once the creature disappears, it can't be summoned again until 2 days have passed.

The creature is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the creature shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediaftely after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its movement to avoid danger.

The creature has a carrying capacity of 480 pounds and can travel at a rate of 8 miles per hour while flying. It must rest 1 hour for every 3 hours it flies, and it can't fly for more than 9 hours each time it's summoned.

item.name: Ear Cuff of the Vampire Bat
entries: While wearing this ceramic bat ear cuff, you can mentally command the cuff (no action required) to enhance your hearing when you fail a Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) check to that relies on hearing. When you do, the bat's eyes flash with a red glow as its ceramic fangs bite down on your ear, causing you to take {@damage 1d4 + 1} Necrotic damage and gain a bonus to the check equal to the amount of Necrotic damage you took, potentially turning the failure into a success. This damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}.

item.name: Edible Book of Recipes
entries: This tome of cooking recipes is enchanted to turn sheets of fine paper into delectable meals. You can write a simple recipe on one of the book's pages over the course of 1 minute. As you do, the paper becomes aromatic, smelling like the cuisine you're writing. The sheet of paper has the taste, texture, and smell of the food described on its page. Eating the sheet as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action nourishes you as if you ate a meal.

If you add a more complex recipe to the book that takes at least 10 minutes to write—potentially using sensory language, descriptions of fine ingredients, or illustrations of the dish—eating the delicious sheet of paper provides enough nourishment to sustain a creature for one day. When a creature eats a sheet detailing one of these complex recipes, its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by {@dice 1d10} + its {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and it gains the same number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, until it finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. In addition, if the creature has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition or is affected by a magical contagion, it can immediately repeat the save to end it. A creature can only gain the benefits of a complex recipe once every 24 hours.

In addition, you can use this book as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} when casting the {@spell Create Food and Water|XPHB} spell if you have it prepared or can otherwise cast it. When you do, the food and drink you create can be aromatic and flavorful, recreating recipes of your choice from the book.
The book can hold up to 50 pages. A book found as treasure usually has {@dice 3d10 + 10} blank pages as well as {@dice 1d10} filled pages containing complex recipes.

When you remove the last page from the book or attempt to rebind the book, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the tome bursts into flame, destroying the book and leaving the air in a 60-foot radius smelling like wine and fine cooking. The smell remains in the area for 30 days. After 1 minute, the book's tattered remains transform into a bountiful meal, duplicating the effect of the {@spell Heroes' Feast|XPHB} spell. On any roll other than a 1, the book can be rebound using fine paper that costs at least 5 GP per page.

item.name: Eldritch Scarf
entries: This dark scarf moves slowly on its own and is always wrinkled. The scarf hides an extradimensional space within its folds that can only be reached while its worn. The space can hold 60 pounds of material, not exceeding a volume of 6 cubic feet. The scarf always weighs 1 pound, regardless of its contents. When you place an object in this space (no action required), your hand vanishes into the scarf's loose folds.

You can't retrieve items placed within the scarf with your hands. Instead, retrieving an item requires you to take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to mentally control the scarf while wearing it, causing its frayed, handlike ends to reach into the space and retrieve a stored item of your choice. The hands can carry up to 10 pounds, and they drop any object weighing more than that on the ground at your feet.

If the scarf is ironed or otherwise flattened, the items stored inside are squeezed out, spilling forth unharmed from the ends of the scarf. If the scarf is destroyed, its contents are lost forever, although an Artifact always turns up again somewhere. A living creature can't be placed inside the scarf.

In addition, while wearing the scarf, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to mentally control it, using the scarf's frayed hands instead of your own to perform a simple task within 5 feet of you, such as to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial. The hands can't attack or activate magic items.

Placing the scarf inside an extradimensional space created by a {@item Bag of Holding|XDMG}, {@item Portable Hole|XDMG}, or similar item instantly destroys both items and opens a gate to the Astral Plane. The gate originates where the one item was placed inside the other. Any creature within 10 feet of the gate and not behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} is sucked through it and deposited in a random location on the Astral Plane. The gate then closes. The gate is one-way only and can't be reopened.

item.name: Enchantment Breaker
entries: This ring's tiny chains rotate slowly around your finger while worn. Whenever you are affected by a spell that restores {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, such as Cure Wounds or {@variantrule Healing|XPHB} Word, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to repeat the saving throw against an Enchantment spell that's currently affecting you.

entries.name: Shatter Enchantment
entries: When you fail a saving throw against an Enchantment spell, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to weaken the spell's grip. You and your allies within 10 feet of you that were targeted by the spell can reroll the save, potentially turning the failure into a success. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until {@dice 1d4} days have passed.

item.name: Essence of Rage
entries: This swirling potion tastes like iron and bitter raisins. When you drink this potion, you enter a rage that lasts for 1 minute or until you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. This rage grants you the same effects as the level 1 Barbarian class feature with the following changes:

items: You can enter and maintain this rage even if you're wearing Heavy armor.

If you end your turn and haven't made an attack roll against an enemy, forced an enemy to make a saving throw, or taken a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to extend your rage, you take {@damage 1d4} Psychic damage, and the rage extends for another round.

item.name: Ether Spear
entries: This {@item Spear|XPHB} can slip through the Ethereal Plane to reach its destination. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals Force damage instead of Piercing. When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action while holding the {@item Spear|XPHB}, you can choose a space you can see within 20 feet of you. The tip of the {@item Spear|XPHB} vanishes and reappears in that space, allowing you to make melee attack rolls with the {@item Spear|XPHB} against a target within 5 feet of that space until the end of the turn. These attacks ignore {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} and treat {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover} as if it were {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} instead.

entries.name: Ethereal Sight
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane while on the Material Plane and vice versa. This effect lasts for 1 minute. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Everice Box
entries: This large wooden box weighs 25 pounds and is 3 feet long, 2 feet deep, and 2 feet tall. It has rugged ropes on its left and right sides as well as on the top of its lid. The left and right sides of the box are covered by enchanted slabs of stone that magically keep the contents on the inside of the box at a consistent 40 degrees Fahrenheit. The box can hold up to 24 Rations (or an equivalent amount of food). For a creature stuck inside the box to escape, it or a creature within 5 feet of the box must make a DC 10 Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check, freeing the trapped creature on a success.

While the box's lid is closed and its latch is shut, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and use a command word to shrink it and its contents down to a more portable size. The weight of the box and anything inside it is halved while it's shrunk in this way, and the box measures a mere 1 foot long by 8 inches deep and tall. You can repeat the command word to return the box to its normal size. Opening the latch, whether by choice or by force, immediately returns the box and its contents to their full size and weight. A living creature can't be shrunk in this way, and the lid and latch are forced open if the box becomes too small for it.

item.name: Everlasting Sugarbomb
entries: This large sugarbomb is roughly two inches in diameter. Its flavors are a mix of bitter, salty, savory, spicy, sour, and sweet, but also refreshing. The sugarbomb is solid and slowly dissolves in your mouth, changing in flavor each time you reach a new layer. An Everlasting Sugarbomb provides enough nourishment to sustain a creature for one day after 1 minute of letting it dissolve in your mouth. The sugarbomb fully dissolves after 30 uses. You can bite through to the center of the sugarbomb with a successful DC 25 Strength or Constitution check (your choice). If you succeed, make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute and suffer from painful nausea.

item.name: Eye of Dendallen
entries: While you're attuned to this item, you can cast the {@spell See Invisibility|XPHB} spell from it at will.

entries.name: Curse
entries: The Eye of Dendallen is cursed. When you attune to it, you must make a DC 20 Wisdom saving throw as you're suddenly overcome with the urge to see what it sees. On a successful save, you are aware of the curse's effects and can immediately end your attunement to the eye before it extends the curse to you. On a failed save or if you choose not to end your attunement to it, you are cursed until you are targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic. When you become cursed, you forcefully tear out one of your own eyes, taking {@damage 4d10} Necrotic damage, and allow the Eye of Dendallen to crawl into the now vacant socket. If you're already missing an eye, you simply let the eye crawl into that socket without taking the Necrotic damage.

You can see normally through the Eye of Dendallen while it's in your socket. In addition, you have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already had {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, the eye increases its range by 30 feet. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can focus on looking through the eye to see through magical {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} for 1 minute as if it were nonmagical {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dusk.

If your attunement to the eye ends after being cursed by it, it pulls itself out of your socket and tries to escape. The eye uses the Spider stat block except its creature type is Undead; it has HP 5; and it doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep. Slaying the eye while it isn't attuned to a creature destroys the item.

entries.name: Dendallen Familiar
entries: The eye also doubles as a gruesome companion while you're attuned to it. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can command the eye to crawl out of your socket and move independently of you. You control the eye as if you summoned it with the {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB} spell, using the statistics above. If the eye drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or you dismiss it, it reappears inside your open socket with a sickly sound. If it does so as a result of dropping to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it turns pitch black, and none of its magical properties can be used until the next dusk.

item.name: Eye of the Bookwurm
entries: This small, glass sphere is enchanted with weak Divination magics to help you find specific words or phrases in writing. It has a stylized iris etched into the glass.

You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to use a command word and then speak the word or phrase you're searching for. The word or phrase magically appears and floats within the sphere. By holding the eye in a free hand, your vision becomes magically enhanced to subconsciously highlight the word or phrase you searched for in a green light wherever you see it. Once you've used the eye's ability for the first time, you can change the word or phrase as many times as you like for the next hour, after which the item can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Fable's End
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Whenever you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll made with Fable's End against a Large or smaller creature, the target must succeed on a DC 15 Charisma saving throw or be banished to a harmless demiplane. At the end of its next turn, the target reappears in the space it left or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied. If you make an attack roll against a Fey with this weapon, you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} and force the save against being banished on a roll of 19 or 20; Fey creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this save. When a creature is banished in this way, the weapon's center emits a brilliant magenta flash of light.

item.name: Ferryman's Take
entries: These tarnished copper coins are always found in pairs and bear the portrait of a horned man on one side (heads) and a rowboat on the other (tails).

You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to place the coins over the eyes of a Humanoid that is dead, willing, or has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition. When you do, the coins create an effect based on which side is facing up. You can remove one or both of them from the creature's eyes as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to end the effect early. If the coins aren't facing the same direction, they have no effect.

entries.name: Heads
entries: While the coins are heads up over the eyes of a creature that has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition, that creature is {@variantrule Stable|XPHB}. If placed over the eyes of a conscious creature, it enters a physical and mental state indistinguishable from death for 1 hour or until the coins are removed, and it has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Psychic damage for the duration. Once a living creature leaves this state, it can't enter it again for 24 hours.

entries.name: Tails
entries: While the coins are tails up over the eyes of a dead creature, that creature is under the effect of the {@spell Gentle Repose|XPHB} spell. Its body can't be detected through magical means, and the soul of the creature can't be contacted.

item.name: Festerwood Buckler
entries: This festerwood Shield is riddled with amber pustules that glow with a low, yellow light. While holding this magic Shield, the pustules emit {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 5-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 5 feet. In addition, while you're holding the Shield, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when a creature that you can see within 5 feet of you misses you with an attack roll to attempt to bash that creature with the Shield. When you do, the target must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d4 + 1} Acid damage.

item.name: Festerwood Claymore
entries: This heavy, dark wood is incredibly strong and forms part of the symbiotic relationship it shares with destructive beetles that live inside. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

When you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll made with this weapon, some of the sword's beetles leave the weapon and latch onto the target, biting and hindering it for its next {@dice 1d4} turns. While affected by the beetles in this way, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage at the start of each of its turns and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved. The target or a creature within 5 feet of it can take an action to swat away the beetles, removing them and ending the effect early. If the target is a plant, a Plant creature, or an object made of wood, this effect deals maximum damage to it at the start of each of its turns and lasts for 4 turns unless ended early by a creature.

item.name: Festerwood Fungal Stave
item.reqAttune: by a Cleric, Druid, or Sorcerer
entries: This fungal staff has 25 charges and regains {@dice 1d10 + 15} expended charges daily at dusk. While holding the staff, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and expend 1 or more charges to cause clusters of noxious mushrooms to magically appear in a number of unoccupied spaces equal to the number of charges you expend. The spaces must be on solid ground within 60 feet of you. Each space becomes {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} for the duration of the effect. When a creature moves into or within an area covered by these mushrooms, it takes {@damage 2d4} Poison damage for every 5 feet it travels. The mushrooms live for 1 minute or until you dismiss them as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

In addition, while holding the staff, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells from it (regardless of the spell's normal casting time), using your spell save DC: {@spell Cloudkill|XPHB} (5 charges), {@spell Fog Cloud|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Insect Plague|XPHB} (5 charges), or {@spell Stinking Cloud|XPHB} (3 charges). A spell cast in this way doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}, but it ends at the end of your next turn and must target a point adjacent to a space occupied by your mushrooms.

If you expend the last charge from the staff, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the fungi on the staff begin to overgrow, destroying the staff and forcing you to drop it in an unoccupied space adjacent to you. A {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} Violet Fungus grows in its place.

item.name: Festerwood Logger
entries: This magic axe is a favorite among lumberjacks. The axe is made entirely of the magically hardened festerwood and sharpened to a menacing edge. The weapon's glimmering yellow pustules leech acid with each attack, causing any target hit by it to take an extra {@damage 1d4} Acid damage. This Acid damage is maximized if the target is a plant, a Plant creature, or an object made of wood.

item.name: Festerwood Vizard
entries: This dark wooden mask is strangely insect-like and magically conforms to fit your face. While wearing the mask, you have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 10 feet.

In addition, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cause beetles to fly out of the mask's mouth, which use the Swarm of Insects ({@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}) stat block. The swarm doesn't take its own turns, but you can command it to move and make one attack as part of the action to summon it and then again on each of your subsequent turns as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, using its statistics. If you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, the swarm takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its movement to avoid danger. The swarm disappears when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. If it goes 10 minutes without attacking a creature, it disperses harmlessly to seek food elsewhere. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dusk.

item.name: Festerwood's Light
entries: This festerwood {@item Rapier|XPHB}'s blade is inlaid with scrapings from the Lightspore, the radiant mushroom growing at the center of the Festerwood. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, attuned to the {@item Rapier|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks made to find locations within the Festerwood, and you are immune to magical contagions. While holding the sword, it sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet.

You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to raise the blade overhead and use a command word. When you do, all other creatures within 30 feet of you are dusted with a sudden wave of magical spores. An ally affected by the spores is suddenly invigorated, allowing it to immediately take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to either make one attack roll using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} or move up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. An enemy affected by the spores must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dusk.
The {@item Rapier|XPHB} has 5 charges for the following properties. It regains {@dice 1d3 + 2} expended charges daily at dusk.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While holding the {@item Rapier|XPHB}, you can expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells (save DC 17, +9 attack bonus) from it: {@spell Charm Person|XPHB} (level 4 version), {@spell Compulsion|XPHB}, {@spell Confusion|XPHB}, {@spell Contagion|XPHB}, {@spell Faerie Fire|XPHB}, {@spell Locate Animals or Plants|XPHB}, or {@spell Moonbeam|XPHB} (level 4 version). In addition, you can cast the {@spell Dancing Lights|XPHB} cantrip while holding the {@item Rapier|XPHB}.

entries.name: Spore Cleansing
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch a willing creature with the {@item Rapier|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to remove 1 Spore Counter from that creature.

item.name: Fife of Dragonsong
entries: This magical fife can be played as an instrument or wielded as a deadly weapon. You are proficient with the Fife of Dragonsong as both an instrument and {@item Blowgun|XPHB} while you're attuned to it. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Thunder damage on a hit.

entries.name: Dragonsong
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to play a powerful note that repels foes and empowers allies. The note is audible out to 100 feet. Enemies within a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 2d6} Thunder damage and is pushed 10 feet away from you. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.

Allies within the {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} that can hear the note are bolstered by it instead. Each ally affected by the note gains {@dice 2d6} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Fire Dervish Cloak
entries: This breathable cloak sounds like a crackling fire as it billows behind you and can create a small, fiery cyclone around you as you twirl in it. The cloak has 5 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. Once on each of your turns, you can expend 1, 2, or 3 charges from the cloak (no action required) and reduce your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by 10, 20, or 30 feet, respectively, until the end of the turn in order to spin in place instead. When you do, each creature within 5 feet of you must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 1d8} Fire damage for each charge you expended, and it can't make {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} against you until the end of the turn.

item.name: Fire Fire!
entries: This hollow metal crossbow was created by a team of kobolds and contains an enchanted ember at its core. The crossbow has 3 charges. While the crossbow has at least 1 charge, the inner ember burns brightly when you hold the weapon. While it burns, the crossbow emits {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet, and any nonmagical Bolt fired from the crossbow catches fire and deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Fire damage on a hit.

You can also expend 1 charge as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cause the ember to spew forth devastating flames, sending out a wave of fire in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. Each creature in the {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried start burning.

The crossbow regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the magical ember inside the crossbow dies out, and the weapon becomes nonmagical.

item.name: Fire Wand of the Unbroken Circle
entries: This wooden wand is carved out of ash and is always faintly warm to the touch. Whenever you grip the wand's handle and later look at your palm, it's strangely covered in a fine layer of charcoal. This wand has 7 charges.

While holding the wand, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast either Flame Blade (2 charges) or Produce Flame (0 charges, or 1 charge to cast at level 5) from it, using an attack bonus of +5. When you cast the {@spell Flame Blade|XPHB} spell using this wand, the sword's fiery blade appears above the wand's haft instead of in a free hand.

Whenever you would fail a Constitution saving throw to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} on a {@spell Flame Blade|XPHB} spell cast in this way, you can expend 1 charge as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You can only take this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} if you're holding the wand.
The wand regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

item.name: Fireweaver Gloves
entries: While wearing these fine golden gloves, you can cast the {@spell Produce Flame|XPHB} cantrip from them (+5 to hit). You can hold this flame and other small nonmagical ones (such as that from a Candle or Torch) as if they were tangible objects and can sculpt them like clay. Once sculpted, a flame retains its form for 1 minute before returning to normal. A nonmagical flame carried in this way burns for 1 hour before dying. If you drop or throw a nonmagical flame, it immediately burns out.

When you cast {@spell Produce Flame|XPHB} using the gloves, the spell has a duration of 1 hour, instead of 10 minutes. In addition, you can place the spell's conjured flame on the ground at your feet (no action required). The placed flame is harmless and burns out after 1 minute, ending the spell, unless you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to pick it back up while within 5 feet of it. Until you pick up the flame, you can't hurl fire as described in the spell unless you cast it again.
In addition, when you take Fire damage while wearing these gloves, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reduce the damage by {@dice 1d8 + 2}, provided you have a free hand.

item.name: Flesh of Dendallen
entries: This patchwork shirt is made of enchanted pieces of stitched-together flesh. The shirt has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dusk. While wearing the shirt, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell False Life|XPHB} spell. In addition, while you're {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d4} plus your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1) at the start of each of your turns. You don't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} in this way if you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition or have taken Fire or Radiant damage since the start of your last turn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: When you put on this cursed shirt for the first time, make a DC 20 Strength saving throw as the fleshy fabric begins to bubble, hiss, and fuse to your skin. On a successful save, you are aware of the curse's effects and can immediately remove the shirt before it extends the curse to you.On a failed save, you become cursed, and the shirt attaches to your flesh; you can't remove the shirt unless you are targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic. Any nonmagical clothing you were wearing under the shirt is destroyed, and magical objects worn beneath it rise to the surface as it seeps through them to attach to your skin.

As long as you remain cursed, you have the Undead creature type in addition to any other creature type you already have, and you have {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Radiant damage. When you remove the shirt after being cursed by it, you take {@damage 4d10} Necrotic damage as the shirt is peeled from your skin.

item.name: Force Gauntlet
entries: This jeweled gauntlet has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a target with a melee attack roll using a weapon in the hand that's wearing this gauntlet or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} with it, you can choose to expend 1 charge as part of the attack.

On a hit, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} Force damage and must make a DC 15 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target is pushed 10 feet away from you. On a successful save, you're pushed 10 feet away from it instead. Targets two or more sizes larger than you automatically succeed on the save.

item.name: Fortune's Fivepence
entries: This electrum coin has a smiling human woman's face on one side (heads) and a scowling fiendish woman's face on the other (tails). While you're within 30 feet of the coin, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and use a command word to magically summon it to either your hand or pocket. Despite its unique appearance, this coin can be spent like a normal Electrum Piece.

entries.name: Fate Flip
entries: This coin has 2 charges. You can attempt to regain expended charges once every 24 hours by flipping a coin. The Fortune's Fivepence regains all expended charges if the coin is heads or 0 if it's tails.

When the coin is on your person and your total for a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} is 10 or lower, you can expend 1 charge (no action required) to channel the coin's magic and forge your own luck. When you do, flip a coin. If it's heads, add {@dice 1d10} to the {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}; if it's tails, subtract {@dice 1d10} instead. The coin loses this property if using it reduces the total of your {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} to 0 or lower.

item.name: Fourarm Bracers
entries: These bracers can summon another pair of spectral arms below your existing ones. These arms can be summoned as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and last for up to 1 minute before disappearing. While active, you can use the spectral arms to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial.
Each arm can carry up to 10 pounds. The arms stay attached to your torso at all times and can't be moved to other parts of your body, but they can be turned around to face the opposite direction.

In addition, the gems adorning the bracers can amplify the arms' strength for short bursts. The gems have 5 charges and regain {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can use a command word and expend 1 charge to magically empower the arms and immediately make two {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} with them. The arms use a Strength modifier of +3 and use your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} for their attack rolls. They use a damage die of {@dice 1d4} and deal Force damage on a hit.
Alternatively, you can expend 1 charge when you summon the arms to double the amount of weight they can carry and increase their duration to 5 minutes.

item.name: Fragment of Elder Starlight
entries: This smooth, egg-shaped crystal is about 6 inches tall, weighs 3 pounds, and can shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} out to a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. The light is sunlight. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can touch the crystal to mentally control the light's brightness, magically reducing or enlarging its area (out to its maximum range) or snuffing it out.

While holding the crystal, you can use a command word as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to cause it to release a brilliant wave of light. The light washes over each creature that isn't behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} in a 60-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the crystal. Undead creatures and Fiends in the area must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 8d6} Radiant damage and has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only. Allies of your choice in the area regain {@dice 4d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, provided they're living creatures.

Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn, and the crystal's light dims. While dimmed in this way, the crystal can only shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} out to a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet.

item.name: Frefil's Tiny Tasty Tongue Twisty Sugarbombs
entries: This small bag of marble-sized gumballs contains 12 enchanted candies. While the color and flavor of each gumball is different, each one's effect is the same. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can start to chew one of these gumballs to release its magic. While you chewing the candy, you understand the literal meaning of any language that you hear or see signed. If you speak while chewing the gumball, your accent changes to match that of the creature to whom you're listening, allowing you to sound like a local with minimal effort. The candy's magic and flavor fade 5 minutes after you first start to chew it.

entries.name: Upset Stomach
entries: You can only be under the effects of one sugarbomb that has this property at a time. Eating a sugarbomb that has this property while under the effects of another one causes the effects of the first sugarbomb to immediately end and be replaced by the new one's. This rapid change is difficult to stomach, forcing you to make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute.

item.name: Frost Giant Fork
entries: This massive utensil comes from the table of a frost giant. Latent jotun magic flows through this makeshift {@item Trident|XPHB} and drives your hunger to nearly aggressive levels. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Immediately after you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using the {@item Trident|XPHB}, you can attempt to bite the same target as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Make a melee attack roll using your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and Strength modifier. It deals Piercing damage on a hit, and uses a damage die of {@dice 1d6} as your jaws and teeth are fortified to have the strength of a giant.

entries.name: Curse
entries: While you're attuned to this weapon, your hunger is almost insatiable. You're overwhelmingly compelled to eat twice the amount of food you normally would, and gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level from malnutrition if you go a day without eating enough.

item.name: Frostbitten Buckler
entries: This icy Shield has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. When a creature you can see hits you with a melee attack roll, you can expend 1 charge as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to deal {@damage 2d4} Cold damage to the attacker. After the attack, you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Cold damage dealt.

item.name: Frozen Dagger
entries: Attacks made with this {@item Dagger|XPHB} deal Cold damage instead of Piercing. When you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll made with this magic weapon, the target must make a DC 12 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn as it's frozen in place. On a successful save, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved until the end of its next turn instead, provided it doesn't have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition.

item.name: Funeral Marchers
entries: When you gain the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition as a result of being reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} while wearing these black leather boots, you can choose to not gain the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. While you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition and are standing in this way, you can walk up to 10 feet on your turn after making a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB}.

item.name: Galvanic Steelsnare
entries: This magic {@item Net|PHB} is made with fine steel chains and has the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|Heavy} property. {@action Magic|XPHB}, leaden weights line the edge of the {@item Net|PHB} and send shocking pulses throughout the metal. A creature can't make more than one attack during its turn while it has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition from this {@item Net|PHB}.

While a creature within 30 feet of you has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition from the {@item Net|PHB}, you can use a command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to cause the {@item Net|PHB} to magically release a painful wave of electricity, causing the target to take {@damage 1d6} Lightning damage. If the target is made of metal or wearing metal armor, it takes {@damage 2d6} Lightning damage instead. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the start of your next turn.

To escape, the target or a creature within 5 feet of it must take an action to make a DC 15 Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check, freeing the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature on a success. A Small or smaller creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this check. Destroying the {@item Net|PHB} (AC 12; 15 HP; {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Slashing damage; {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to all other damage) also frees the target, ending the effect. Once destroyed, the {@item Net|PHB} magically repairs itself over the course of {@dice 1d6 + 1} days.

item.name: Gibberbox
entries: This magic box is made of enamel and bits of bone that stick together with the gummy, red remains of a gibbering mouther. The box weighs 7 pounds and is 1 foot long and 6 inches wide and deep. You can open the box's wooden lid as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. When you do, the red paste inside the box swirls and forms a toothy mouth that starts incessantly babbling nonsense. A creature takes {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage if it touches the red paste and toothy mouth while the box is opened and babbling in this way.

Each creature that starts its turn within 20 feet of the open box and can hear the babbling must make a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature can't make {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} until the start of its next turn and rolls a {@dice d8} to determine what it does during the current turn. On a 1-4, the creature does nothing. On a 5-6, the creature takes no action or {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and uses all its movement to move in a random direction. On a 7-8, the creature makes a melee attack against a randomly determined creature within its reach or does nothing if it can't make such an attack.

This effect lasts for 1 minute or until the box's lid is closed again as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. Once this property is used, it can't be used again for 24 hours. Opening the lid again while this property is inactive results in a droning, gurgling sound that turns silent after 1 minute of the box being open. The paste is extremely elastic and can't be removed or separated from the box.

item.name: Give and Take
entries: This pair of mithral (Give) and obsidian (Take) Scimitars are connected by a sturdy chain. When you make an extra attack as a result of using the Scimitars' {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack if you aren't already adding it to the damage.

The Scimitars have a collective 10 charges and regain {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at midday. When you hit a creature with one of these magic weapons, you can create an effect based on the blade used for the attack, as detailed below.

entries.name: Give
entries: When you hit a creature with Give and expend 1 charge, you and all other creatures within 5 feet of the target (other than the target) gain {@dice 2d6} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

entries.name: Take
entries: When you hit a creature with Take and expend 1 charge, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Necrotic damage, and you and all other creatures within 5 feet of the target take half as much Necrotic damage.

item.name: Glove of the Aegis
entries: This glove has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d3 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. When a target that you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend any number of the glove's charges. For each charge expended in this way, you gain a +2 bonus to AC for the attack, potentially causing it to miss instead.
The glove's power can't be used if you are already wielding a Shield.

item.name: Glove of the Grim Fandango
entries: You are proficient with all Musical Instruments while you wear this glove on your dominant hand. In addition, spectral musicians appear behind you to accompany your playing when you play an instrument for at least 1 minute. The musicians are obviously illusory and vanish after you stop playing for 1 minute. While accompanied by the spectral musicians, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Performance|XPHB}) checks.
In addition, you gain a +2 bonus to your spell save DC for Enchantment spells you cast while performing with the spectral musicians if the target can hear your performance.

item.name: Grass Carpet
entries: This soft roll of turf is magically fertile. Its soil is 1 inch thick and allows plants of all kinds to grow well within it. Each roll of turf is 10 feet long and 5 feet wide. Its grass and soil are self-containing and will not grow beyond the size of the carpet. The carpet is so fertile that plants growing in its soil require only half the normal amount of sunlight and water they would normally need in order to grow.

For every 1 inch of the soil's depth, the carpet provides the same amount of nutrition as normal soil does with 1 foot, allowing plants whose roots are normally 1 foot in length to grow with only 1 inch of this soil. Folding the carpet over for added depth, or layering multiple Grass Carpets on top of one another, increases the soil depth to allow for larger plants to grow on the carpet.

The carpet weighs 30 pounds and takes 1 minute to roll up or out. While rolled up, it has a diameter of 1 foot. The carpet can be cut into smaller plots using a magical blade, with each plot measuring at least 1 foot long and wide.

item.name: Grass Whistle Blade
entries: This {@item Dagger|XPHB}'s blade is actually a large, enchanted blade of grass. As wind passes over the blade, it creates a soft, sweet note that magically begins to lull creatures to sleep. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

The {@item Dagger|XPHB} has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using the {@item Dagger|XPHB}, you can choose to forgo the attack's damage and expend 1 charge to attempt to put the creature to sleep. When you do, you swing the blade in a pattern around the creature that creates a magical lullaby. The target must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn, at which point it must repeat the save. If the target fails the second save, it has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. This effect ends on a target if it takes damage or someone within 5 feet of it takes an action to shake it out of the effect. If the target succeeds on the second save, it takes {@damage 3d8} Psychic damage instead.
Creatures that don't sleep, such as elves, or that have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} condition automatically succeed on saves against this spell.

item.name: Gravity Goblet
entries: This glass and silver goblet is enchanted with a harmless, strange magic. A liquid poured in the cup bends gravity in comical ways, often to the surprise and drenching of its drinker. When the cup is tilted, as if to drink, the liquid flows in the opposite direction of gravity, sliding up the cup instead of down it. Once the liquid reaches the edge of the cup, it immediately pours down as normal. Swirling the liquid causes it to flow in the opposite direction in which it's rotated. Any solid object other than ice that floats in the liquid is not affected by the cup's magic.

item.name: Greaves of Dendallen
entries: While wearing these skeletal greaves, you have a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can stand on and move across any liquid surface as if it were solid ground. In addition, once on each of your turns, you can jump up to 30 feet by spending only 10 feet of movement.

entries.name: Curse
entries: When you put on these cursed greaves for the first time, make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw as your legs suddenly ache. On a successful save, you are aware of the curse's effects and can immediately remove the greaves before they extend the curse to you. On a failed save, you become cursed, and your legs fracture; you take {@damage 6d8} Necrotic damage, and you can't remove the greaves unless you are targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic. This Necrotic damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}.

While wearing the greaves, your legs remain broken but don't hinder your movement. When you remove them after being cursed by them, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} becomes 0. For every 24 hours that pass, your legs slowly heal and your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 5 feet (until it returns to normal).

item.name: Grip of Dendallen
entries: These black leather and bone chain gloves spread sickness and decay to anything they touch. While wearing the gloves, your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} deal {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage instead of the usual Bludgeoning damage. This damage is maximized if you hit a plant or Plant creature with the attack.

In addition, while wearing the gloves, you can cast one of the following spells (save DC 16, +8 attack bonus) from them: {@spell Blight|XPHB}, {@spell Contagion|XPHB}, or {@spell Vampiric Touch|XPHB}. This version of Blight has a range of Touch instead of its normal range. Once the gloves have been used to cast a spell, they can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dusk.

entries.name: Curse
entries: When you pull these cursed gloves over your hands for the first time, make a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw. On a successful save, you are aware of the curse's effects and can immediately remove the gloves before they extend the curse to you. On a failed save, you become cursed, and the gloves' bone chains pierce your skin, latching onto the bone in your arms; you take {@damage 4d6} Piercing damage, and you can't remove the gloves unless you are targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic.

While wearing the gloves, the flesh on your hands slowly rots. The stench can't be contained or masked through magical or nonmagical means. While your hands are rotting in this way, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma checks, and immediately after you regain all lost {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you take {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage. When you remove the gloves after being cursed by them, it takes {@dice 1d4} days for your rotting flesh to return to normal and its scent to fade.

item.name: Halberd of the Peacock
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This {@item Halberd|XPHB} has been polished to a mirror finish and is adorned with five ornate gemstones dangling beneath its blade that glow with arcane energy. The weapon has 5 charges, each stored within one of the gems, and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. You can expend 1 charge to cast {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} or 2 charges to cast {@spell Alter Self|XPHB}, {@spell Mirror Image|XPHB}, or {@spell Shatter|XPHB} (save DC 15). The hanging gems lose their glow once their charge has been spent in this way.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This {@item Halberd|XPHB} is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. As long as you remain cursed, you are unwilling to part with the {@item Halberd|XPHB}, keeping it within reach at all times. You also have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls with weapons other than this one. Whenever you reduce a creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} using this weapon, you must succeed on a DC 12 Wisdom saving throw or be compelled to admire yourself in the mirror until the start of your next turn, sacrificing any remaining movement, actions, {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB|Bonus Actions}, or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} you may have had. You automatically fail any Dexterity saving throw you make while admiring yourself in the mirror in this way.

item.name: Hand of the Master
entries: This satin glove has no visible seams and can be easily flipped inside-out to wear on either a left or right hand. While attuned to the glove, you can fit it over a hand that is either missing fingers or missing entirely and have it function as a normal body part.

In addition, you can cast the {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB} cantrip at will using the glove. When you do, the spectral hand originates within the glove instead of at a point of your choosing. The glove covers the spectral hand as it carries out your commands, leaving your hand or limb uncovered while it does so. Casting Mage Hand in this way doubles both the duration of the spell and the amount of weight the hand can carry.

item.name: Harp of Valor
entries: This elegant bow has 10 strings and can also be used as both a harp and a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB}. You are proficient with this harp while attuned to the weapon, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Performance|XPHB}) checks made with the harp. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, Arrows fired from the bow deal Thunder damage instead of Piercing.

The bow has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the bow, you can expend no more than 5 charges to cast either the Magic Missile or {@spell Thunderwave|XPHB} spell from it, using your spell save DC; the spell's level is equal to the number of expended charges.

item.name: Hasty Sugarbomb
entries: This enchanted sweet can be eaten as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to increase your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by 5 feet for 1 hour.

entries.name: Upset Stomach
entries: You can only be under the effects of one sugarbomb that has this property at a time. Eating a sugarbomb that has this property while under the effects of another one causes the effects of the first sugarbomb to immediately end and be replaced by the new one's. This rapid change is difficult to stomach, forcing you to make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute.

item.name: Hat Trick
entries: This gaudy purple hat allows the wearer to perform some minor magical feats. The hat has 3 charges and regains 1 expended charge daily at dawn.

While holding the hat, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB} spell from it as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action instead of its normal casting time, pulling the familiar from the hat. The summoned familiar must take the form of either a rabbit or dove, using the statistics of a Weasel or Owl, respectively. The familiar remains for up to 1 minute and can't be used to deliver spells.
In addition, while wearing the hat, you can cast the {@spell Prestidigitation|XPHB} cantrip from it, using only the "sensory effect" or "minor creation" (illusory image) options.

item.name: Hcor'uk the Colossal's Portable Handheld Weapon of Walloping
entries: This {@item Flail|XPHB} has the Heavy, Reach, and Two-Handed properties. The {@item Flail|XPHB} deals an extra {@damage 1d8} Bludgeoning damage on a hit, or {@dice 2d8} if the target is a structure. When you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll made with this magic weapon against a Huge or smaller creature, the target must succeed on a DC 16 Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
The rope and boulder that make this weapon are magically attached and can't be separated. The boulder weighs 200 pounds but is effortless for you to lift once you attune to the weapon.

item.name: Hellfire Pitchfork
entries: This menacing, black iron {@item Trident|XPHB} is warm to the touch, weighs 10 pounds, and has the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|Heavy} property. As a result of its weight, the {@item Trident|XPHB} has a normal throwing range of 15 feet and a long range of 45 feet. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra {@damage 1d8} Radiant damage on a hit. While the {@item Trident|XPHB} is within 60 feet of you, you can use a command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically cause it to disappear in a puff of smoke and reappear in one of your open hands. If you use the command word immediately after making a ranged attack roll with the {@item Trident|XPHB}, it erupts in a 5-foot-high pillar of flame, instead of smoke, before it reappears in your hand. Any creature in the {@item Trident|XPHB}'s space when it erupts in this way must succeed on a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d8} Fire damage.

item.name: Helm of Heroes
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} if you're wearing no armor while wearing this helmet. You can still use a Shield and gain this benefit. In addition, while you aren't wearing armor, the helm becomes suffused with glory and glimmers for 1 minute whenever you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll. While the helm is glimmering in this way, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of each of your turns equal to half your level (round down), and you and your allies within 10 feet of you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

item.name: Helmsman's Shelter
entries: This magic Shield offers a degree of control over the seas. While holding this Shield, you have a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}. This bonus is in addition to the Shield's normal bonus to AC. While attuned to the Shield, you have a +1 bonus to any ability check you make to control a waterborne vehicle.

The Shield has 5 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the Shield, you can expend 3 charges to cast the {@spell Control Water|XPHB} spell (save DC 15) from it. Alternatively, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and expend 1 charge to take control of a water vehicle you can see within 300 feet of you that has a rudder. If the vehicle has a creature currently controlling its rudder, it must make a DC 15 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, you wrestle control away from the creature and can alter the direction of the vehicle by up to 90 degrees. You maintain control of the vehicle until the start of your next turn.

item.name: Hexaxe
entries: This {@item Handaxe|XPHB} amplifies curses bestowed on its enemies. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon against creatures under the effects of a Warlock spell you cast that requires {@status Concentration|XPHB}.

The weapon has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dusk. When you're forced to make a Constitution saving throw to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} on a Warlock spell while you're holding the axe, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to add your Charisma modifier (minimum +1) to the save.

item.name: Hurricane Lance
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

The {@item Lance|XPHB} has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn. You can expend 1 charge as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to surround yourself with a flurry of wind that lasts for 1 minute. While surrounded by this wind, you can take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You can expend a second charge (no action required) while surrounded by the wind to extend the effect to any mount you're currently riding. If you dismount or are forced off your mount, the effect fades from it. The effect ends early if you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition or are no longer holding the {@item Lance|XPHB}.
In addition, you can cast {@spell Thunderwave|XPHB} as a level 3 spell (save DC 16) from the {@item Lance|XPHB}. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Ice Sickle
entries: This magic {@item Sickle|XPHB} from the Everglacier is perpetually covered in tiny, needle-like icicles along its edge that reform almost as soon as they're gone. This weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Cold damage to the first target it hits on each of your turns.

item.name: Immovable Button
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can press this iron shirt button against a piece of fabric and use the command word, "hold." When you do, the button magically attaches to the cloth, fixing itself in place even if it is defying gravity. The button must be attached to a piece of fabric that is either unworn or worn by a willing creature. It can hold up to 500 pounds of weight and magically prevents the cloth from tearing due to tension. More weight causes the button to deactivate and fall.

A creature can take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to make a DC 20 Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check, moving the fixed button up to 10 feet on a successful check. If a creature takes a {@action Study|XPHB} action to examine the button, the creature can determine that it is magical with a successful DC 10 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check, and if it's already activated, the creature knows that the fabric around the button can be cut away. The button remains fixed in place for 1 minute before falling. The button's magic is lost once it's been used in this way.

item.name: Infernal Aegis
entries: While holding this Shield, you can use a command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to cause it to become wreathed in harmless, magical flames. The flames shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. The flames last until you repeat the command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} or until you are no longer holding the Shield.

While holding the Shield, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage and a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}. This bonus is in addition to the Shield's normal bonus to AC. In addition, the Shield has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn. While the Shield is ablaze, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast {@spell Fire Shield|XPHB} (2 charges, warm shield only) or Hellish Rebuke (1 charge, save DC 16) from it.

item.name: Inferno Rope
entries: This 10-foot-long rope smells of sulfur and crackles with embers when moved. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can lay the rope down in a line next to you on solid ground and use a command word to set it ablaze. When you do, the rope becomes a 15-foot-tall wall of fire along its length. The flames are visible, but not opaque, and are 1 inch thick. Creatures other than you within 5 feet of the wall when the rope is ignited must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d4} Fire damage. A creature must also make this save when it enters the wall of fire for the first time on a turn or ends its turn inside the wall. The flames shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 15-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 15 feet, and they vanish after 1 minute.

The rope rematerializes at the base of the flames when they vanish. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn. The rope can only be broken by magical means and becomes a nonmagical Rope once broken.

item.name: Ironleaf Maul of Entanglement
entries: Despite the head of this {@item Maul|XPHB} being made from vines, it seems impossibly solid and carries with it a formidable heft. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The {@item Maul|XPHB} has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. Immediately after you hit a Medium or smaller target with the {@item Maul|XPHB}, you can expend 1 charge to force the target to make a Strength saving throw; the save DC equals 8 plus your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage and has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Ironleaf Oaken Shield
entries: This reinforced wooden Shield is covered with ironleaf vines that grant you extra protection. You gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} while holding it. This bonus is in addition to the Shield's normal bonus to AC. In addition, the Shield reforms itself to fit you whenever you transform into a Beast while holding it. When you do, the ironleaf vines and wooden Shield latch onto your bestial form, granting you a +1 bonus to AC. When you revert to your normal form, the Shield also reverts, donning itself upon your arm again.

item.name: Ironleaf Plate
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} while wearing this suit of armor, which is lined with ironleaf vines. These vines seem to support and quiet the armor's normally loud movements; it doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks. In addition, the vines dampen the impact you receive from blunt force, granting you {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning damage while you wear the armor.
If you take Fire damage while wearing the armor, the vines wither for 1 minute. For the duration, you gain none of their benefits.

entries.name: Mantle of the Wild
entries: While wearing this armor, you can cast the {@spell Pass without Trace|XPHB} spell from it. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Ironshod Trotters
entries: These boots have iron horseshoes embedded in their soles. When you take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action while wearing the boots, you can move an extra 10 feet.

You can click the boots together and cast the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell on yourself, transforming into a Riding Horse. When cast in this way, you also retain your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores, as well as your skill and saving throw proficiencies. Any bags you were carrying don't meld into the new form and are magically draped over your torso as saddlebags. Your other gear melds into the new form. You can't use or otherwise benefit from any of that equipment.

This version of the spell has a duration of 8 hours. The spell ends early if you take any damage while transformed, causing you to take the damage in your normal form instead. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Jotun's Jodhpurs
entries: While wearing these pants, you count as one size smaller while riding a non-Humanoid creature as a mount, provided you aren't wearing Heavy armor.

item.name: Killskull Longbow of Dread
entries: This magic {@item Longbow|XPHB} is adorned with a bear skull and claws. The bow can have up to 4 charges and gains 1 charge whenever you reduce a Small or larger creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} with it. For each charge the bow has, a small, etched line appears on the bear skull adorning the weapon. An etched line vanishes from the skull when its charge is expended.

When you hit a creature with a ranged attack roll using the weapon, you can expend 1 charge to cause the target to take an extra {@damage 1d8} Psychic damage and make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

item.name: Kindle Egg Sugarbomb
entries: When you eat this spicy chocolate sweet as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage for 1 hour, and you can tolerate temperatures as low as -50 degrees Fahrenheit without any additional protection for the duration.

entries.name: Upset Stomach
entries: You can only be under the effects of one sugarbomb that has this property at a time. Eating a sugarbomb that has this property while under the effects of another one causes the effects of the first sugarbomb to immediately end and be replaced by the new one's. This rapid change is difficult to stomach, forcing you to make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute.

item.name: Knight's Standard of Valor
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While wielding this {@item Halberd|XPHB}, you radiate fervor and valor. When you or an ally within 10 feet of you makes a saving throw to avoid or end the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, the creature can add its {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the save if it wouldn't already. In addition, when an ally starts their turn within 10 feet of you, their {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 5 feet until the end of the turn.
You can change the design and color of the weapon's flag by focusing on it over the course of 1 hour, which can be done during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Knight's Standard of Vigor
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While wielding this brass {@item Pike|XPHB}, you radiate health and vitality. When you or an ally within 10 feet of you makes a saving throw to avoid or end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition, the creature can add its {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the save if it wouldn't already. In addition, when an affected ally regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} from a spell or potion while in combat, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cause that ally to regain an extra {@dice 1d6 + 2} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
