You can change the design and color of the weapon's flag by focusing on it over the course of 1 hour, which can be done during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Knight's Standard of Virtue
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While wielding this decorated {@item Glaive|XPHB}, you radiate righteousness. When you or an ally within 10 feet of you makes a saving throw to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition, the creature can add its {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the save if it wouldn't already. In addition, when an ally you can see within 10 feet of you takes damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to magically reduce the damage they take by 5. When you do, you take Force damage equal to the amount of damage you reduced in this way.
You can change the design and color of the weapon's flag by focusing on it over the course of 1 hour, which can be done during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Knocking Boots
entries: These steel-toed boots of dwarven make are adorned with an embossed key on the toe. You can kick an object that contains a mundane or magical means of preventing access and cast the {@spell Knock|XPHB} spell on it using the boots. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Kraken's Whip
entries: This {@item Whip|XPHB} is made using the shrunken tentacle of a great sea creature. It still writhes slightly on its own. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can use the {@item Whip|XPHB} to force a Large or smaller creature you can see within 10 feet of you to make a DC 13 Strength or Dexterity saving throw (its choice).
On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition and takes {@damage 1d4 + 2} Bludgeoning damage.

To escape, the target or a creature within 5 feet of it must take an action to make a DC 13 Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check, freeing the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} creature on a success. While {@condition Grappled|XPHB}, the creature takes {@damage 1d4 + 2} Bludgeoning damage at the end of each of its turns.

You can't use the {@item Whip|XPHB} to attack while grappling a creature in this way. While holding the {@item Whip|XPHB}, you can end the grapple at any time (no action required), and if you move more than 10 feet away from the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} creature, you drag it with you, but every foot of movement costs you 1 extra foot unless the creature is Tiny or two or more sizes smaller than you.

item.name: Lance of the Hungering Dead
entries: This magical, gruesome {@item Lance|XPHB} can drain the life force from another creature. The first time on each of your turns that you hit an enemy with a melee attack roll using the {@item Lance|XPHB}, you deal an extra {@damage 1d12} Necrotic damage to it, provided the target is a living creature. You gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the Necrotic damage dealt (round up).

item.name: Leeching Quarterstaff
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a target with the weapon, you can expend 1 charge to siphon away some of the target's life force, causing it to take an extra {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage. You regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal the Necrotic damage dealt in this way. This property has no effect on Undead or Constructs.

item.name: Liar's Lyre
entries: This magical {@item lyre|XPHB}'s strings reverberate with Enchantment magic when you tell lies, allowing you to deceive other creatures more easily. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB}) checks you make when you spend at least 1 minute attempting to convince a creature of a lie. In addition, when you cast the {@spell Vicious Mockery|XPHB} cantrip while holding the {@item lyre|XPHB}, you have a +1 bonus to your spell save DC. You are proficient with this {@item lyre|XPHB} while you're attuned to it.

item.name: Lightning Pylons
entries: These 3-foot-long rods are made using a blend of magic and science and normally come in sets of three. The pylons are tipped with a fragment of a blue dragon's horn. A Lightning Pylon can extend electricity to any neighboring pylons to create an electrical barrier between them. Each pylon has an electrical charge. Once a charge has been lost, it takes 12 hours to recharge.

You can place a pylon upright in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. If a pylon within 5 feet of you has an electrical charge, you can use a command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically electrify it. When you do, the pylon is electrified for 1 minute, after which it loses its electrical charge. Any other pylon with an electrical charge placed within 20 feet of an electrified pylon also becomes electrified, creating a wall of lightning between them that is 10 feet tall and 1 foot thick. The wall is opaque and sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet. You can have up to six pylons connected in this way at a time. Electrified pylons are magically bound to the ground until they lose their charge.

When the wall appears, each creature within its area must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw; creatures made of metal or wearing metal armor have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this save. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 5d8} Lightning damage, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much Lightning damage, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved instead. A creature must also make this save when it enters the wall for the first time on a turn or ends its turn within 5 feet of the wall.

Each magic pylon has AC 15, HP 20, and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison and Psychic damage. A creature that touches an electrified pylon or hits it with a metal Melee weapon takes {@damage 1d8} Lightning damage for each pylon connected to it, including itself. When a pylon is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it falls over and loses its electrical charge, ending any connection it has to other pylons. A pylon reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} can't be used again until 2 days have passed, at which point it regains all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

item.name: Locksmith's Bane
entries: This silver lock pick twists and reorients itself to more easily open locks. You gain a +3 bonus on Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB}) checks you make with it to pick a lock. If you roll a 1 on such a check using the lock pick, it casts the {@spell Knock|XPHB} spell on the lock before it turns to dust and is destroyed.
If you roll a 1 in this way and use an ability to reroll the die or otherwise change the result, the lock pick becomes nonmagical instead but doesn't cast the spell or turn to dust.

item.name: Love's Embrace
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The weapon can be attuned to by two allies together over the course of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. If two creatures are attuned to the weapon in this way, its bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls becomes +2 instead.

This {@item Greatsword|XPHB} is made of two long Scimitars that are held together by a powerful, indeterminate magic. You can use a command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically separate it into a pair of Scimitars or recombine them into the {@item Greatsword|XPHB}. If you are not already holding the {@item Greatsword|XPHB} or at least one of the Scimitars when you use the command word and are on the same plane of existence as it, you can choose to summon the {@item Greatsword|XPHB} to your free hand or summon one or both Scimitars as individual blades.
The Scimitars have the {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} property with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet when separated. They deal Piercing damage when thrown.

Each {@item Scimitar|XPHB} also has its own elemental effect—either cold or fire—that deals an extra {@dice 1d6} Cold or {@damage 1d6} Fire damage on a hit, respectively. The {@item Greatsword|XPHB} instead deals an extra {@dice 1d6} Cold and {@damage 1d6} Fire damage on a hit.
The weapon can be used to cast the {@spell Fire Shield|XPHB} spell twice, and it regains all expended uses daily at dawn. An attuned creature can't cast the spell in this way more than once each day.

item.name: Mageplate Cap
entries: This pointed hat is fitted with chain and metal that have been enchanted with Abjuration magic. The cap has 2 charges and regains 1 expended charge daily at dawn. While wearing the cap, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Mage Armor|XPHB} spell from it, targeting only yourself. When the spell is cast in this way, the magical force appears as platelike armor, and your base AC becomes 15 plus your Dexterity modifier instead of the AC the spell normally provides.

item.name: Magma War Pick
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Fire damage on a hit. This weapon has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a target that is either made of metal or wearing metal armor using the weapon, you can expend 1 charge to deal an extra {@damage 2d8} Fire damage to the target.

item.name: Mammoth Boots
entries: These magic boots are as sturdy as the feet of a mammoth. While wearing the boots, your weight increases by 50 pounds (although you don't feel any different), and {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} composed of ice or snow doesn't cost you extra movement. In addition, whenever you're pushed, pulled, or otherwise moved against your will, the distance you would be moved is reduced by 5 feet.

item.name: Mantle of the Pack Lord
entries: While wearing this enchanted pelt cloak, creatures don't have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on an attack roll against you as a result of having an ally within 5 feet of you if they normally would, such as from the Pack Tactics trait.

item.name: Mask of the Pact Bearer
entries: This mask looks like a normal wooden mask while no one is attuned to it. When you attune to the mask, your patron's lingering presence over you magically reshapes it to represent the visage of your patron. While wearing this mask, you can cast one spell from the table of spells shown in your level 3 Warlock subclass feature without expending a spell slot. The spell must be of a level equal to or less than your Warlock spell slot level. A spell cast in this way is always cast at its lowest level, regardless of your normal Warlock spell slot level. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Master Machinist's Marvelous Mallet
entries: This immaculate silver hammer is polished to a mirror finish. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

The weapon has 7 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the hammer, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Mending|XPHB} cantrip from it, touching the hammer to the object. Casting Mending in this way affects an area up to 5 feet on a side, instead of 1 foot. Alternatively, you can expend 3 charges as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and touch a creature's armor with the hammer to magically enchant it. The enchantment lasts for 1 minute and grants the armor's wearer a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}. A creature can only be affected by one of these enchantments at a time.

item.name: Mastery Gem
entries: This red gemstone seems to glint with some sort of liquid inside. The gem is immune to all damage. It contains magic absorbed from countless other sources that it uses to overpower the attunement requirements of certain magic items. While holding this gem against an item with an attunement requirement you don't meet, you can ignore that requirement and attune to the item anyway; the item can't be a Legendary item or Artifact. The gem fuses to the surface of the item and encases the area around it in a crystalline shell.

You can end your attunement to the item as normal, even if the item is cursed. Ending your attunement to a cursed item in this way breaks the curse's effects on you. When you end your attunement to an item the gem is fused to, it detaches from the item, allowing you to use it on another one and leaving behind the crystalline shell. When it detaches in this way, it removes the item's magic with it, causing the item to become nonmagical.

item.name: Merry Berry
entries: This small fruit is highly sought after by cooks and sweet-lovers everywhere, both for its flavor as well as its unique sensory-enhancing properties. Eating a Merry Berry raw yields a taste and texture like that of a rich shortbread—the perfect way to end a day of adventure or travel.

Cooking the berry, whether it be in a cuisine or simply roasted over a campfire, brings out more magical effects. When properly cooked, a creature that eats the fruit has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence and Wisdom checks that rely on smell or taste for the next 24 hours, as well as on ability checks made using {@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB} for the duration.
Overcooking the fruit not only cooks out the berry's benefits but also results in a soggy and overall bland dish.

item.name: Mirrorlight Edge
entries: This roughly hewn crystal sword bends light in unexpected and dangerous ways. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can use a command word to cause it to emit up to two ghostly projections of itself. The sword can have up to two of these projections at a time, which last until you dismiss them (no action required). When swung, the ghostly projections make the sword difficult to see. When you miss a target with an attack roll using the sword while it has at least one projection, you can dismiss one of its projections to reroll the {@dice d20}, and you must use the new roll.

When you hit a target with this weapon while it has at least one projection, the ghostly projections become razor sharp. Targets hit by the sword take an extra {@damage 1d4} Slashing damage for each projection it has.

The sword sheds {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius while it has one projection or in a 20-foot radius while it has two. Sheathing the {@item Scimitar|XPHB} contains the projections and their light without dismissing them.

item.name: Mirrorlight Piercer
entries: This crystalline {@item Spear|XPHB} bends the light it generates in such a way that it becomes tangible. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which has the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property.

While you hold the {@item Spear|XPHB} in one hand and have another hand free, you can use a command word as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to create a ghostly projection of the {@item Spear|XPHB} in your free hand. The projection uses the same weapon statistics as the {@item Spear|XPHB}. When you create a projection in this way, you can make one attack roll with either the {@item Spear|XPHB} or the projection as part of that action. If you make a ranged attack roll with the projection, it disappears after it hits or misses the target. If you're still holding the crystalline {@item Spear|XPHB} when this happens, it magically recreates the projection in your free hand again (no action required).

The projection lasts for 1 minute, until you dismiss it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, or until a creature other than you holds it. If you drop or stow the crystalline {@item Spear|XPHB}, its projection disappears until you hold the {@item Spear|XPHB} again. The {@item Spear|XPHB} can only have one projection at a time. While it has a projection, both the {@item Spear|XPHB} and its projection shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet.

item.name: Mockingblade
entries: This magic sword helps you more effectively add insult to injury. You are proficient with the Mockingblade while you're attuned to it. The sword has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a creature with this weapon on your turn, you can immediately take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Vicious Mockery|XPHB} cantrip from it, using your spell save DC, against that creature. When you do, any words you use to insult the creature appear written along the blade for 1 minute or until replaced by a new insult.

When a creature rolls a 20 on its saving throw against a {@spell Vicious Mockery|XPHB} spell cast in this way, the face adorning the sword's hilt screams insults at you instead until the end of your turn. The insults are audible out to 30 feet and are directed at your deepest insecurities and regrets. Any creature that hears these insults is made aware of your weaknesses, granting it {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on its next attack roll against you until the start of your next turn. Once the sword screams at you in this way, it can't be used to cast the {@spell Vicious Mockery|XPHB} spell again until {@dice 1d4} days have passed.

item.name: Monocle of Clarity
entries: This single lens has three filters that you can peer through. You can change the monocle's filter by rotating its outer ring. A small arrow along the edge of the frame indicates the monocle's selected filter. The monocle has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d3 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the monocle, you can expend 1 charge as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to activate one of the following filters and gain the corresponding benefits. These effects last for up to 1 hour, ending early if you take off the monocle or you change its filter.

entries.name: Filter of Comprehend Languages
entries: When you peer through this filter, you can understand any written language you see, as if by the written language portion of the {@spell Comprehend Languages|XPHB} spell.

entries.name: Filter of Darkvision
entries: While looking through this filter, you have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Filter of See Invisibility
entries: When gazing through this filter, you can see creatures and objects that have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition as if they were visible.

item.name: Monsterbane Lasher
entries: This mithral {@item Whip|XPHB} was once used by a powerful monster hunter. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Slashing damage on a hit.

The {@item Whip|XPHB} has seven runes on its handle that correspond to different damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Radiant, or Thunder. When you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using the weapon, you can channel one or more of the runes to deal an extra {@damage 2d4} damage of the corresponding type or types. For example, you can channel two runes to add {@dice 2d4} Acid and {@damage 2d4} Fire damage to an attack against a troll, or one rune to add {@damage 2d4} Thunder damage to an attack against a clay golem. Once you channel a rune in this way, that rune can't be channeled again until the next dawn.

item.name: Necrolace
entries: This necklace allows you to absorb the life energy of creatures you slay. When you reduce a Small or larger enemy to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the necklace emits a faint glow and gains 1 charge. The necklace can have up to 3 charges and has {@dice 1d3} charges when found. You can expend 1 or more charges as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically turn death into life. You take {@damage 1d8} Necrotic damage for each expended charge, and another creature you can see within 30 feet of you regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice the amount of Necrotic damage you took. This damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}.

If this damage reduces you to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition but are {@variantrule Stable|XPHB}. When this happens, the target regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice the amount of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} you had remaining before you took this damage.

item.name: Nightstalker's Kanabo
entries: This studded {@item Greatclub|XPHB} once belonged to a hungering oni who excelled at subterfuge. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which has the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|Heavy} property. When you hit a creature that has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition with this weapon, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d8} Psychic damage. In addition, when you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll made with this weapon, the target must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or magically have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.

The condition ends on the target if it takes damage or someone takes an action to shake it out of the effect. Creatures that don't sleep, such as elves, or that have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} condition automatically succeed on saves against this effect.

The weapon has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dusk. While holding the weapon, you can expend 1 charge as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to cast either Invisibility (targeting only yourself) or Sleep from it.

item.name: Null Chalk
entries: This drab pouch contains {@dice 1d4 + 1} sticks of dull gray chalk. The chalk feels slightly tingly to the touch.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch one of the sticks of chalk to the ground, wall, or ceiling to magically create a line that is up to 50 feet long and 1 foot thick. The line extends from your location in the direction and shape of your choice. You can shape the line in any way you choose as long as it makes one continuous path along the surface or similar adjacent surfaces. Once a stick of chalk has been used in this way, it disintegrates into a pile of fine, nonmagical powder.

Magical effects can't cross this line. The line's effect extends 20 feet perpendicularly from the surface on which it is drawn. Targets have {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} against spells and other magical effects originating from the other side of the line. {@action Magic|XPHB} items that cross the line become mundane and nonmagical for 1 round unless they're Artifacts.

The first time a Celestial, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, or Undead attempts to cross the line on a turn, that creature must make a DC 16 Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} becomes 0 until the start of its next turn, it's forced backward up to 10 feet away from the line, and it takes {@damage 2d8} Force damage. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only, and it passes through the line. If the line cuts through an affected creature's space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side of the line (you choose which side).

This effect lasts for 8 hours or until a break of at least one foot is made in the line, which causes it to instantly lose all magical properties. A creature can spend 1 minute to smudge, smear, erase, or otherwise remove the line enough to disable its effects. When targeted by the {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} spell, the line is treated as if it were a level 6 spell. If the line is dispelled in this way, its effects are disabled for 1 minute.

item.name: Omenbringer's Mantle
entries: This raven-feathered mantle is almost iridescent. While wearing the mantle, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage, and your rate of descent when falling slows to 60 feet per round, taking no damage from the fall when you land.

In addition, while wearing the mantle, you can cast the {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB} spell from it as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action instead of its normal casting time. When you do, the familiar takes the form of a Raven. If the familiar drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it can't be summoned again until the next dusk. Otherwise, the familiar remains as long as you are attuned to the mantle.

item.name: Orator's Quill
entries: This red feather quill generates its own mundane ink and acts on its own as you speak to it. While holding the quill, you can use a command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to activate it. While activated, the quill magically floats upright and writes the words you speak to it on whatever stationary, nonliving surface it's been placed on. When it reaches the edge of that surface, it begins a new line below before continuing. If the quill reaches the end of the surface, such as a piece of parchment, it taps against the surface expectantly until a new one is provided. You must be within 15 feet of the quill in order to speak to it. If the quill is behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}, it can't understand you. If you repeat the command word, even if you're not holding the quill, it deactivates and falls down. If the quill doesn't write anything for 1 minute, it deactivates on its own.

item.name: Otyugh Cloak
entries: This gruesome, magical otyugh pelt still holds some of its natural abilities. A single tendril bearing three eyes hangs from the back of the cloak's hood. While wearing the cloak, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to telepathically communicate with a creature you can see within 60 feet of you that can understand a language, sending either simple messages or images when you do. The receiving creature can't respond to these messages.

The tendril hanging from the hood is 3 feet long and offers some limited articulation. While wearing the cloak, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to mentally control the tendril enough to perform a simple task within 5 feet of you, such as to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial. The tendril can't attack, activate magic items, or carry more than 10 pounds.

In addition, while wearing the hood, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to close your eyes and magically see through the eyes of the tendril. When you do, you have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. You can control the tendril while looking through its eyes in this way. You return to your normal senses when you reopen your eyes or lower the hood.

item.name: Ouroboros Rapier
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Whenever you roll an 8 on the damage die for an attack made with this weapon, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d8} Poison damage. In addition, when you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll made with this weapon against a creature, the target must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 3d6} Poison damage and has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

item.name: Overshield
entries: While holding this large Shield, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}. This bonus is in addition to the Shield's normal bonus to AC. The bonus becomes +2 while you have at least 1 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB|Temporary Hit Point}. While you have {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, the Shield emits a slight blue glow.

In addition, you can channel the heroic resolve within the Shield as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to gain {@dice 1d6 + 8} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Pack of LeviBubble Sugarbombs
entries: This delicious gum comes in packs of five and can be blown into large, levitating bubbles 1 foot in diameter. You need to chew a piece of the gum for 1 minute before its magic can be used. Once it's been chewed for at least 1 minute, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while the gum is in your mouth to blow a single bubble with it. When you do, you gain the effect of the {@spell Levitate|XPHB} spell, as if you had cast it, provided the bubble stays attached to the gum in your mouth. While the effect doesn't require your {@status Concentration|XPHB}, it does end early and cause you to fall if you take an action to pop it or if the bubble takes any damage; the bubble is a loose object for the purpose of any attack or effect that would target it. The effect ends on its own after 10 minutes, allowing you and the bubble to float gently down to the ground if you're still aloft. The gum becomes nonmagical, albeit still delicious, once this bubble's effect ends.

When you take the {@action Magic|XPHB} action to blow this bubble, you can remove it from your mouth as part of the same action and stick it on another creature or loose object within 5 feet of you. When you do, the stuck target gains the effect of the {@spell Levitate|XPHB} spell, instead of you, but you have no control over it; the effect otherwise functions as described above. An unwilling creature that succeeds on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw avoids having the bubble stuck to it.

item.name: Pair of Tiny Violin Rings
entries: This pair of golden rings play a sad, sad melody when you rub the two fingers wearing them together. Creatures that know the {@spell Vicious Mockery|XPHB} cantrip can use this set of rings as a Somatic component for the spell instead of the normal Verbal component required by it.

item.name: Pearl of Wisdom Quiver
entries: This magic Quiver is made from the shell of an enormous oyster. Arrows you pull from it are magical and are coated with a pearlescent, hydrophobic coating. Ranged attack rolls made with these Arrows ignore the normal drawbacks of underwater combat. The Arrows become nonmagical once they hit a target or are held by a creature other than you.

entries.name: Create Pearl
entries: You can use a command word to cause the Quiver to create a magical pearl. You can hold onto this pearl or give it to another creature. When a creature with the pearl makes a Wisdom check or Wisdom saving throw, it can choose to make the check or save with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. When it does, the pearl turns to dust. The pearl is visibly magical and disintegrates on its own after 24 hours if left unused. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Pendant of the Tempered Fury
entries: This magic silvered pendant glows with a simmering flame of rage trapped inside its clenched fist. Small sparks shoot out from between the pendant's metal fingers. The fiery anger from the pendant tightens your focus and allows you to shrug off some of the damage you take. If you aren't wearing armor or wielding a Shield while you wear this pendant, all Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage you take is reduced by 3.

In addition, the pendant has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to ignite your hands in a magical flame, causing them to shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet. Whenever you deal damage with your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} while your hands are wreathed in this fire, it can deal your choice of Fire damage or its normal damage type, and it deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Fire damage on a hit. Your hands remain lit for 1 minute or until you take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to quench them.
Any flame created by the pendant, whether it be on your hands or on the pendant itself, is harmless to you.

item.name: Pendant of Thieves' Sight
entries: While wearing this pendant, you know Thieves' Cant. In addition, you can more easily recognize the patterns used in linguistic codes, granting you a +5 bonus to Intelligence checks made to understand a written or spoken cipher.

item.name: Peppermince Sugarbomb
entries: This hard, minty sugarbomb slowly dissolves in your mouth over the course of 1 hour. At any point, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to crunch down and break the sugarbomb into tiny, painful shards. You take {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage from the shards but are temporarily enchanted by the candy. Whenever you hit a target with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} that deals Piercing or Slashing damage while you're enchanted in this way, the target takes an extra 2 damage. This enchantment fades after 1 minute.

entries.name: Upset Stomach
entries: You can only be under the effects of one sugarbomb that has this property at a time. Eating a sugarbomb that has this property while under the effects of another one causes the effects of the first sugarbomb to immediately end and be replaced by the new one's. This rapid change is difficult to stomach, forcing you to make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute.

item.name: Periapt of Reflection
entries: This perfectly reflective charm magically helps you meditate and focus your mind. Whenever you fail an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw while wearing the necklace, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reroll the save, and you must use the new roll.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This periapt is cursed, a fact that is only revealed when the curse extends to you due to rolling a 1 on a saving throw after using the periapt to reroll it. Until the curse ends, the mirrored surface of the necklace dulls into an ugly, flat gray while you wear it. The curse persists even if you aren't wearing the necklace, and you remain cursed until you are targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic.

While you're cursed, your reflection in any mirrored surface within 10 feet of you turns into an ugly, horrifying visage of your deepest insecurities. In addition, when you fail an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw (including the roll that reveals this curse), you take {@damage 1d8} Psychic damage.

item.name: Permanent Parchment
entries: This violet piece of parchment is smooth and pleasant to write on. You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and use either of the command words, "lock" or "unlock," to magically lock or unlock the parchment, respectively. While locked, the sheet can't be torn by hand, damaged by fire, or damaged by immersion in water, and any ink already on the page can't be removed, smeared, or otherwise changed without the use of magic. New ink added to a locked sheet of Permanent Parchment runs off the page like water. While unlocked, the parchment behaves like a nonmagical sheet of paper.
If the sheet is unlocked, you can use another command word, "clear," to cause any ink on the sheet of parchment to magically evaporate without leaving any marks behind.

item.name: Petal Dancer Ward
entries: This cape is made of colorful and aromatic petals. While wearing the cape, petals drift behind you as you walk and disappear before touching the ground. You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically change the color of these petals. While wearing the cape, you can cast the {@spell Druidcraft|XPHB} cantrip from it, and Beasts and Plant creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} against you.

item.name: Phase Axe
entries: This crystalline {@item Battleaxe|XPHB} seems to be almost transparent when looking at the bladed head from different angles. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which has the {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} property with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. The first time on your turn when you hit a target with a ranged attack roll using the axe, you can force another creature within 10 feet of the target to make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, that creature takes {@damage 1d8} Force damage.

If you can see the axe and are within 60 feet of it, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically return the axe to your outstretched hand. When you do, the weapon phases through any obstacle in its path that isn't more than 5 feet thick or made of lead.

item.name: Pike of the Forgotten Legion
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The weapon has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dusk. While holding this {@item Pike|XPHB}, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 17) from it: {@spell Mirror Image|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Phantom Steed|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Speak with Dead|XPHB} (1 charge), or {@spell Spirit Guardians|XPHB} (2 charges).

entries.name: Spectral Warrior
entries: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with this {@item Pike|XPHB}, you can choose to summon a spectral warrior from it. Until the start of your next turn, the target is haunted by this warrior, causing it to have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against you. This property has no effect against Undead creatures.

entries.name: Haunting Phalanx
entries: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while holding this {@item Pike|XPHB} to expend 3 charges and point it forward commandingly. Five spectral, {@item Pike|XPHB}-wielding warriors appear before you and march in a 25-foot-wide, 50-foot-long {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} in a direction you choose. Any creature within the area must make a DC 17 Intelligence saving throw; Undead creatures automatically succeed on the save. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 3d10} Necrotic damage and {@damage 3d10} Psychic damage and is affected by the weapon's Spectral Warrior property. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.

item.name: Plaguebane Mask
entries: This birdlike mask's blend of magic and science is as unique as it is helpful. While wearing it, you can easily lift it above your face to eat or speak more clearly. The mask's beak is lined with magical flowers that react to and purify airborne toxins before they pass through it. While wearing the mask, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition as well as the effects of magical contagions if they came from an airborne source, and you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to any Poison damage that those effects might deal.

In addition, the flowers have 3 charges and regain {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn as new flowers bloom. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can hold the beak's nostrils closed with a free hand and expend 1 charge to exhale a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} of thick pollen in front of you. Each creature in the area must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 2d8} Poison damage, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved until the end of its next turn unless it has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.

item.name: Planar Rapier
entries: This thin blade seems to toe the line between planes of existence, occasionally allowing it to bend the planes to extend beyond its normal reach. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

The {@item Rapier|XPHB} has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. When you make a melee attack roll with the {@item Rapier|XPHB}, you can expend 1 charge to extend the weapon, increasing its reach for the attack by 5 feet.

Alternatively, you can expend 1 charge immediately after making a melee attack roll with the {@item Rapier|XPHB} against a target within 5 feet of you, causing the weapon to extend and pierce through the original target. Make an additional melee attack roll with the {@item Rapier|XPHB} against a second target 5 feet behind the original one; the second target must be within 10 feet of you. This additional attack is made as part of the initial attack, and the second target doesn't benefit from using the original one as cover.
Whenever you expend a charge from this weapon, the planes around the extended blade begin to tear, causing the target to take an extra {@damage 2d8} Force damage on a hit.
After the attack with the extended blade, it returns to its normal size.

item.name: Pomade of Ten Thousand Styles
entries: This canister of waxy pomade has magical properties, allowing you to change your hair's color, style, thickness, and length (up to 1 foot longer or shorter per use). The canister steadily replenishes its pomade over time. It can hold up to 4 uses at a time and regains {@dice 1d4} expended uses daily at dawn. Applying 1 use of the pomade takes 1 minute.

Once you've used this product in your hair, it returns to its normal appearance after you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Hair produced by the pomade that's been cut off turns to dust after 24 hours. If you use the pomade every day for 7 days to make the same changes, the changes become permanent.

item.name: Potion of Spell Recovery
entries: When you drink this potion, you recover expended spell slots of your choice of levels 1-5. The spell slots must have a combined level that is less than or equal to {@dice 1d3 + 3}.

item.name: Profane Mask
entries: This cloth mask converts the words that pass through it into vile obscenities. While wearing the mask over your mouth, your words are heard as angry insults and swears instead of the ones you intend. You don't hear these words. If a creature takes a {@action Study|XPHB} action to examine the words, the creature can determine that they aren't your own with a successful DC 10 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check. Verbal spell components, command words, and actual obscenities you say are unaffected by the mask's magic and are heard normally by creatures within earshot.

item.name: Purging Dagger
entries: This chiseled wooden {@item Dagger|XPHB} is a relic crafted from the bough of a holy tree and is always miraculously sharp. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Radiant damage on a hit. This extra damage increases to {@dice 2d4} when you hit an Undead with the {@item Dagger|XPHB}, causing blue tongues of holy flame to flash along the weapon's edge.

When you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll made with this weapon against an Undead, you can choose to plunge the blade into the target's body and leave it there. When you do, the {@item Dagger|XPHB} emits {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 15-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 15 feet. The light is sunlight. While the blade is lodged in a target in this way, that target takes {@damage 3d4} Radiant damage at the start of each of its turns, and the {@item Dagger|XPHB} continues to emit sunlight. To end this effect, the target or a creature within 5 feet of it must take an action to make a DC 16 Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check, removing the {@item Dagger|XPHB} on a success. Once removed, the blade vanishes and reappears on the ground at your feet or in your free hand (your choice each time).

item.name: Purity Spear
item.reqAttune: by a Creature of Good Alignment
entries: This beautiful {@item Spear|XPHB} has a long handle carved of ivory and is tipped with the spiraling horn of a unicorn. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

While attuned to the {@item Spear|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison damage and the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. In addition, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution and Wisdom saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

The {@item Spear|XPHB} has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the {@item Spear|XPHB}, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 17) from it: {@spell Calm Emotions|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Detect Evil and Good|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Dispel Evil and Good|XPHB} (5 charges), {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} (5 charges), {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} (2 charges), or {@spell Purify Food and Drink|XPHB} (1 charge). In addition, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to expend 1 charge to touch a creature with the {@item Spear|XPHB}. The target regains {@dice 2d8 + 2} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition ends on it if it had that condition.

entries.name: Charge
entries: If you move at least 20 feet in a straight line toward a target immediately before hitting it with a melee attack roll using the {@item Spear|XPHB}, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d8} Piercing damage.

entries.name: Curse Breaker
entries: While attuned to the {@item Spear|XPHB}, you are immune to being cursed by spells and other effects. If you are already affected by a curse when you attune to the {@item Spear|XPHB} or are attuned to a cursed item, the effects of the curse are suppressed for you as long as you remain attuned to the {@item Spear|XPHB}.

item.name: Purse Piglet
entries: This ceramic piglet figurine is glazed with a friendly pink coating that's soft to the touch, and it has a hole in the top that can be stoppered with a cork. When you drop a coin in the piglet and stopper it, the figurine animates and imprints on you, considering you its owner. As the piglet's owner, you can magically learn how much money is inside it by holding and gently shaking it, causing it to oink happily.

Once animate, the piglet happily eats any coin handed to it, storing it inside. The piglet will spit out exact change when you or an ally asks for it, even converting the coins within it into larger or smaller denominations. The figurine can only hold coins and will spit or sneeze out anything else you place inside it. The piglet squeals with glee and prances about excitedly when you deposit or feed it coins. The piglet can eat up to 1,000 coins of any denomination every hour it spends eating them; it will eat Electrum Pieces with less enthusiasm. The piglet remains the same size regardless of how many coins are inside it.

The piglet is exceptionally loyal and will listen to commands given by you and allies you designate. It walks beside you or the nearest designated ally in their space as best it can, but it vanishes into a pocket dimension if neither you nor a designated ally is within 30 feet of it. The figurine can be magically summoned to you or a designated ally as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} by using the command words, "Come home, little piggy." You or a designated ally can tell the figurine to return to its pocket dimension by taking a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to use the command words, "Go to market, little piggy."

When asking for 10 percent or more of its total stockpile, the piglet looks disappointed before carrying out the request. The piglet can eject its own stopper and roll over on its back to empty itself of coins when it's asked for a large amount, but it will always retain at least one Copper Piece unless you physically remove it. The figurine returns to its inanimate form when there are no more coins inside it.

The piglet is a Tiny object and can be attacked while it is animate. It has AC 10, HP 5, {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison and Psychic damage, and {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Bludgeoning damage. If the piglet drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it cries out and shatters into hundreds of ceramic pieces, causing the coins inside it to scatter. The piglet rematerializes before you again after {@dice 1d4} days. If you die, the piglet becomes distraught and empties itself of all its coins, returning to its inanimate ceramic form atop whatever coins were inside it.

Placing the piglet inside an extradimensional space created by a {@item Bag of Holding|XDMG}, {@item Portable Hole|XDMG}, or similar item instantly destroys both items and opens a gate to the Astral Plane. When this happens, the piglet is permanently destroyed, and its contents are lost. The gate originates where the one item was placed inside the other. Any creature within 10 feet of the gate and not behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} is sucked through it and deposited in a random location on the Astral Plane. The gate then closes. The gate is one-way and can't be reopened.

item.name: Puzzle Ring of Vitality, Life, and Power
entries: This ring is blessed by a god of life and healing. While wearing the ring, you can use your Lay On Hands on a creature up to 10 feet away from you instead of needing to touch it.

entries.name: The Outer Ring
entries: The inner, golden ring is covered by a silver puzzle ring that grants various benefits. The puzzle ring is made up of three distinct bands. As the bands are fitted together in an elaborate and ever-changing puzzle, you radiate an aura that heals and supports you and your allies. As a Study action while holding or wearing the ring, you can attempt to solve one band of the puzzle, which must each be attempted within a set amount of time and in a fixed order:

items: The first band slides into place with a successful DC 5 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check. Once solved, you begin to radiate an aura in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}, which lasts for 1 minute. When you create the aura and at the start of each of your turns while it persists, you can restore {@dice 2d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to one creature in it.

The second band can then be solved with a successful DC 10 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check. When it slides into place, the aura's power increases. While in the aura, you and your allies have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage, and your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximums can't be reduced. If an ally with 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} starts its turn in the aura, that ally regains 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}.

After that, the third and final band can be solved with a successful DC 15 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check. When the band fits into place to complete the puzzle, the aura's power is maximized. While in the aura, you and your allies have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects. When an affected creature makes a saving throw against a spell or magical effect that allows a save to take only half damage, it takes no damage if it succeeds on the save.

entries: The aura ends early if you take off the ring or drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Once the aura ends, this property can't be used again until 1 day has passed for each band that you solved. If you solve all three bands, roll {@dice 1d3} when the aura ends. On a 1 or 2, the number of days that must pass before this property can be used again is reduced by the number rolled. On a 3, this property can be used again immediately.

item.name: Quagmire Maul
entries: This old, withered hammer seemingly belongs in a swamp. Regardless of the conditions the hammer is kept in, it is always damp and smells of bog water. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

The hammer has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 1 or more charges and slam the hammer into the ground at a point within 5 feet of you. The area in a 5-foot-radius circle centered on that point transforms into a sickly, swamp that lasts for 1 minute. This area is {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. For each charge you expend after the first, the circle's radius increases by 5 feet. While you're holding the hammer, you can traverse the magical swamp as if it were normal terrain.
If you expend the last charge from the hammer, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the hammer turns into viscous muck in your hands and is destroyed.

item.name: Quake Hammer
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can smash the hammer into the ground at a point within 5 feet of you or throw it at a point on the ground within 60 feet of you, sending out intense tremors in a 10-foot-radius circle centered on that point. Each creature in the area other than you must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 2d6} Bludgeoning damage and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.
Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Quickdraw
entries: This curious contraption is in fact a small, surreptitious {@item Hand Crossbow|XPHB} that can be worn on a wrist or forearm without requiring a free hand to use it. In order to use the crossbow, you must also wear two special rings on adjacent fingers on the arm the weapon is attached to. One ring carries a thin but flexible string that connects to the firing mechanism of the crossbow. The other ring is equipped with a very small but functional blade that's used to sever the first ring's string. When the string is severed, the crossbow fires. It takes 1 minute to reload the crossbow and reset its rings.

While the rings are set and the crossbow is loaded, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make a ranged attack roll with it. Alternatively, you can make this attack roll as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} at the start of your first turn of a combat.

item.name: Quicksilver Clay
entries: This small orb of silver, watery liquid is strangely dry to the touch. While holding the 1-inch-diameter metallic sphere in the palm of your hand, you can mentally control the form that it takes as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. The liquid stretches and turns into the imagined shape and remains centered in your hand.

Regardless of the form, it can be no larger than the palm of your hand and is always a liquid. You can cause the liquid to revert to its natural orb state at any time (no action required). It immediately reverts if it leaves your hand. Consuming the liquid is harmless, but it takes {@dice 1d4} days to exit the body.

item.name: Radiant Defender
entries: This {@item Maul|XPHB} has a narrow Shield covering its handle that can be rotated to protect the wielder's grip. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

While wielding the guarded {@item Maul|XPHB}, taking the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action also gives you a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn. In addition, when a creature within 5 feet of you misses you with an attack roll while you're benefiting from the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a melee attack with the {@item Maul|XPHB} against that creature. On a hit, the attack deals an extra {@damage 1d8} Radiant damage. Once you take this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you lose the {@item Maul|XPHB}'s bonus to AC until you take the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action again.

item.name: Reap and Sew
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals Force damage on a hit instead of Piercing. While attuned to the weapon, you can cast the {@spell Mending|XPHB} cantrip from it.

A special, needle-like Bolt is magically bound to the crossbow. When fired, this Bolt leaves a faint thread of arcane energy behind it. The Bolt returns to the crossbow and loads itself after each attack made with it, allowing you to ignore the crossbow's Loading property.

The crossbow has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a Large or smaller creature with this crossbow's special Bolt, you can expend 1 charge to tether the target to a point on the ground within 5 feet of it. If the target attempts to move more than 10 feet away from that point before the end of its next turn, it must make a DC 15 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn.

If the special Bolt is somehow destroyed or lost, it reappears loaded in the crossbow after 24 hours. The weapon functions like a nonmagical crossbow while separated from the Bolt for longer than 1 minute.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This weapon is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. As long as you remain cursed, you must make a DC 12 Wisdom saving throw whenever you attempt to begin a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. On a failed save, you are overcome with the urge to craft and are forced to sew, knit, crochet, or cross-stitch feverishly for at least 1 hour before being able to rest.

item.name: Red Claw's Regalia
entries: This dragon scale robe is miraculously insulated and comfortable. While wearing the robe, you gain the following benefits:

items: You can tolerate temperatures of 0 degrees Fahrenheit or lower without any additional protection.
Your Constitution increases by 2, to a maximum of 22.
You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws.
You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage.
You can stand on and move across molten rock, unharmed, as if it were solid ground.

entries: The hem of this robe glimmers with slowly shifting embers. While wearing the robe, your steps leave behind small motes of harmless fire and scorched earth that disappear after 1 second.

item.name: Retaliating Bloom Shield
entries: This steel Shield hides a slim chamber in its center where magical flora grow, accumulating toxic pollen. The chamber is covered with an intricate, interlocking series of metal plates and flaps that can be released, unfurling to a shape that resembles a flower.

While holding the Shield with its plates collapsed, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when a Large or smaller creature you can see within 5 feet of you misses you with an attack roll to release the interlocked plates and fire the accumulated pollen at the attacker. When you do, that creature must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 3d10} Poison damage and has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dusk when the flowers accumulate another wave of pollen.

Curious, whirring mechanisms inside the Shield automatically retract the chamber's unfurled metallic plates at dusk. Alternatively, you can manually collapse the Shield's plates over the course of 1 minute.

item.name: Retaliating Shield of Ink
entries: This Shield has a hidden compartment inside it that can fire thick squid ink at attacking creatures. When a Large or smaller creature within 5 feet of you misses you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to press a button on the Shield's handle, spraying ink from the front of the Shield. The attacker must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition until the start of its next turn.

If the Shield is filled with a Vial of squid ink (worth 20+ GP), the effect uses a save DC of 13. If it's filled with a bottle of common Ink (worth 10 GP), it uses a save DC of 10 instead. The Shield can be used in this way up to three times before its ink needs to be refilled, which takes 1 minute.

This Shield is a mechanical item whose purpose and functionality can be discerned by taking a {@action Study|XPHB} action to make a DC 12 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check. On a success, you understand what the Shield does, how to use it, and how to take care of it. You also understand that filling the Shield with other volatile or sticky liquids (such as acid, poison, grog, or oil) or any solid material (such as sand) will damage the machinery inside and take 8 hours of work to repair.

item.name: Ring of Anchoring
entries: While attuned to this hammered lead ring, you can breathe underwater, and whenever you're pushed, pulled, or otherwise moved against your will, the distance you're moved is reduced by 10 feet.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This ring is cursed. Attuning to it curses you until you are targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic. The curse also ends if the finger that's wearing the ring is severed from your hand. As long as you remain cursed, you can't take off the ring, and the arm that's wearing it becomes unbearably heavy while you're underwater, giving you {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Strength and Dexterity checks until you resurface.

Whenever you become submerged in a liquid, you must make a DC 20 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, your arm becomes immobile for 1 minute; for the duration, you have a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0, and you're dragged 30 feet downward at the start of each of your turns. On a successful save, you can move your arm, albeit sluggishly, for 1 minute; for the duration, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved, and you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls you make with the arm wearing the ring. If you're still underwater at the end of the minute, you must repeat the save. These effects immediately end once you're no longer submerged.

item.name: Ring of Eldritch Ire
entries: This cold, warped ring has three yellow gemstones adorning its surface that swivel and move like strange eyes. The ring has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dusk. While wearing the ring, you can expend 1 charge as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically mark a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you for 1 hour. The mark is lost if you end it early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} or have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition.

A marked creature appears to be shrouded in a strange, sharpening mist that magically helps you focus. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls against marked creatures, and they take a -1 penalty to saving throws against spells you cast. You know a marked creature's general distance and direction from you at all times while it's on the same plane of existence as you. In addition, you perceive a marked creature within 60 feet of you as if you had {@sense Truesight|XPHB}, and any visual illusions between you and the marked creature appear transparent to you.

item.name: Ring of Gestures
entries: This gaudy ring has a delicately carved pink gem in the shape of a hand at its center. While wearing the ring on your hand, you can cast the {@spell Minor Illusion|XPHB} cantrip from it to alter the appearance of that hand, such as to make your hand or any of its fingers appear larger, smaller, or a different color. In addition, whenever you make a gesture with the hand wearing this ring, the pink gemstone shifts to mimic the gesture and remains that way until you make a new one.

item.name: Ring of Roses
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can cause a single rose to appear and grow in your hand. Its stem is 1 foot long and has small, dull thorns. The flower is odorless and blooms in a color of your choice, but it withers and turns to dust after 24 hours.

item.name: Ring of Seething
entries: This dark band is cool to the touch and has several cracks throughout its otherwise smooth body. When you attune to the ring, choose a creature type: Aberration, Celestial, Dragon, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, or Undead. While wearing the ring, its normally cool band glows red hot when a creature of the chosen type is within 60 feet of you. In addition, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions caused by creatures of that type. Though it seemingly radiates heat, the ring feels only faintly warm to you. Small pieces of the metal band hover above the surface of the ring when glowing in this way.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This ring is cursed. Attuning to it curses you until you are targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic. As long as you remain cursed, you can't take off the ring, and you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) and Charisma checks you make that involve a creature of the chosen type. In addition, while you're within 60 feet of one or more creatures of that type, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against creatures of a different type.

item.name: Ring of the Fowl Sentinel
entries: This plain bronze ring is embossed with a rooster's head at its center. You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to tap the ring to the beak of a chicken or rooster that isn't {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} toward you, causing it to have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for 8 hours. Until the condition ends, the target has AC 10, HP 1, a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0, a +3 bonus to Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks, and it remains quiet. The condition ends early if the target sees another Small or larger creature it hasn't seen before. When the condition ends, the target lets out a cry, which can be heard up to 150 feet away. Only one creature can be {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by the ring at a time. If you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to tap the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} target's beak with the ring again, the condition ends and the target doesn't let out a cry.

item.name: Ring of the Sandskimmer
entries: This ring is made entirely of sand that ebbs and flows around your finger. The sand is magically held together by a single, perfectly smooth tiger's eye stone that spins in place as the sand brushes past it. While wearing the ring, you leave no footprints behind when you walk in sand, and you treat {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} made of sand as if it were normal terrain.

item.name: Rocborne Hand Ballista
entries: This massive, beast-hunting crossbow is carried with its two handles from above instead of by being held from below. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The crossbow uses twice the normal amount of ammunition each time you fire it, and it deals an extra {@damage 1d10} Piercing damage on a hit. It emits a roc-like screech that's audible out to 100 feet each time it's fired.

This crossbow is blessed with the spirits of great ancestral hunters. The weapon has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a Huge or smaller creature with a ranged attack roll using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to force the target to make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} drops to 0 until the start of its next turn and it can't make {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} until then.

item.name: Rocborne Robe
entries: This warm canvas robe is lined with roc down. While wearing it, you can tolerate temperatures of 0 degrees Fahrenheit or lower without any additional protection.

In addition, the robe has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the robe, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to expend 1 charge and unfurl it. The robe opens into two large, stylized wings before flapping back down, buffeting the area in front of you and refolding into the robe. The wings create a 15-foot-wide, 10-foot-long {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} of wind in front of you. Each Medium or smaller creature in the area must make a DC 13 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is pushed 10 feet away from you. At the same time, you are flung away, flying 10 feet straight backward without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

item.name: Rocborne Rod
entries: This wooden rod is carved in the likeness of five ancient rocs. Motes of their spirits flow through it and allow you to channel some of their lingering power.

This rod has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the rod, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} while falling, or a {@action Magic|XPHB} action otherwise, and expend 1 charge to cause two handles to extend from the rod's top and bottom. While holding the handles, a pair of magical wings appear above the rod, slowing your rate of descent to 10 feet per round, or 20 feet per round if you weigh more than 200 pounds. While falling in this way, you can move horizontally or downward as if you had a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 40 feet, but you can't move upward without a significant updraft. If you land while falling in this way, you take no damage from the fall. The wings disappear at the end of the fall, or at the end of your next fall if you summoned them as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. You can also dismiss them early (no action required).

In addition, while holding the rod aloft, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and expend 1 charge to summon an instantaneous gust of strong wind. When you do, powerful winds {@item whip|XPHB} around you, blowing things skyward in a 20-foot-radius, 60-foot-high {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on a point within 5 feet of you. Large and smaller creatures in the area must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be launched 30 feet upward before crashing back to the ground. Objects in the area that weigh less than 200 pounds are also launched 30 feet upward by the wind. You can choose to automatically succeed on or fail the save. If you choose to fail it and immediately take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to summon the rod's magical wings, or if they were already summoned, you're launched 60 feet upward instead.

item.name: Sandstorm Dancer
entries: This golden cloak billows illusory sand around you when you walk or cast a spell. The cloak has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the cloak, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 15) from it: {@spell Blur|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Call Lightning|XPHB} (3 charges), {@spell Create or Destroy Water|XPHB} (1 charge), or {@spell Hallucinatory Terrain|XPHB} (4 charges).

item.name: Scorching Cleaver
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This {@item Battleaxe|XPHB} has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. As long as the weapon has at least 1 charge, attacks you make with it deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Fire damage on a hit. When you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll made with this weapon, it regains 3 expended charges.

entries.name: Erupting Slash
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 3 or more charges to slash the air in front you with the {@item Battleaxe|XPHB}. When you do, a 5-foot-wide, 30-foot-long {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} of fire erupts from the blade. Creatures in the {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d6} Fire damage for each expended charge. The DC for the save is equal to 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and the number of charges you expended. If you expend 4+ charges in this way and leave the {@item Battleaxe|XPHB} with 0 remaining charges, the weapon overheats. When it overheats, this effect deals an extra {@damage 3d6} Fire damage, and creatures that succeed on the save take half as much damage from the effect.

item.name: Screaming Longbow
entries: Arrows fired from this sickly {@item Longbow|XPHB} let out a screeching howl that is audible up to 100 feet away as they streak toward their target. On a hit, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d4} Thunder damage. When you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll made with this weapon, the target and each creature within 5 feet of it must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute.

item.name: Sea Serpent Hairpin
entries: This golden, twisting hair pin wraps around a tiny silver sword and gives your fingers and skin a slight grip while underwater. Whenever you use the Grapple option of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} while you're wearing the pin and are either submerged or swimming in water, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on any ability check or saving throw it makes to avoid or end the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition.

item.name: Seafarer's Jacket
entries: This long captain's jacket carries with it an air of authority. Water slides off of it with ease and magically enhances your comfort in the ocean. While wearing the jacket, you gain a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and you have proficiency with {@item Navigator's Tools|XPHB}. If you already had proficiency with {@item Navigator's Tools|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} with them instead.
In addition, you can cast the {@spell Fog Cloud|XPHB} spell (level 2 version) from the jacket. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Seat Belt
entries: While wearing this belt and standing on a solid surface, you can begin to sit down in an open space as if there were a chair beneath you. When you do, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically summon an {@condition Invisible|XPHB}, incorporeal chair, sitting on it instead of crouching or falling to the ground. The chair can be of any style you choose (such as a stool, recliner, throne, or rocking chair) and can support up to 500 pounds. While it is comfortable, it can't be interacted with beyond simply sitting on it. The chair disappears if it is no longer on a solid surface or if you're no longer sitting on it.

item.name: Seed of Rebirth
entries: This fist-sized, magic seed can be attuned to by four or fewer creatures over the course of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. If each creature attuned to the seed participates in gently planting it in fertile soil, the seed immediately begins to flourish, growing into a majestic, flowering tree over the course of 7 days. At the end of that time, the tree is 60 feet tall and 5 feet in diameter at its base, and its canopy extends out in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} around the tree. The tree has a number of large limbs equal to the number of creatures attuned to the seed. Once planted in this way, no other creatures can attune to the seed, and the attuned creatures' attunement doesn't end as a result of being more than 100 feet away from the seed for 24 hours or dying. A creature's attunement to the seed immediately ends if it commits a malicious affront to nature.

When a creature attuned to the seed is dead for at least 1 minute, its soul returns to the tree, and its body crumbles into a rich soil that recreates the "enrichment" effect of the {@spell Plant Growth|XPHB} spell, centered on the body. The soul returns to the tree regardless of the circumstance of the creature's death or how far away it was from the tree when it died (even if it was on another plane of existence). The returned soul is stored in a shelled nut that the tree grows at the time of the creature's death from the large limb associated with that creature.

After {@dice 1d6 + 1} days, the nut falls from the tree and breaks apart, revealing the creature in its new body, as if by the {@spell Reincarnate|XPHB} spell. Once a creature is reincarnated in this way, its attunement to the seed ends.

When a creature's attunement to the seed ends, the tree limb associated with that creature withers and dies. The tree is magical and can't be destroyed by nonmagical means. If the tree is destroyed, all creatures attuned to the seed are no longer attuned to it. Any creature attuned to the seed is magically alerted if the tree is being attacked, if it's destroyed, or if one of its large limbs dies. Once all of the tree's large limbs die, the tree splits apart and disintegrates, releasing another Seed of Rebirth to the astral winds that carry it to a new location on a different plane of existence.

item.name: Septum Ring of the Great Minotaur
entries: This septum barbell is made from tempered gold and is studded with rubies. While wearing the ring as a nose piercing, your Strength score increases by 2, to a maximum of 22, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks. In addition, your carrying capacity is doubled, and you can perfectly recall any path you've traveled within the last 24 hours.

item.name: Serpent Dart
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically coat this {@item Dart|XPHB} with thick, emerald green poison. The poison remains for 1 minute or until an attack using this weapon hits a creature. That creature must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or take an extra {@damage 2d8} Poison damage and have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Servitude Choker
entries: This necklace can animate small nonliving objects and have them fulfill simple tasks for you. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can drape or wrap the necklace around a Small or smaller nonmagical object that weighs less than 50 pounds and isn't attached to or carried by anything else. The adorned object springs to life, sprouting little arms and legs, and becomes a mindless creature under your control until you remove the necklace or it takes damage. The servant has an AC of 10 and a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 20 feet.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can mentally command the servant if it's within 60 feet of you. The servant can perform simple tasks that a Human servant could do, such as fetching things, cleaning, mending, folding clothes, lighting fires, serving food, and pouring wine. The servant can't attack, activate magic items, or carry more than 10 pounds. Once you give it a command, the servant performs the task to the best of its ability until it completes the task, then waits for your next command. If you command the servant to perform a task that would put it in danger or move it more than 60 feet away from you, the servant does nothing.
The necklace can animate an object for up to 8 hours or until it takes damage. When the effect ends, the necklace can't be used again for another {@dice 1d8} hours.

item.name: Set of Numat's Trick Tubes
entries: This drab pair of empty ceramic cylinders are magically linked. Each tube is 3 inches in diameter and 1 foot tall. Placing an object inside one of the tube and closing its lid creates a quiet whoosh sound as the object is magically teleported to the other tube, which makes a similarly quiet thud. A tube can't send more than 2 pounds of material at a time and can't be used to send a creature. Both tube must be on the same plane of existence and have their lids closed in order for an object to be teleported between them.

item.name: Set of Secret Scribe Candles
