entries: This pair of candles are magically linked, and they never seem to run out of wick. They can be used as normal candles, but magically won't light Paper or Parchment on fire.

If you light one of the candles while the other one isn't lit, the first candle begins to burn with a blue flame. By holding a piece of Paper or Parchment over the flame, any nonmagical ink on the page is burnt off, leaving the page unmarked. When you finish using the candle in this way, you can extinguish the flame as normal.

Once the blue flame is extinguished, the linked candle immediately comes alight with a regular orange flame, which smells of lavender and can't be extinguished by nonmagical means. By holding a blank piece of Paper or Parchment over the orange flame, the ink from the first page is magically burnt onto the new one over the course of 1 minute as the candle heats it. Once all the ink has been transcribed onto a new page in this way, the orange flame winks out.

item.name: Shadow Drinker
entries: This wicked {@item Dagger|XPHB} can absorb the shadows cast by your foes. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a Small or larger enemy with a melee attack roll using this {@item Dagger|XPHB}, you can choose to absorb that creature's shadow as part of the attack.

When you do, the creature no longer casts a shadow, and the {@item Dagger|XPHB} becomes shrouded in a dark haze, which extends to surround you while you're holding the {@item Dagger|XPHB}. As long as the haze is surrounding you, you have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition while you're entirely in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}.

The dark haze remains for up to 10 minutes. A creature hit by the shrouded {@item Dagger|XPHB} takes an extra {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage from the attack; this extra damage increases to {@dice 2d6} if the target is the creature missing its shadow. The haze ends early immediately after you make an attack roll, deal damage, or cast a spell, or if you take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to end it. Once the haze ends, the shadow immediately returns to the creature from whom it was taken.
You can only have one shadow absorbed in this way at a time. Once the {@item Dagger|XPHB} steals a creature's shadow, it can't steal that creature's shadow again until the next dusk.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This {@item Dagger|XPHB} is cursed. Attuning to it curses you until you are targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic. As long as you remain cursed, you have {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Radiant damage.

item.name: Shadow Ink
entries: Once penned, this special ink can only be seen and read in total darkness. While in {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, any written or tattooed words, illustrations, or other marks made with the ink glow with a soft, colored light. One bottle of Shadow Ink provides enough ink to fill 50 pages with writing, illustrations, or other such markings.

A {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB} spell reveals only that there is something faintly magical about the area where this ink has been used, but it doesn't reveal the kind of magic or how to interact with it.

item.name: Shadow Ward
entries: This eerily still cloak seems to absorb some of the light that touches it. While wearing the cloak, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks. In addition, the cloak has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dusk. When you're hit by an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to halve the attack's damage against you (round down) and teleport in a plume of black smoke to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of you.

item.name: Shadowshawl
entries: This light gray shawl is made of thick fabric that looks like woven spider webs. While wearing the shawl with its hood up, your face is hidden within a small pocket of magical {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, and you have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet. If you already had {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, its range increases by 60 feet. You can see through the hood's pocket of {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} as if it were nonmagical.
In addition, while the hood is up, light-based effects (such as the {@spell Sunbeam|XPHB} spell) can't cause you to have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition.

item.name: Shadowsmoke Dragon Pipe
entries: This magic pipe leaves behind odorless smoke that dissipates quickly. Its head easily detaches, allowing you to refill it. The pipe has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d3 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 1 charge to blow a small, condensed ring of smoke at yourself or an ally that you can see within 30 feet of you.

When you do, the target can choose to immediately gain the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition (no action required), or it can choose to wear the smoke as if it were a physical ring. A creature can only wear one of these smoke rings at a time, and it lasts for 1 minute or until the creature takes a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically gain the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition. A creature that turns {@condition Invisible|XPHB} in either way has that condition until the start of its next turn or until it makes an attack roll, deals damage, or casts a spell.

item.name: Shaedenstaff
item.reqAttune: by a Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
entries: This staff is made from the reclaimed horn of a huge Undead creature. While you're attuned to the staff, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to resist mental stress effects. The staff can also be wielded as a magic {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}.

The staff has 15 charges for the following properties and regains {@dice 2d6 + 3} expended charges daily at dusk. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the staff disintegrates in a plume of pitch black smoke and emits a terrifying scream audible out to 300 feet.

entries.name: Annihilating Strike
entries: When you hit with a melee attack using this staff, you can expend 1 charge to deal an extra {@damage 1d10} Necrotic damage to the target.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While holding the staff, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC: {@spell Calm Emotions|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Darkness|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Dispel Evil and Good|XPHB} (5 charges), {@spell Inflict Wounds|XPHB} (level 3 version, 3 charges), or {@spell Circle of Death|XPHB} (6 charges).

item.name: Shape-Shifter's Circlet
entries: This ivory circlet allows you to recreate some of the natural features of other creatures. While wearing the circlet, you can spend 1 minute to gently massage your ears, forehead, or temples. When you do, you can choose to create one of the following features or effects:

items: You can cover or replace your own ears with the form of another creature's.
You can cause one or more horns or antlers to grow atop your head.
You can change your eyes, including their color and pupil shape.

entries: You can only have 1 of these effects active at a time. The effect lasts until you dismiss it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, remove the circlet, or have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition. You don't gain any of the senses or abilities of another creature by recreating its physical features in this way.

item.name: Shared Burden
entries: This set of chained rings and bracers is a sign of the pact you forged with your patron. Once you attune to the item, the small spikes that line the inside of the bands become harmless to you and feel like a warm, gentle caress against your skin. You can also use the item as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Warlock spells.

Whenever you deal damage to a creature with a Warlock spell, you can choose to add your Charisma modifier (minimum 1) to the spell's damage. When you do, you take Psychic damage equal to half your Charisma modifier (round up). This Psychic damage ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}.

item.name: Sharkrazor Mantle
entries: While wearing this magic sharkskin cloak, you have a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} of 60 feet and can breathe underwater. While you're wearing the cloak, whenever a creature successfully grapples you or causes to you have the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition and is touching you, it takes {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage. The creature takes this damage again at the start of each of its turns until that condition ends or it is no longer touching you.

In addition, while wearing the cloak, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB} or {@skill Survival|XPHB}) checks made to find or track a creature that's missing any of its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and your attack rolls against targets that have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} as a result of that condition if the target is missing any of its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

item.name: Sheer Cold
entries: The blade of this {@item Battleaxe|XPHB} is made from a shard of the Everglacier and never melts. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals Cold damage on a hit instead of Slashing.

When you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll made with this weapon, the ground in the direction of the target in either a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} or 30-foot-long, 5-foot-wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} (your choice) is covered in a layer of slick ice for 1 minute. The ice is {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. When a creature enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it must succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity saving throw or have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. In addition, the original target of the attack must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or have the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn. The {@condition Restrained|XPHB} target can take an action to make a DC 15 Strength check. On a successful save, that condition ends.

item.name: Shield of Yggdrasil
entries: This massive tower Shield requires a Strength score of 17 or higher in order to adequately wield it. The Shield is of dwarven make and functions as a small battlement, granting you a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} while you wield it. This bonus is in addition to the Shield's normal bonus to AC.

entries.name: Shield Plant
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can plant this Shield on the ground in your space to barricade yourself behind it, granting you {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} against ranged attacks from the direction you're facing. While wielding the planted Shield, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 5 feet and can't increase, and Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage you take from weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} is reduced by 3. You can uproot the Shield to end this effect as a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action.

You can doff the Shield when planting it by taking a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to do so instead of a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. When you do, the Shield remains upright and in place, and it can be used as {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} by any creature standing directly behind it in the space it was left in. The Shield falls down if you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition, or if a creature within 5 feet of it takes a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to knock it over with a successful DC 20 Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check. You can move the Shield while it's planted by donning it again.

This Shield has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. You can expend 1 charge when you plant the Shield, or on a subsequent turn as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, to magically send out metal roots along the ground in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Shield. The area is {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} but it doesn't cost you or your allies extra movement. Moving the Shield causes the roots to shift and move with it. The roots remain until you uproot the Shield or it falls down.

item.name: Shielding Turtle Sugarbombs
entries: These sticky chocolate candies are filled with an enchanted molasses and caramel center and come in packs of two. You can eat one of these sugarbombs as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. When you do, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet for 1 minute. If you have training with Shields and are wearing one on your back, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} while your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced in this way. If you're wielding a Shield, you don't gain this bonus to AC.

entries.name: Upset Stomach
entries: You can only be under the effects of one sugarbomb that has this property at a time. Eating a sugarbomb that has this property while under the effects of another one causes the effects of the first sugarbomb to immediately end and be replaced by the new one's. This rapid change is difficult to stomach, forcing you to make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute.

item.name: Signet Rings of Blindness, Deafness, and Silence
entries: Each of these three signets is decorated with the carving of a skull and a pair of skeletal hands that cover either its eyes (blindness), ears (deafness), or mouth (in an area of silence). Each ring has 1 charge, which it regains daily at dawn. If you are wearing the corresponding signet when you have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} or {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition or are in an area of magical silence, you can expend that ring's charge (no action required) to end that condition or speak a word or phrase (such as a spell's Verbal component) that can be heard through magical silence. Wearing the rings together as a set combines their collective charges, potentially allowing you to use one ring's property multiple times.

item.name: Signet Rings of the Fey Court
item.reqAttune: by an Elf or Fey Creature
entries: This set of wooden rings once belonged to a powerful Fey who ruled over a domain on the Feywild. You must be wearing all four rings in order to attune to them and use their magic. While wearing the rings, you know the {@spell Druidcraft|XPHB} cantrip. In addition, you gain the Elf's Trance trait if you don't already have it.

Each ring has an associated season: autumn, spring, summer, or winter. The four rings each contain 2 charges and regain all expended charges when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Once a ring has no remaining charges, the small landscape adorning the top of it withers until it regains its charges.

entries.name: Season's Resistance
entries: You can expend 1 of a particular ring's charges as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you take damage of the type corresponding to that ring: spring (Radiant), summer (Fire), autumn (Necrotic), or winter (Cold). When you do, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage type until the start of your next turn, including against the triggering damage.

entries.name: Magic Resistance
entries: You can expend any 2 charges from the rings as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when an enemy targets you with a spell or other magical effect. When you do, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects until the start of your next turn, including against the triggering spell or effect. The 2 charges don't need to be from the same ring.

item.name: Silken Socks of Slickening
entries: This pair of magic, smooth socks are iridescent and oily to the touch. While wearing both socks on your feet, you can use them to cast the {@spell Grease|XPHB} spell (save DC 13) centered on a point within 5 feet of you. The magical oil runs off the socks to the ground at your feet, causing the ground to become slick. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Silver Lycan Chime
entries: This small silver bell exudes Divination magic, especially while under a full moon. While hung, the bell rings a unique, magical melody if a Lycanthrope passes within 15 feet of it, or within 60 feet of it during a full moon. The melody sounds quiet but is nevertheless audible out to 100 feet. Different kinds of Lycanthropes cause the bell to emit a different melodies.

item.name: Sirensong Silencer
entries: You can place this golden ear cuff over your ear as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. While wearing the cuff, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition.

Tapping the ear cuff twice (no action required) causes you to have the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition, although you can still faintly hear the sound of the ocean. Doing so again ends this effect on you.

item.name: Sling of the Tiny Giant
entries: This wicked {@item Sling|XPHB} is devastating against foes larger than yourself. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a creature with a ranged attack using the {@item Sling|XPHB}, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d4} Force damage for each size larger than you that it is, to a maximum of {@dice 3d4}.

In addition, when you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll made with this {@item Sling|XPHB}, the target must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 10 feet away from you and have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. If the target is Large or larger, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the save.

item.name: Smash Potatoes
entries: This hearty root vegetable is grown by the Clerics and champions of a god of war and combat, whose strength is said to make its way to the Material Plane through their gardens. If you eat one of these vaguely fist-shaped potatoes, your hands itch for the thrill of combat for 1 hour. For the duration, you can roll {@dice 1d4} in place of the normal damage of your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}, provided you make the attack with one of your hands. Each additional Smash Potato you eat extends the duration by 1 hour. You can eat up to three of these potatoes before needing to finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} to benefit from them again.

item.name: Snuff
entries: This dark metal {@item Sickle|XPHB} was forged with an unusually harsh angle and has a unique power over light and shadow. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage on a hit.

While holding the weapon, you can use a command word as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to extinguish nonmagical flames within 60 feet of you. Any area of {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} within 60 feet of you that was created by a spell is also dispelled as if it were targeted by {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} (+4 spellcasting ability modifier). Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dusk.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can draw your hand over the {@item Sickle|XPHB}'s handle to extend it downward, turning the {@item Sickle|XPHB} into the form of a scythe wreathed in shadow. While in this form, the weapon has the Two-Handed and Reach properties, and the extra Psychic damage it deals on a hit increases to {@dice 2d6}.
As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding the scythe, you can magically return it to its {@item Sickle|XPHB} form.

item.name: Socks of the Well-Rested
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} while wearing these comfortable enchanted socks, you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d8} + your Constitution modifier. You must be wearing both socks on your feet in order to gain this benefit.

item.name: Spellwoven Robe
entries: This robe is spun from special, magic-sensitive threads, causing arcane glyphs to appear on it whenever you cast a spell. While wearing the robe and not wearing armor, your base {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} is 10 plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

In addition, whenever you cast a level 1+ spell while wearing the robe, you can change its apparent style, color, and material. Regardless of its appearance, it is always a robe and its weight remains the same.

item.name: Spiderbite Daggers
entries: This pair of Daggers is made using the harvested fangs of a large, venomous spider. When you hit the same creature on your turn with both Daggers, that creature must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or take an extra {@damage 2d4} Poison damage and have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Spire Seed
entries: This large tree seed is 1 foot long and is surrounded by a protective cone. If you're attuned to the seed and plant it and its cone in soil, it immediately takes root and begins to grow rapidly, becoming fully grown after 30 days. The stem of the seed's cone is vaguely key-shaped, and it breaks off in your hand when planted. While you're attuned to the seed, the stem magically remains on your person at all times.

Once planted, no other creature can attune to the seed. Your attunement to the seed doesn't end as a result of being more than 100 feet away from it for at least 24 hours, but rather only after being on a different plane of existence than it for at least 24 hours. It also doesn't end as a result of you dying, but rather only after you've been dead for more than 1 minute. If your attunement ends after the seed has begun to grow but before it becomes fully grown, the tree dies and the seed is destroyed.

Once fully grown, the spire tree is 300 feet tall and 40 feet in diameter at its base, which tapers gently inward toward the top of the tree. Its canopy extends out in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} around the tree. Soil beneath the tree's canopy is exceptionally rich and fertile.

At the base of this magic tree is a door large enough for Medium creatures to enter. The door is guarded by a Dryad who is loyal to you and allies you designate. The dryad accepts the seed's stem as proof of ownership and uses it as a key to open the door. If your attunement to the seed ends before the dryad first opens the door, the stem disintegrates and the door can't be opened. Once opened, your attunement to the seed ends, and the tree belongs to you. You and your designated allies can freely enter and leave the tree.

Inside the tree are ten floors. You choose how to distribute the floors throughout the tree. A wooden spiral staircase climbs upward through the center of the tree and its floors. Each floor has ten small windows that can't be seen from outside the tree. In addition, the interior of the tree can't be targeted by Divination spells or perceived through magical scrying sensors, and it can't be entered using teleportation or interplanar travel.

The tree has AC 20; HP 500; {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison and Psychic damage as well as Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage except that which is dealt by siege equipment, magic items, spells, or other magical effects; and {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all other damage. The door has AC 15, HP 100, and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison and Psychic damage. The tree regains {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} daily at dawn. If a part of the tree, such as the door, is destroyed, that part regrows after 7 days.

If the tree is attacked, it immediately creates a Treant at its base to defend it. The treant isn't under your control and acts in accordance with its normal disposition and alignment, doing its best to defend the tree. The treant disappears at dawn or when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Once the tree creates a treant in this way, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

Neither the treant nor the dryad can move farther than 1 mile away from the tree. If the dryad drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it vanishes, reappearing at the base of the tree at the next dawn. If you or one of your allies kills the dryad, it doesn't reappear, and the tree becomes nonmagical. The tree is still hollow inside with the floors you'd chosen, but its windows and all its magical properties are lost.
The tree creates another Spire Seed once every 100 years.

item.name: Spirit Pike
entries: If you're not proficient with Pikes, you are nevertheless proficient with this weapon while you're attuned to it. When you cast the {@spell Spiritual Weapon|XPHB} spell while holding the {@item Pike|XPHB}, you can cause it to dissolve into the air to make the spell's spectral weapon take the form of the Spirit {@item Pike|XPHB}. On a hit, with the spectral weapon, the target takes Force damage equal to {@dice 1d10} plus your spellcasting ability modifier instead of the spell's normal damage. If you cast {@spell Spiritual Weapon|XPHB} using a higher-level spell slot, the spell's damage still increases by {@dice 1d8} for every slot level above 2.
Your hands are left free when you dissolve the {@item Pike|XPHB} in this way. When the spell ends, the {@item Pike|XPHB} reappears in your hand or on the ground at your feet if your hands are full.

In addition, the weapon has 4 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a target with an attack roll using the {@item Pike|XPHB} as either a physical or spectral weapon, you can expend 1 charge to deal an extra {@damage 1d8} Radiant damage to the target.

item.name: Splintershot
entries: This {@item Shortbow|XPHB} can cause an Arrow to shatter and unleash a devastating spray of magical shrapnel. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can use a command word to destroy one nonmagical Arrow you have nocked in the bow and send splintering shards of arcane energy out in front of you. When you do, each creature in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage, or {@damage 3d6} Piercing damage if the creature was within 5 feet of you.

item.name: Splitter
entries: This {@item Halberd|XPHB}'s haft is made of rough, dark wood and is miraculously easy to heft. The bladed end of the {@item Halberd|XPHB} looks like a hatchet that's been absorbed by the hewn wood. While holding the {@item Halberd|XPHB} with two hands, you can use a command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically split the weapon into its more basic components.

When you do, the {@item Halberd|XPHB} deconstructs in your hands, leaving you with a {@item Handaxe|XPHB} in one hand and {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} in the other. Both the {@item Handaxe|XPHB} and {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} have the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property. You can combine the two weapons to reform the {@item Halberd|XPHB} by repeating the command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while you're holding both the {@item Handaxe|XPHB} and the {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. If the weapon is split into the {@item Handaxe|XPHB} and {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}, the bonus is a +1 with each weapon instead.

entries.name: Halberd
entries: If you hit a creature with the {@item Halberd|XPHB} and roll a 10 on the damage die, that creature's energy is sapped. Its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn, and your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet for the same duration.

entries.name: Handaxe and Quarterstaff
entries: If you're wielding the {@item Handaxe|XPHB} and {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} in different hands, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}, and when you make an extra attack as a result of using these weapons' Light property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack if you aren't already adding it to the damage. The {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} deals Necrotic damage on a hit, and it deals maximum damage to plants and Plant creatures. Immediately after you make a ranged attack with the {@item Handaxe|XPHB}, it flies back to your hand.

item.name: Squasher
entries: This enormous squash is strangely durable and makes for a functional magic weapon. Druids wielding this weapon can use their Wisdom modifier instead of Strength for the attack and damage rolls of melee attacks using it.

item.name: Staff of Favorable Winds
item.reqAttune: by a Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
entries: While holding this staff, {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} caused by wind doesn't cost you extra movement. The staff has 10 charges. While holding it, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC: {@spell Cone of Cold|XPHB} (5 charges), {@spell Feather Fall|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Gust of Wind|XPHB} (2 charges), or {@spell Wind Wall|XPHB} (3 charges).

In addition, while holding the staff, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to create a harmless sensory effect using the air around you. For example, you can cause leaves to rustle, slam shutters closed in a sudden gust, or cause your clothing to billow dramatically.
The staff regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the staff turns to dust and is blown away by a sudden flurry of wind.

item.name: Staff of the Magpie
entries: This magic stone staff weighs 10 pounds and has the bust of a magpie at its top. While holding the staff, you gain a +1 bonus to Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks you make to search for treasure or a specific item you're looking for.

The staff has 10 charges. While holding the staff, you can expend some charges to cast either Fly (3 charges) or Locate {@variantrule Object|XPHB} (2 charges) from it. The staff regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the staff becomes a nonmagical {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} as the magpie springs to life and flies away.

item.name: Staff of the Mirage
entries: This staff has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the staff vanishes and is lost.

entries.name: Spells
entries: You can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC; you gain a +2 bonus to your spell save DC when casting a spell in this way: {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Hallucinatory Terrain|XPHB} (4 charges), {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Mirror Image|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Mislead|XPHB} (5 charges), or {@spell Silent Image|XPHB} (1 charge).

item.name: Staff of the Mustang
entries: This dark {@item wooden staff|XPHB} is carved in the shape of a majestic stallion. The staff has 10 charges. While holding the staff, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Phantom Steed|XPHB} spell from it. In addition, you can use a command word while holding the staff and expend 1 charge to take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

The staff regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the staff breaks in two as if bitten and is destroyed. When this happens, the sound of hooves can be heard galloping away in an indiscernible direction.

item.name: Staff of the Reverent Warrior
entries: This staff was made as a means of worship rather than war by a retired warrior using the weapons he'd once wielded. The staff is adorned with blades and channels the inner focus of a warrior. This staff can be wielded as a magic {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} that grants a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with it. Even if you aren't attuned to the staff, attacks with it can deal either Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage on a hit (your choice each time).

This staff has 10 charges. While attuned to the staff, you know the {@spell True Strike|XPHB} cantrip. When you cast this spell while holding the staff, you can expend 1 or more charges to enhance the spell in one or more of the following ways:
You can expend 2 charges to cast the spell as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
You can expend 1 charge to cause the next attack roll made against the target before the end of your next turn to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
On a hit, you can expend 1 charge to cause the attack to deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Radiant damage.

The staff regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the staff becomes a nonmagical {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}, but attacks with it can still deal either Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage.

item.name: Stalaga Spear
entries: This magic {@item Spear|XPHB} is made of dark, polished granite and has a large ruby set in its head. Though it is made of stone, it weighs no more than a normal {@item Spear|XPHB} and is enchanted to prevent it from shattering. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

The {@item Spear|XPHB} has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn. While you're within 5 feet of a surface made of unworked stone or earth, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and expend 1 charge to plunge the {@item Spear|XPHB} into that surface. The {@item Spear|XPHB} vanishes into the stone or earth and emerges as a sharp stone spike, 1 foot wide and 10 feet long, from another stone or earthen surface that you can see within 60 feet of you, such as the ground or a cave wall. The spike erupts perpendicularly from that surface and forces any creature in a 5-foot-wide, 10-foot-long {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} in that direction to make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 4d6} Piercing damage and is pushed up to 5 feet away from the spike. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.

While you're within 60 feet of the stone spike and within 5 feet of a surface made of unworked stone or earth, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and use a command word to magically recall the {@item Spear|XPHB}. The stone spike crumbles, and the {@item Spear|XPHB} inside vanishes back into the surface it erupted from before reemerging from the surface next to you into your open hand. If you don't have an open hand, it falls to the ground at your feet.

item.name: Starbreaker
entries: This glowing {@item Morningstar|XPHB} sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet while you hold it. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Radiant damage on a hit. In addition, you can cast the {@spell Daylight|XPHB} spell using the weapon. Once the weapon has been used to cast this spell, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

item.name: Starmetal Ring
entries: This roughly crafted metal ring seems to have an otherworldly pull on other metallic items. While touching an object with the hand that bears this ring, you can spend 1 hour forging a magical bond between the ring and the object, provided the object weighs no more than 25 pounds and is at least 50 percent metal. Only one object can be bound to the ring in this way at a time.

While wearing the ring, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn to magically pull its bound object up to 20 feet toward you, provided the object is within 40 feet of you and isn't behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}. The pulled object bounces harmlessly off of creatures and other objects in its path. If you pull the object into your space, you can catch it in your free hand if you have one. Otherwise, it falls to the ground at your feet.

item.name: Starmetal Shield
entries: This open metal Shield hovers above its paired gauntlet, protecting you but leaving your hand free. While wearing the gauntlet, you are considered to be wielding the Shield, but you have a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} instead of a Shield's normal bonus to AC. Whenever you're targeted by an attack roll made with a metal weapon or projectile, this bonus becomes +3 instead.

entries.name: Shooting Star
entries: While wearing the gauntlet, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to throw the Shield, bouncing it between clustered targets before it returns to your arm. Choose up to three creatures, objects, or points. The first target must be within 30 feet of you, and any additional targets the Shield bounces toward must be within 10 feet of the target before it. You can't select the same target more than once each time you throw the Shield.

Make a ranged attack roll against each target. The targets gain no benefit from {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} or {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover} for the attack. You are proficient with these attacks and add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) to their attack rolls and damage rolls. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 2d8} Slashing damage. The Shield flies back to the gauntlet after reaching its final target. It bounces harmlessly off of any other creatures or objects in its path.

item.name: Starmetal Solar Axe
entries: This magic weapon is forged using iron from the stars. While you're attuned to this weapon, you can call upon its latent stellar energy to catalyze change within the metal, creating endothermic and exothermic reactions. The weapon can be fissioned into two separated Battleaxes or fused back into a {@item Greataxe|XPHB}. It has the following properties based on its current state.

entries.name: Fused State (Greataxe)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra {@damage 2d6} Fire damage on a hit. While holding the axe, flames erupt from its edges and a small star burns in its center. These flames shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. The light is sunlight.

entries.name: Fissioned State (Battleaxes)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with each of these magic weapons, which deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Cold damage on a hit. The axes orbit you slowly while they aren't being held or stowed. You can equip an orbiting {@item Battleaxe|XPHB} as if it were sheathed. In addition, if you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make one melee attack with an orbiting {@item Battleaxe|XPHB} against a target you can see within 20 feet of you.

entries.name: State Change
entries: This weapon can change between its two states using the following properties:

entries.name: Fission
entries: While holding the fused {@item Greataxe|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and use a command word to magically split it apart into its fissioned state. When you do, electrical energy is released, forcing other creatures within 30 feet of you to make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes {@damage 3d6} Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. This property can't be used again for 12 hours.

entries.name: Fusion
entries: While holding both fissioned Battleaxes, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and use another command word to magically recombine the weapon into its fused state. When you do, the sudden generation of heat creates a wave of fire around you, forcing other creatures within 30 feet of you to make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes {@damage 3d6} Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. This property can't be used again for 12 hours.

item.name: Starmetal Striker
entries: This {@item Light Hammer|XPHB} was forged using the remains of a comet and is cold to the touch. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Cold damage on a hit. The hammer leaves behind a crackling mist when thrown, and it flies back to your hand immediately after making a ranged attack with it.

item.name: Steps of the Trickster
entries: These shoes allow you to freely change the sound of your steps while wearing them. Changing the sound of your steps doesn't change their volume but can make them sound as though the shoes are made of a different material. For example, your steps can sound like a beggar's bare feet, a dancer's sharp heels, or an approaching guard's metal boots.

item.name: Storm Sickle
entries: This {@item Sickle|XPHB} sparks with electricity as beads of water form and drip from its blade. The weapon can be used to make special ranged attacks by slashing it through the air to send out blades of storming wind.

You're proficient with these attacks, which have a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet, use your Strength modifier for their attack rolls and damage rolls, and deal {@damage 1d4} Slashing damage on a hit.
Attacks with this magic {@item Sickle|XPHB} deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Lightning damage on a hit, regardless of whether you attack with the weapon itself or use its special ranged attack.

item.name: Stormstruck Staff
entries: This wooden {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} has been scarred by lightning, and an amplifying crystal is embedded at its head. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra 1 Lightning damage on a hit.

Whenever the {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} takes Lightning damage or you take Lightning damage while holding it (from a source other than the {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} itself), the crystal glows brilliantly and pulses with electrical energy for 1 minute. It also glows in this way while under the effect of the {@spell Shillelagh|XPHB} spell. While the crystal is glowing in this way, it emits {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet, and the extra Lightning damage the {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} deals on a hit increases to {@dice 1d6}.

item.name: Storm Thrower Harpoon
entries: You have a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you make a ranged attack with the {@item Trident|XPHB}, it deals an extra {@damage 1d8} Lightning damage on a hit. Immediately after hitting or missing, you can use the chain attached to the weapon's handle to pull it back to your hand as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

In addition, this {@item Trident|XPHB} acts as a conduit for the sky's ferocious power. Immediately after hitting a creature with the {@item Trident|XPHB}, you can call down a bolt of lightning upon the target, provided it's unobstructed from the sky. The target and each creature within 5 feet of it must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 6d10} Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that is submerged in water or wearing metal armor has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the save, and you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the Lightning damage dealt by this effect. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Impale
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can try to impale and pull a creature toward you. If the target is no more than one size larger than you, make a ranged attack against it using the {@item Trident|XPHB}. On a hit, the target must succeed on a DC 17 Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 20 feet toward you.

item.name: Sundersludge Net
entries: This gelatinous {@item Net|PHB} is made with the enchanted remains of a gray ooze. A creature takes {@damage 1d6} Acid damage when it gains the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition from the {@item Net|PHB}, and it takes that damage again at the end of each of its turns until that condition ends. If the creature is wearing nonmagical metal armor, its armor is partly corroded and takes a cumulative -1 penalty to the AC it offers whenever it takes Acid damage at the end of one of its turns. The armor is destroyed if the penalty reduces its AC to 10. The penalty can be removed by casting the {@spell Mending|XPHB} spell on the armor. A creature other than you who touches the {@item Net|PHB} takes {@damage 1d6} Acid damage.

The {@item Net|PHB}'s sticky composition makes it more difficult to escape from than a mundane one. To escape, the target or a creature within 5 feet of it must take an action to make a DC 13 Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check, freeing the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature on a success. Destroying the {@item Net|PHB} (AC 10; 5 HP; {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Poison, and Psychic damage; {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all other damage) also frees the target, ending the effect and tearing the {@item Net|PHB} apart.

The torn {@item Net|PHB} is harmless. If more than half of the {@item Net|PHB} can be salvaged and stored within a container large enough to hold 5 gallons of liquid for 12 hours, the {@item Net|PHB} is repaired and can be used once more. Your attunement to the {@item Net|PHB} ends if the {@item Net|PHB} remains torn for longer than 48 hours.

You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to damage from the {@item Net|PHB} while you're attuned to it. Picking up the {@item Net|PHB} after throwing it renders it harmless until the next time you throw it. The {@item Net|PHB} deals no Acid damage if there isn't a creature attuned to it.

item.name: Switch
entries: This simple, springy rod is made of twisting vines and twigs. While holding the rod, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape feature as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to activate it, magically transforming it into a weapon. It can take the form of any kind of Simple or Martial weapon of your choice that deals Bludgeoning or Piercing damage and lacks the Loading and Reach properties. If you transform the rod into a weapon with the {@itemProperty AF|XDMG|Ammunition} property, it magically creates its own ammunition when you make a ranged attack with it.

Regardless of the weapon's form, you're proficient with it and can use your Wisdom modifier for its attack and damage rolls instead of Strength or Dexterity. On a hit, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d8} damage of its normal type.

While holding the weapon, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to transform it into another qualifying weapon or temporarily back into its rod form until you transform it into a weapon again. Alternatively, you can expend a second use of your Wild Shape feature as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to extend the bark of the activated rod or weapon to your body, covering yourself in treelike armor. When you do, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to four times your Druid level, and until the rod deactivates, you have an {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} of 17 if your AC is lower than that.

The rod deactivates after a number of hours equal to half your Druid level (round down), immediately reverting to its rod form if it's still a weapon. It deactivates early if you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or choose to deactivate it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

item.name: Sword of Resonance
entries: This peculiar brass {@item Longsword|XPHB} is hollow and has {@item lute|XPHB} strings that run along its blade, allowing you to use it as a {@item Musical Instrument|XPHB} and {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB}. You are proficient with the Sword of Resonance as both an instrument and {@item Longsword|XPHB} while you're attuned to it. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which has the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} property.

In addition, the sword's hollow metal blade magically amplifies the music made when using it to inspire allies. Whenever you expend a use of your Bardic Inspiration while holding the sword, it begins to reverberate with thunderous energy. The next time you hit a target with an attack using the sword before the end of your next turn, that target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} Thunder damage from the attack.

item.name: Sword of the Spelldrinker
entries: This sword seems to absorb any light that touches it, making it appear darker than the deepest midnight. You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While holding the sword, you treat any magical light or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} within 30 feet of you as if it were nonmagical, and you can breathe normally in any environment.

In addition, this sword has 2 charges and regains {@dice 1d2} expended charges daily at dusk. While holding the sword, you can expend 1 charge to cast {@spell Counterspell|XPHB} from it. When cast in this way, the target automatically fails its saving throw if the triggering spell is level 5 or lower, and it automatically succeeds if the spell is level 6 or higher. If you successfully interrupt a spell in this way, you can choose to absorb it into the sword instead of allowing it to dissipate. The absorbed spell must have a casting time of an action or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. The sword can have only one absorbed spell at a time.

While holding the sword, you can release its absorbed spell. Doing so requires you to take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action or {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} (as determined by the spell's normal casting time), and it uses the slot level, spell save DC, spell attack bonus, and spellcasting ability of the original caster but is otherwise treated as if you cast the spell.
The interrupted spell remains absorbed by the sword for 8 hours or until it's released. Absorbing a new spell replaces the one stored within the sword.

item.name: Talyard the Great's Wand of Power
entries: This normal-looking spruce stick is actually the wand of a famous drunkard. A drunken creature that sees the stick perceives it as a magnificent, colorful wand that radiates with an awesome power.

This wand has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dusk. While you're {@condition drunk|HelianasGuidetoMonsterHunting} and are holding the wand, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (+3 spellcasting ability modifier) from it: {@spell Heroism|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Lesser Restoration|XPHB} (2 charges), or {@spell Thaumaturgy|XPHB} (0 charges).

item.name: Tear of Gaia
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can crush this small, green gem underfoot to activate its magic. When you do, plants in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you instantaneously become healthy and full. Fruit and vegetable-bearing plants in the area grow their produce, which become ripe over the course of 1 hour. If there are no plants within range or you crush the gem against an inorganic material, fertile topsoil 1 inch deep spreads across the area. Healthy green grass immediately grows from the new soil.

item.name: Telescoping Bladelance
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn to extend or retract this mechanical weapon by twisting its handle. The weapon is a normal {@item Lance|XPHB} while it's extended. If the {@item Lance|XPHB} is retracted, the end of the weapon rests inside the wider, bladed base, and it is treated as a {@item Greatsword|XPHB} instead. This {@item Greatsword|XPHB} deals {@damage 1d10} Slashing damage on a hit instead of {@dice 2d6}.

While using the weapon as a {@item Greatsword|XPHB}, you can choose to extend it as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} immediately after hitting a target with an attack. If you do so and the target is a Large or smaller creature, it's pushed 5 feet away from you.

item.name: Tempest Griffon Feather Cape
entries: While wearing this cape, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws against spells and other effects that deal Lightning damage.

While wearing the cape, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and use a command word to transform into a bolt of lightning. When you do, choose an unoccupied space that you can see within 120 feet of you. You immediately travel in your lightning form to the chosen space without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}, filling a 5-foot-wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} in your path. Other creatures in the {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} or within 5 feet of either your old or new location must make a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d10} Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Tethervine Quiver
entries: This seemingly overgrown Quiver keeps a large plant magically concealed inside it, and it can hold up to 20 Arrows or Bolts. When you draw an Arrow or Bolt from the Quiver, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and use a command word to magically connect the end of the ammunition to the plant inside the Quiver. The plant is a ropelike vine that flies effortless behind its connected piece of ammunition when fired. It becomes taut and climbable if the ammunition hits an object or surface, able to hold up to 250 pounds before bursting.

While wearing the Quiver, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically retract the vine and its connected ammunition up to 60 feet back into the Quiver. If the connected ammunition hit an object that weighs less than 25 pounds, that object is also pulled back toward the Quiver when the vine is retracted in this way.

If you hit a creature with a piece of ammunition that was connected to the vine, the vine magically disconnects itself from the ammunition and falls. You can disconnect the vine from its connected ammunition by taking a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to repeat the command word or sever the vine from the Quiver.

The vine grows up to 120 feet long, and a connected piece of ammunition can't be fired longer than the vine's current length. If any portion of the vine is severed, it regrows up to 30 feet of length daily at dawn. A severed portion of vine becomes a mundane length of Rope.

item.name: The Rose Basket
entries: This silver {@item Rapier|XPHB} has a rose adorning its crossguard and pommel and has thorns lining its hilt. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. If you make an attack with the weapon against a Fey or a creature that is shape-shifted, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d8} Radiant damage on a hit.

entries.name: Field of Roses
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can use a command word and touch the {@item Rapier|XPHB}'s point to the ground at your feet. When you do, silver rosebushes sprout from the ground in a 25-foot square centered on your location. The area becomes {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} until the rosebushes disappear. When a creature moves into or within the area, it takes {@damage 2d4} Piercing damage for every 5 feet it travels. While holding the {@item Rapier|XPHB}, this {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} doesn't cost you extra movement, and you don't take damage from moving into or within the area. The rosebushes disappear after 1 minute or when you dismiss them as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Thunderous Flail
entries: This {@item Flail|XPHB} has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. As long as the {@item Flail|XPHB} has at least 1 charge, you deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Lightning damage to the first target you hit with it on each of your turns.

While holding the weapon, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Thunderwave|XPHB} spell (save DC 15) from the ball of the {@item Flail|XPHB}. Alternatively, if you make a melee attack using the {@item Flail|XPHB}, you can immediately expend 1 charge to cast {@spell Thunderwave|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} in the direction of the attack's target.

The ball of the {@item Flail|XPHB} is attached to its chain with a powerful magnetic connection that can be released at will. The {@item Flail|XPHB} can be used to make special ranged attacks by releasing the ball mid-swing. You're proficient with these attacks, which have a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet, use your Strength modifier for their attack rolls and damage rolls, and deal {@damage 1d8} Bludgeoning damage on a hit. While the ball is within 60 feet of you, you can magically pull it up to 20 feet toward you as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. It bounces harmlessly off of creatures and other objects in its path. The ball magically reattaches itself to the chain when it enters your space.

While the ball is detached, you can still use the {@item Flail|XPHB} to cast {@spell Thunderwave|XPHB}. When you do, the spell originates from the ball, affecting creatures in a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on the ball instead of the spell having its normal area of effect.

item.name: Thwackstaff
entries: This festive {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} is decorated with ribbons and other glittering elements. It is perfectly balanced and seems to be made to perform with. This magic {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} has the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} property. In addition, you have a +1 bonus to Charisma ({@skill Performance|XPHB}) checks you make in order to put on an entertaining performance using the {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}.

entries.name: Punishing Parry
entries: This {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}, you can expend 1 charge as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when a creature you can see hits you with a melee attack. When you do, roll {@dice 1d6}. You gain a bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equal to the number rolled against that attack, potentially turning the hit into a miss. If the attack misses you as a result of this bonus to AC, you can immediately make one attack with the {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} against the attacking creature as part of the same {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. On a hit, the attack deals extra damage equal to the number rolled on the {@dice d6}.

item.name: Timber
entries: This magical axe is imbued with a destructive force that builds with each consecutive strike. You have a bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon equal to the number of charges it currently has. The {@item Handaxe|XPHB} gains 1 charge each time you hit a target with it, up to a maximum of 3 charges. When Timber gains its third charge, the built-up energy immediately explodes; it loses all charges, and the target takes an extra {@damage 3d6} Force damage, or {@damage 6d6} Force damage if it's made of wood. When this happens, if the target is no more than one size larger than you, you can choose to push it up to 10 feet away from you.
Timber's charges last for up to 1 minute. It loses all charges if you miss with an attack using it, make an attack against a different target, or make an attack using a different weapon.

item.name: Timepiercer
entries: This item appears to be a sword hilt that has a small hole on either end of it. The hilt is made of clear quartz and contains an hourglass with sand inside it. Despite the holes on the hilt, the sand never seems to flow out of the hourglass. You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and use a command word to cause it to magically form a thin blade of sand. The hourglass spills sand from the bottom of the hilt that circulates up and around to form the blade and guard of a {@item Rapier|XPHB} before returning to the hourglass again through the hole at the top. You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and repeat the command word to cause all the sand to return to the hourglass. While the {@item Rapier|XPHB} is formed, you gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

While holding the hilt, you can cast either the Haste or {@spell Slow|XPHB} spell (save DC 16) from it. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws you make to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} on a spell cast in this way. While {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on such a spell, the sands in the hourglass move quickly if you cast {@spell Haste|XPHB} or more slowly if you cast {@spell Slow|XPHB}. Once the hilt has been used to cast one of these spells, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn.

item.name: Timeshifter's Mantle
entries: If a Large or smaller creature hits you with a melee attack while you're wearing this cloak, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cause time to briefly warp around you. After taking the damage from the attack, you can immediately move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}, and the attacker's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Toadstone
entries: This small jade carving of a frog dangles from a simple cord of leather to be worn as a necklace. While you wear it, you gain a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can hold your breath for up to 1 hour. In addition, you can cast the {@spell Jump|XPHB} spell using the necklace, targeting only yourself. Once the necklace has been used to cast this spell, it can't do so again until the next dusk.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This necklace is cursed. Attuning to it curses you until you're targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic. Removing the necklace fails to end the curse on you. While you're cursed, your skin is perpetually slightly wet and clammy, and you have only an appetite for insects and other grubs instead of the foods you would normally enjoy eating.

item.name: Tome of Dreams
entries: This beautifully embossed book is full of gripping short stories. You can read a story from the book in 10 minutes. The underlying nature of each story is unclear, leaving your subconscious mind to slowly process and unpack its deeper meanings over the course of your next {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

The next time you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} after reading a story from the book, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a roll of 10 or lower, you take {@damage 2d4} Psychic damage as your mind is plagued with intense terrors from the story you read. On a roll of 11 or higher, you gain {@dice 2d4} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} as your mind is filled with joy and courage instead. If you start a story but don't finish reading it, nothing happens the next time you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Torchbearer's Reach
entries: When held, a magical flame appears at the top of this weapon's handle. The flame sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. You can snap the handle down as if cracking a {@item whip|XPHB} (no action required) to cause the flame to extend into a 10-foot-long coil of fire, which you can use as a {@item Whip|XPHB}. This weapon has the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property and deals Fire damage on a hit instead of Slashing. The flame doesn't hurt you while you hold the {@item Whip|XPHB}. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can snuff the fire out with your hand or blow it out to extinguish it.
The {@item Whip|XPHB}'s flame is intangible and can't be used to bind a creature or retrieve objects from afar, but can be used to light other fires.

item.name: Torrent Taiaha
entries: A mote of swirling, magic water circles the top of this {@item wooden staff|XPHB}, which can be wielded as a magic {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}. The staff can also be used to make special melee attacks by extending and lashing out with the water. You're proficient with these attacks, which have a reach of 10 feet, can use your Wisdom modifier for their attack rolls and damage rolls instead of Strength, and deal {@damage 1d4} Bludgeoning damage on a hit. After the attack, the water shrinks back to the staff. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, regardless of whether you attack with the weapon itself or use its special melee attack.

While holding the staff, you can stand on and move across any liquid surface as if it were solid ground. If you end your turn on a liquid in this way, you sink into it as normal until you stand on solid ground again. In addition, while holding the staff, you can cast the {@spell Control Water|XPHB} spell ("flood" or "part water" only) from it. The spell ends early if you are no longer holding the staff. Once the staff is used to cast this spell, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

item.name: Trident of the Dryad
entries: This magical wooden {@item Trident|XPHB} has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d3 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. While you're holding the {@item Trident|XPHB} and are within 5 feet of a Large or bigger tree, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to magically teleport to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of a second Large or bigger tree that is within 60 feet of the previous tree.

entries.name: Tree Form
entries: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to plant the bottom of this {@item Trident|XPHB} in fertile ground and use a command word to transform it into a healthy tree. The tree is 60 feet tall and has a 5-foot-diameter trunk, and its canopy extends out in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} around the tree. While you're within 5 feet of the tree, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to magically teleport to another point on its trunk or within its branches.

The tree appears ordinary but radiates a faint aura of Transmutation magic that can be discerned with the {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB} spell. While touching the tree, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and repeat the command word to return the {@item Trident|XPHB} to its normal form. Any creature in the tree falls when the tree reverts to a {@item Trident|XPHB}. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Trident of the Hydra
entries: This oddly shaped {@item Trident|XPHB} is made of hammered mithral and has a strangely short handle, but it is well-balanced for single-handed combat. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which lacks the {@itemProperty V|XPHB|Versatile} property.

When you make an attack by throwing the {@item Trident|XPHB}, the forked end of the weapon magically splits apart to form three separate spears, allowing you to make three ranged attack rolls with it instead of one. These spears each deal {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage on a hit instead of the {@item Trident|XPHB}'s normal damage; you don't add your ability modifier to their damage unless that modifier is negative. After the attack, the {@item Trident|XPHB} regrows its missing prongs from one of the separated spears, and the other two spears dissolve.

item.name: Uorik the Conqueror's Juice Cup
entries: This brass cup changes the flavor of any water it's holding. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can whisper the name of a fruit into the cup. If the cup is filled with water, the water's color and flavors magically change to resemble that kind of fruit juice. Saying the name of another fruit changes the water's color and flavors again. Regardless of its color and flavors, the water is still only water. Shouting into the cup causes the juice to effervesce and bubble. If the water is {@condition poisoned}, the drink tastes foul and badly fermented.

item.name: Vancian Helm
entries: This helmet is made from the reclaimed and partially disenchanted remains of a shield guardian, no longer bound to an amulet. You gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} while you wear the helm.

entries.name: Spell Storing
entries: Any creature can cast a spell of level 1 through 4 into the helm by touching the helm as the spell is cast. The spell has no effect other than to be stored in the helm. The helm can only hold one spell at a time.

While wearing this helm, you can cast the spell stored in it. The spell uses the slot level, spell save DC, spell attack bonus, and spellcasting ability of the original caster but is otherwise treated as if you cast the spell. The stored spell is lost when it is cast from the helm or a new spell is stored.

If you are wearing the helm when a level 5+ spell is cast into it, the spell is lost with no effect, the spell slot used to cast it (if any) is expended, and you take {@damage 2d6} Psychic damage. Your eyes glow with a brilliant color of your choice when you wear the helm while a spell is stored within it.

item.name: Vigilus
entries: This {@item Hand Crossbow|XPHB} has a small spring-loaded case attached to its side, which can hold six Bolts and automatically loads the next Bolt into the crossbow whenever it's fired. While the case has Bolts in it, you ignore the crossbow's Loading property. A creature can take a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to reload the case with more Bolts.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can unfold the crossbow's three collapsible legs and plant it firmly on the ground in your space to become a Tiny defensive turret. While you're within 30 feet of the crossbow, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically command it to attack a target you can see within 30 feet of it as if you were holding it. The crossbow uses your ability modifier for its attack rolls and damage rolls. When commanded to attack, it magically draws itself and loads the next Bolt in its case, if any.

The first time a creature with the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition (excluding you) enters a space within 30 feet of the deployed crossbow, the crossbow emits a small chirping sound audible out to 100 feet. The deployed crossbow is an object with AC 12, HP 30, and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison and Psychic damage. If it's reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it falls over and can't be deployed again or used in this way for 1 minute. You can retrieve the deployed crossbow as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while you're in its space.

item.name: Visage of Delight and Disaster
item.reqAttune: by a Creature with a Charisma of 17+
entries: The expression of this mask subtly changes to reflect your expression and mouth's movement behind the mask. While wearing the mask, you gain two Bardic Inspiration dice, which are d10s (these dice are added to any Bardic Inspiration dice you have from another source). These dice are used in the same way and grant the same benefits as the level 1 Bard class feature, but they carry with them additional benefits based on the mood of your performance, as described below. You regain all expended Bardic Inspiration dice when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Delight
entries: When you confer one of these Bardic Inspiration dice on a creature with a joyful or uplifting performance, that creature has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. In addition, when that creature uses the Bardic Inspiration die, it gains an amount of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice the number rolled on the die.

entries.name: Disaster
entries: When you confer one of these Bardic Inspiration dice on a creature with a grim or haunting performance, that creature has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. In addition, when that creature hits a target with an attack roll using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, it can expend the Bardic Inspiration die, rolling the die and dealing extra Psychic damage to the target equal to twice the number rolled.

item.name: Volcanic Boots
entries: These heavy volcanic stones rearrange themselves to better fit your feet once you've attuned to them. Despite their appearance, the boots are comfortably light and warm. While wearing the boots, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage.

The boots have 10 charges and regain {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. When you walk at least 5 feet on solid ground while wearing the boots, you can expend 1 or more charges as part of that movement. Choose an unoccupied space you just walked through for each expended charge. The ground and air in a 5-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} within the chosen space or spaces become volcanic and impossibly hot for 1 minute. Any creature other than you that enters one of these {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cubes} or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} Fire damage. The {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cubes} return to normal if you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition or end the effect early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

item.name: Volt Gauntlets
entries: These metallic gauntlets have several sharp points extending from their knuckles and are lined with an insulating material. {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} made with your fists while wearing the gauntlets deal Piercing damage instead of Bludgeoning.

In addition, the gauntlets have 6 charges and regain {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn. While the gauntlets have at least 1 charge, they give off occasional snaps and sparks of electricity while worn. When you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} using the gauntlets, you can expend 1 charge to deal an extra {@damage 1d8} Lightning damage to the target. When you do, the target must also make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it can't make {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} until the start of its next turn.

item.name: Vox Helm
entries: This metal helm has a second guard that rests in front of your mouth and alters your voice. While wearing the helmet with the guard lowered, your voice sounds obviously enchanted and can be adjusted to modify its pitch, masculinity or femininity, and reverberation at will (no action required).

item.name: Wand of Whispers
entries: These wands are sometimes found in the hands of clandestine mages posing as simple townsfolk. Its magic wax and iron design resembles a candle and candlestick. The wand produces a harmless, magical flame that is indistinguishable from normal candlelight, although the wax never melts or changes shape. The flame is harmless and sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 5-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 5 feet. While holding the wand, you can extinguish or relight the flame as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

This wand has 7 charges. While holding the wand, you can cast the {@spell Message|XPHB} cantrip from it, or you can expend 3 charges to cast the {@spell Sending|XPHB} spell from it. Alternatively, when you cast a level 0-5 spell while holding the wand, you can expend 1 charge per slot level to ignore the spell's Verbal components.

The wand regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dusk. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the magical flame burns out and the wand becomes a nonmagical Candle and candlestick.

item.name: War Standard of Battlecries
entries: This magic battle standard reinforces the fervor of nearby allies. The standard stands up to 10 feet tall but can be collapsed down to 2 feet tall for ease of transport. You can extend the standard to its full height as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and either hold it aloft with one hand or plant it upright in an unoccupied space on solid ground. You can retrieve the planted standard when you enter its space (no action required). While the standard is extended, you and your allies within 15 feet of it at the start of combat gain a +3 bonus to {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}.

This standard has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn. If you're within 15 feet of the extended standard, you can expend 1 charge and shout the command word, "Charge!" to immediately take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. When you do, your allies within 15 feet of the standard can take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on their next turn.
You can change the emblem depicted on the standard over the course of 1 hour, which can be done during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Warmind Wand
entries: This wand is made from the shattered hilt and jawbone of a defeated warband leader. It has 7 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the wand releases a small gasp of breath as its magic escapes it, leaving the wand as a nonmagical sword hilt.
While holding the wand, you can use it to command and bolster your allies by expending 1 or more charges to use any of the following properties.

entries.name: Forced Endurance
entries: When an ally within 30 feet of you who can see or hear you is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but not killed outright, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend 2 charges to cause that creature to drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead. Once this property is used on a creature, that creature can't benefit from it again until it finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Form Ranks
entries: You can expend 1 charge as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to maneuver one of your comrades into another position and bolster its resolve. When you do, choose an ally who can see or hear you. That ally gains {@dice 2d4 + 2} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} and can use their {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half their {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

entries.name: Unyielding Critical
entries: When an ally within 30 feet who can see or hear you scores a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to amplify the brutality of that attack. Roll {@dice 2d4} and add the total to the ally's damage roll.

entries.name: Warband Strike
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks and expend 1 charge to direct one of your companions to strike. When you do, choose a willing creature who can see or hear you. That creature can immediately use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

item.name: Wavebender's Leiomano
entries: This long wooden oar is ornamented with burnt designs and has a line of shark teeth along its edge. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
