While holding the leiomano, you can cast the {@spell Control Water|XPHB} spell ("redirect flow" only) from it. The spell ends early if you are no longer holding the weapon. Once the weapon has been used to cast this spell, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

In addition, if you use this weapon as an oar when rowing a waterborne vehicle, that vehicle's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is increased by 5 feet (or 1/2 mile per hour) for each Wavebender's Leiomano being used to row it.

item.name: Webgrip Rucksack
entries: Even though the eyes adorning this spiderskin bag are lifeless and unmoving, they still blink on rare occasion. The bag is lined with a thin layer of adhesive web. Items stored inside the bag are held in place by the web, preventing them from clattering around or from being removed without your knowledge while the bag is on your person. While you're wearing the bag, creatures other than you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB}) checks to steal from the bag.

The bag and its eight straps adhere to the surface they're pressed against, such as your torso or the wall of a tavern. If you're wearing the bag, you can gently remove the straps from your body as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. Other creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Strength checks made to wrench the bag from you. The bag can hold up to 8 cubic feet or 80 pounds. Spiderskin is pliable and stretches easily, allowing the bag to shrink and grow to accommodate the objects placed inside to some degree.

item.name: Wildman's Spinning Cleaver
entries: This elaborate weapon channels your bloodlust. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Immediately after hitting or missing with a ranged attack using the weapon, it flies back to your hand.

In addition, once on each of your turns when you make a ranged attack with this weapon while your Rage is active, you can immediately make a second ranged attack with it against a different target as part of the same attack. The second target must be within 10 feet of the first and can be beyond the {@item Handaxe|XPHB}'s long range; this second attack roll is made as if it were against a target within the same range as the initial target. The axe flies back to your hand after it hits or misses the second target.

item.name: Windrazor
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which has the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} property. The sword has 10 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 4} expended charges daily at dawn. While holding the weapon, you can expend 1 or more charge to cast one of the following spells (save DC 17, +5 spellcasting ability modifier) from it: {@spell Heroism|XPHB} (1 charge) or {@spell Wind Wall|XPHB} (3 charges).

entries.name: Air Rend
entries: This weapon can manipulate the air around it to generate blades of wind, delivering devastating attacks at a distance. When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can replace one or more of your attacks, expending 1 charge for each attack you replace in this way. For each replaced attack, you make a special ranged attack against a target within 30 feet of you as if you were attacking it with the sword.

entries.name: Sudden Storm
entries: When you take damage from a creature within 5 feet of you while holding the sword, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to make a melee attack with the sword against that creature. Additionally, if a creature hits you with a ranged attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend 1 charge to slash at the projectile and reduce the attack's total damage against you by {@dice 1d10 + 5}. If you reduce the damage to 0, you destroy the projectile, provided it's nonmagical.

item.name: Windswept Wyvernplate
entries: This armor seems to grow lighter when you inhale. You can hold your breath at any point to both lighten the armor and quiet its clattering, negating the normal {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks imposed by Half Plate.
In addition, if you fall while wearing this armor, you can hold your breath as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cast the {@spell Feather Fall|XPHB} spell, targeting only yourself.

item.name: Winter's Embrace
entries: This frosty armor is enchanted to keep its wearer at a constant, comfortable temperature. While wearing the armor, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold and Fire damage, and {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} composed of ice or snow doesn't cost you extra movement.

In addition, while wearing the armor, you can cast the {@spell Ice Storm|XPHB} spell (level 6 version; save DC 16) from it. When cast in this way, the spell is centered on a point within 5 feet of you, and you are immune to its effects. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Wisplight Lantern
entries: This dark Hooded Lantern doesn't require fuel; it continuously glows with a will-o'-wisp's blue flame that can reveal the final moments of a creature's life. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can adjust the lantern's hood to change the light's radius, causing it to cast {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 5- to 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional number of feet equal to the chosen radius. Alternatively, you can close the lantern's hood entirely, causing it to cast no light.

The lantern has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d3 + 1} expended charges daily at dusk. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while holding the lantern, you can expend 1 charge and touch it to a Large or smaller corpse. When you do, a ghostly blue illusion of the fallen creature appears above its corpse and silently reenacts the last 6 seconds of its conscious life. The illusion only shows the creature's actions (for example, reacting to an unseen monster or trying to extinguish the flames of a fiery trap but not showing the responsible monster or effect). This property has no effect on Undead creatures. Once a corpse's death has been shown, it can't be shown by this Wisplight Lantern again.

Alternatively, while holding the lantern, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and use a command word to expend 1 charge. If there are any Undead creatures within 60 feet of you that aren't behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}, the lantern's light flashes red before returning to its normal blue glow.

item.name: Wooden Chain of the Unbroken Circle
entries: This magic chain shirt is crafted using strange wooden links enchanted by a militant circle of Druids. The wooden chain is reinforced with old, natural magic that responds to the wearer's reverent connection to nature. While wearing this armor, you gain a bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} equal to your Wisdom modifier (maximum of +2). You are considered trained with this armor even if you lack training with Medium armor.

item.name: Wyvernwing
entries: This enchanted {@item Greatsword|XPHB} contains a wyvern's wing that'sstretched between three thin, collapsed blades. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

The sword has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 1 charge to flick open the sword like a fan and swing it down to create a buffeting wind. When you do, you can choose for the wind to either affect creatures in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} or affect all other creatures within 5 feet of you. Each affected creature must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 2d6} Bludgeoning damage and is pushed up to 10 feet away from you. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage only. The weapon then magically collapses again as the wind settles.

item.name: Zafu of the Wandering Mind
entries: This soft, elaborately stitched pillow set focuses the intensity of your meditation. It takes 1 minute to settle into a meditation while sitting on the pillows. You can hold a light conversation while you meditate in this way but are otherwise unable to move or take actions. While meditating in this way, you can magically levitate yourself and the pillows up to 5 feet in the air. This meditation ends if you take damage, if you are no longer on the pillows, or if you decide to end it (no action required). While you're within 100 feet of at least one of the pillows, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and use a command word to cause them to be magically shunted into a special pocket dimension. Repeating the command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} returns them softly to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you.

In addition, this pillow set has 3 charges and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn. While meditating on the pillows, you can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Arcane Eye|XPHB} spell without ending your meditation. If your meditation ends, your {@status Concentration|XPHB} on the spell is broken.

item.name: Galea of the Soulfire Phoenix
entries: This magical helmet once belonged to the Soulfire Phoenix Fara, a paragon to the citizens of the Elemental Plane of Fire who led her people to victory during the Savrayan Wars. While some believe that her ferocity and heroism were a result of phoenix blood running through her veins, others claim that a hero will always rise from the ashes when the time is right.

A spark of this heroism resides within the helmet, imparting its passion and phoenix-like properties to you. While wearing the helmet, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage, and while you're in combat, magical flames erupt from it. The fire is harmless and sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. If you die while attuned to the helmet, it vanishes in a cloud of embers and smoke and reappears somewhere on the Elemental Plane of Fire.

entries.name: Fiery Spirit
entries: While wearing the helm, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions, and if you roll a 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20} for a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB}, you regain 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. These benefits also extend to your allies within the helmet's {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}.

entries.name: Ignite
entries: While wearing the helmet, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to cast the {@spell Fire Shield|XPHB} spell (warm shield only) from it. When you do, you also sprout a pair of flaming wings that last for the duration of the spell and give you a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Call of the Phoenix
entries: This helm has 7 charges and regains {@dice 1d4 + 3} expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the helmet, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 18, +5 spellcasting ability modifier) from it: {@spell Death Ward|XPHB} (3 charges), {@spell Flame Strike|XPHB} (4 charges), or {@spell Healing Word|XPHB} (level 2 version; 1 charge).

entries.name: From the Ashes
entries: If you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but not killed outright while wearing the helmet, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to call upon the spark of heroism that resides within the galea to drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead. When you do, you immediately regain 14 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and each creature within 10 feet of you must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d10} Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. In addition, two flaming wings unfurl from your back, carrying you to an unoccupied space of your choice up to 60 feet away from you without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. The wings then disappear. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until 2 days have passed.

entries.name: Random Properties
entries: The Galea of the Soulfire Phoenix has the following random properties:

items: 1 {@table Artifact Properties; Major Beneficial Properties|XDMG|major beneficial} property
2 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|XDMG|minor detrimental} properties
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Major Detrimental Properties|XDMG|major detrimental} property

entries.name: Destroying the Helmet
entries: The only way to destroy the helmet is to feed it to an ancient phoenix. The phoenix dies and is immediately reborn as a result of the intense magic stored within the Artifact. When it's reborn, the phoenix has a pronounced crown of golden feathers upon its head. Regardless of how many times it's reborn, the phoenix retains this crown of feathers.

item.name: Precipit, the Formless
entries: This magic coral staff was first found within the Great Reef of the Elemental Plane of Water. Its origins beyond that are entirely unknown, with some suggesting it was made by the plane itself rather than forged or crafted by a mere creature's hands. Attuning to the staff bestows some of its planar powers to you, allowing you to breathe underwater and ignore any of the drawbacks caused by a deep, underwater environment. In addition, while holding the staff, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage.
This staff has 15 charges, which are used to power its various properties. The staff regains {@dice 1d8 + 7} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: State Change
entries: The staff has a powerful control over the various states of water. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding the staff, you can mentally command the staff's ever-present bounty of water to magically change its state into a gas, liquid, or solid. The properties of each state are detailed below. Alternatively, you can expend 1 charge as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you are targeted by an attack or spell to force the staff to change its state more rapidly.

When you do, the staff's change is immediate, potentially affecting the outcome of the incoming attack or spell. You take this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you know you are the target of the attack or spell, but before knowing the result of the roll.

entries.name: Gas
entries: When the staff enters its gaseous state, the water surrounding it becomes a fine mist. While holding the staff in this state, you gain the following benefits:

items: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning and Piercing damage.
Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet.
You have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
You can move through the space of any creature, regardless of its size, and another creature's space isn't difficult terrain for you.
{@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} against you are made with {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

entries.name: Gas Spells
entries: While holding the staff in its gas state, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 18) from it: {@spell Cloudkill|XPHB} (5 charges), {@spell Fog Cloud|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Gaseous Form|XPHB} (3 charges), {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} (2 charges), or {@spell Wind Walk|XPHB} (6 charges).

entries.name: Tailwind
entries: While holding the staff in its gas state, you can expend 2 charges and use a command word as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to grant additional benefits to you and nearby allies. Until the end of your next turn, you and your allies within 30 feet of you can take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and don't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} by moving.

entries.name: Liquid
entries: When the staff enters its liquid state, the water surrounding the staff becomes a swirling torrent. While holding the staff in this state, you gain the following benefits:

items: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning and Slashing damage.
Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet.
You have a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
You can stand on and move across any liquid surface as if it were solid ground.

When an enemy that you can see misses you with a melee attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to force that creature to repeat the same attack against a different creature of your choice (other than itself).

entries.name: Liquid Spells
entries: While holding the staff in its liquid state, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 18) from it: {@spell Blur|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Control Water|XPHB} (4 charges), {@spell Create or Destroy Water|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Mirror Image|XPHB} (2 charges), or {@spell Water Breathing|XPHB} (3 charges).

entries.name: Tide Turner
entries: While holding the staff in its liquid state, you can expend 3 charges and use a command word as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to send out a wave of ethereal water to buffet and push your enemies. Each enemy of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you must make a DC 18 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 4d8} Bludgeoning damage and is pushed either 20 feet toward you or away from you (your choice). On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.

entries.name: Solid
entries: When the staff enters its solid state, the water surrounding the staff becomes a frozen shell. While holding the staff in this state, you gain the following benefits:

items: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Piercing and Slashing damage.
Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 5 feet.
You have a +3 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}.
If you're pushed against your will, the distance you are pushed is halved.
You gain 10 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of each of your turns, which vanish if the staff changes state.

entries.name: Solid Spells
entries: While holding the staff in its solid state, you can expend some charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 18; +10 attack bonus) from it: {@spell Cone of Cold|XPHB} (5 charges), {@spell Ice Storm|XPHB} (4 charges), {@spell Ray of Frost|XPHB} (0 charges, or 1 charge to cast at level 5), or {@spell Wall of Ice|XPHB} (6 charges).

entries.name: Deep Freeze
entries: While holding the staff in its solid state, you can expend 4 charges and use a command word as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to send out a chilling blast to freeze your foes. Enemies of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 4d8} Cold damage, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a target takes half as much Cold damage, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved until the end of its next turn instead.

entries.name: Random Properties
entries: Precipit, the Formless has the following random properties:

items: 2 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|XDMG|minor beneficial} properties
2 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|XDMG|minor detrimental} properties

entries.name: Destroying the Staff
entries: The only way to destroy the staff is to keep it untouched by water for 100 years while on the Elemental Plane of Water, at which point it crumbles to dust. Any amount or precipitation (such as a puff of misty air, a drip of water, or the touch of ice) during that time reinvigorates the staff's bounty of water.

item.name: Tectonic Gauntlets
entries: This magical set of stone gauntlets imbues your fists with the power of the Elemental Plane of Earth. While wearing the gauntlets, your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} with your fists deal {@damage 1d8} Bludgeoning damage instead of the usual Bludgeoning damage (unless it's already higher), and they deal double damage to objects and structures. In addition, these gauntlets have 15 charges and regain {@dice 1d10 + 5} expended charges daily at dawn. When you make an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} using your fists, you can expend up to 5 charges to extend your fist's reach by 5 feet for each expended charge.

entries.name: Powerglove
entries: When you hit a target with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend up to 3 charges to deal an extra {@damage 1d8} Bludgeoning damage for each charge spent. If you spend any charges in this way, the target must also succeed on a DC 18 Strength saving throw or be pushed 5 feet away from you.

entries.name: Geomancy
entries: You can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 18) from them: {@spell Earthquake|XPHB} (8 charges), {@spell Move Earth|XPHB} (6 charges), {@spell Shatter|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Spike Growth|XPHB} (2 charges, with stone projections), or {@spell Wall of Stone|XPHB} (5 charges).

entries.name: Tectonic Plate Armor
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can cause rocks to envelop and protect your body. For 1 hour, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved, and you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity checks and Dexterity saving throws. You can end this property early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. While enveloped in this way, you can burrow through nonmagical, unworked earth and stone using a {@variantrule Burrow Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet. While doing so, you don't disturb the material you move through. If you end this property while burrowed in this way, you're shunted to the nearest unoccupied space and take {@damage 1d10} Force damage. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Curse
entries: These gauntlets are cursed. Your mind and body become sluggish once you attune to the gauntlets. The gauntlets become one with your flesh and prevent you from removing them. Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 5 feet, and your Intelligence score is reduced by 4 (to a minimum of 7) while cursed. The curse remains even if the attunement ends but can be undone by any effect that removes a curse such as a {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} or {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell, allowing you to remove the gauntlets and end the curse on you.

entries.name: Random Properties
entries: The gauntlets have the following random properties:

items: 1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|XDMG|minor beneficial} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|XDMG|minor detrimental} property

entries.name: Destroying the Gauntlets
entries: The gauntlets can only be destroyed in the depths of the crystalline mines of Durhun on the Elemental Plane of Earth where they were first created.

item.name: The Griffon's Saddlebag
entries: These leather bags have the stylized face of a griffon on their front and appear across the multiverse in strange locations. One may be found in the belly of a dragon, while another in the limbs of a great tree. Regardless of where it's found, a Griffon's Saddlebag is typically discovered by an adventurer with a destiny.

The saddlebags are made by a great, six-armed griffon on the Astral Plane named Criir. Believed by some to be a demigod of pocket dimensions and craftsmen everywhere, Criir is a patron of artificers and creators across the multiverse.

The griffon creates magic items in its great forge using materials collected from various planes of existence to add to their collection. While no one knows exactly how to contact Criir, the astral griffon has a knack for knowing what items are needed by whom.

entries.name: Superior Holding
entries: This 1-foot-wide bag always weighs 3 pounds, regardless of what's inside it. It functions as a {@item Bag of Holding|XDMG} that can only be pierced by another Artifact. Placing a {@item Bag of Holding|XDMG}, {@item Heward's Handy Haversack|XDMG} , {@item Portable Hole|XDMG}, or similar item inside the extradimensional space created by the saddlebag, or vice versa, destroys the other extradimensional item but leaves the saddlebag unharmed. Any item stored within the other extradimensional space is magically moved to the space within the saddlebag instead. If doing so overloads the bag, random contents from either space are scattered in the Astral Plane until the bag isn't overloaded.

entries.name: Magic Courier
entries: At the GM's discretion, the saddlebag's beak opens up to reveal a letter. The magical script can be read by any creature with an Intelligence of 4 or higher. The note is a delivery request or similar task that includes the name of an item as well as either specific or vague descriptions of a person or place. At the bottom of the letter is a price listed in coins or other form of payment as well as a date or time depending on the task's urgency.

If the saddlebag is brought to the described person or place within the allotted time, it dispenses the magic item into the hand of the appropriate recipient. If the payment isn't received by the bag within 1 hour of dispensing the magic item, the item vanishes and is lost. After receiving the payment for an item in this way, the bag's beak reopens to dispense a reward for the courier.

entries.name: Sell Magic Items
entries: The saddlebag has a connection to Criir's forge on the Astral Plane. Depending on the magic item, you can sell a magic item to the saddlebag for half the cost of its base price.

entries.name: Spells
entries: The saddlebag has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the saddlebag, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells from it: {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Identify|XPHB} (1 charge), or {@spell Legend Lore|XPHB} (5 charges).

entries.name: Additional Spells
entries: At the GM's discretion, such as after reaching certain milestones or completing Magic Courier quests, the saddlebag may offer one or more of the following additional spells that can be cast from it: {@spell Blink|XPHB} (3 charges), {@spell Dimension Door|XPHB} (4 charges), Divination (4 charges), {@spell Etherealness|XPHB} (7 charges), {@spell Fly|XPHB} (3 charges), {@spell Mordenkainen's Magnificent Mansion|XPHB} (7 charges), {@spell Plane Shift|XPHB} (7 charges), or {@spell Leomund's Secret Chest|XPHB} (4 charges).

entries.name: Sentience
entries: The saddlebag is a sentient Chaotic Neutral item with an Intelligence of 13, a Wisdom of 15, and a Charisma of 16. It has hearing and normal vision with a range of 60 feet.

The bag knows Common. When in the presence of only creatures it trusts, the bag can speak audibly, its beak moving as it speaks. Otherwise, the bag communicates telepathically with its wearer. The bag can also speak and communicate with griffons.

entries.name: Personality
entries: The bag's purpose is to carry, collect, and deliver magic items. Different bags have different personalities, but they are for the most part largely pleasant to talk to. Their voices are birdlike and are filled with occasional coos and squawks, and their stitched eyes widen, squint, and move expressively.

If the wearer or its allies fail to fulfill the bag's Magic Courier quests, it may stop offering its magic to them. If it does, their items may be lost or inaccessible within the bag or potentially spat out. While the bag provides the names of item recipients and delivery locations, it doesn't know any more information than what was provided to it by the astral griffon. Regardless, it does its best to help its wearer and allies find the specified person or location on the letter.

entries.name: Destroying the Griffon's Saddlebag
entries: While there is no way to permanently destroy the saddlebag, piercing it with an Artifact returns it to its place of origin for repair before it's returned to the multiverse in a new location. Placing a Griffon's Saddlebag inside the extradimensional space created by another Griffon's Saddlebag returns both items to their place of origin and sends all the items stored within them to Criir's forge on the Astral Plane.

item.name: Bird of a Feather (Common)
entries: This small, stuffed roc toy is filled with enchanted down. While holding the toy, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 20 feet for 1 minute or until your {@status Concentration|XPHB} ends. The stuffed toy's weak magic can carry up to 200 pounds. If you take any damage while flying in this way, you lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} on the effect and immediately start falling.
When the effect ends, the toy becomes nonmagical.

item.name: Bird of a Feather (Uncommon)
entries: This small, stuffed roc toy is filled with enchanted down. While holding the toy, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 20 feet for 1 minute or until your {@status Concentration|XPHB} ends. The stuffed toy's weak magic can carry up to 200 pounds. If you take any damage while flying in this way, you lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} on the effect and immediately start falling.

When the effect ends, the toy becomes nonmagical. If the toy is of Uncommon rarity, it instead can't be used again for {@dice 1d4} hours after the effect ends. It gives a happy little chirp once its magic can be used again.

item.name: Chromacloth
entries: This silken cloth is enchanted with a unique blend of Abjuration and Evocation magic, allowing it to be used for a number of purposes. It can be one of four colors: red, yellow, green, or blue. While wearing or holding the Chromacloth, you can change its color to another one as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action.

The first time you take damage of the type associated with the Chromacloth's color while wearing it as a headband or wrap, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage. Once the cloth reduces damage in this way, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

Alternatively, the cloth can be fashioned to be an effective {@item Sling|XPHB}. Ammunition fired from it while using it as a {@item Sling|XPHB} deals damage of the type associated with the cloth's color instead of Bludgeoning.
Refer to the table below for a reference of the Chromacloth's associated colors and damage types:

item.name: Doomsday Cookie
entries: This black sugarpaste cookie is made using a grim recipe passed down through generations of Clerics devoted to gods of death. Inside this hollow cookie is a blank strip of enchanted paper whose writing only appears when you break open the cookie. The paper's writing vaguely predicts the manner in which you'll die. When you open this cookie, roll {@dice 1d20} and use the table below to determine the paper's prediction.

Once a creature's death has been predicted in this way, all future Doomsday Cookie papers will predict the same outcome for that creature. If a creature dies in a way foretold by the cookie (at the GM's discretion), the cookie's paper transforms into a {@item Spell Scroll|XDMG} of Revivify. This scroll can only be used on the creature whose passing it foretold. If the scroll is unused within 1 minute, it crumbles into flour.
Regardless of its grim foretellings, the cookie itself is a delicious snack that is sweet, salty, and smoky.

entries.caption: Doomsday Cookie Predictions
rows: Your fiery passions burn too hot.
The light of the full moon casts the deepest shadows.
Vainglorious you stumble, entranced by your own reflection.
Great heights bring low all who reach them.
Betrayal strikes deeper than any blade.
Hasty hands make for quick lives.
Getting ahead of yourself may separate you both.
The crushing weight of the world is too much.
Your demise is met with revelry, gnashing jaws, and gluttony.
Beware the viridian, in color and emotion.
Mortality is shed as quickly as skin or clothing.
Hold onto each breath. Savor it as your last.
70 percent of accidents happen in the home.
Stars die as new ones rise.
The stench of failure burns your nostrils.

item.name: Frefil's Jolly Oozebean Sugarbombs
entries: A pack of oozebeans comes with 4 inside it. When you open a pack, roll {@dice 4d8} and record each number. Use these numbers to determine the kinds of oozebeans found in the bag as shown in the table below. You can eat an oozebean as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Each color has an effect that lasts for 1 minute.

entries.name: Upset Stomach
entries: You can only be under the effects of one sugarbomb that has this property at a time. Eating a sugarbomb that has this property while under the effects of another one causes the effects of the first sugarbomb to immediately end and be replaced by the new one's. This rapid change is difficult to stomach, forcing you to make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute.

rows: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Acid damage and can safely eat otherwise inedible and nonpoisonous organic materials.
Whenever you successfully {@condition grappled|XPHB|grapple} a creature or another creature successfully grapples you, that creature takes {@damage 1d8} Acid damage.
You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions.
You gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage and {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any ability check you make to end the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions.
You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage, and you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to damage from the {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB} spell.

item.name: Firecracker Crystals
entries: These multicolored crystals are usually purchased in small bags at festivals and holiday events. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can throw one of the 1-inch crystals against a solid surface within 20 feet of you to crack it apart. When a crystal breaks, it releases the magic inside. The magic ends at the end of your turn and is entirely sensory.

A bag of Firecracker Crystals comes with {@dice 1d4 + 8} crystals inside. Roll {@dice 1d8} for each crystal in the bag and record each number rolled. Use these numbers to determine the kinds of crystals found in the bag, as shown on the table below.

rows: Releases a small flash of {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 5-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 5 feet, followed by the smell of spices and citrus
Releases small spiraling motes of energy that fizz and fly about in a 5-foot radius
Releases a cloud of bright, verdant smoke that fills a 5-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} and smells both foul and sickly sweet
Releases a series of comically large bubbles that float outward from the point of impact in a 5-foot radius
Releases the sound of music; the song is random, and plays only enough to make it recognizable as music
Releases a dazzling display of sparkling glitter in a 5-foot radius
Releases a mystery effect; when used, roll on this table again to determine the effect, rerolling until you don't roll an 8

item.name: Heart of the Sleeveless
entries: This vest is made from a luxurious, silky material that seems to always make the wearer appear more fit. The clothing gently hugs the wearer, redistributing weight and muscle to flatter and accentuate their physique. It seems to deflate sadly once taken off.

While worn, the fabric's color changes with the wearer's mood or flash of emotion. Refer to the table below for the vest's colors when affected by different emotions. While unworn, the fabric's color becomes a muted blue.

item.name: Mask of Dendallen
entries: This skull mask covers the top of your face and offers several benefits while worn. The mask has 5 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dusk. While wearing the mask, you can expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells (save DC 16) from it: {@spell Bestow Curse|XPHB}, {@spell Fear|XPHB}, {@spell Speak with Dead|XPHB}, or {@spell Tongues|XPHB}. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws made to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} on a spell you cast in this way. In addition, you understand any written language that you see.

entries.name: Curse
entries: When you place this cursed mask over your face for the first time, make a DC 16 Intelligence saving throw. On a successful save, you are aware of the curse's effects and can immediately remove the mask before it extends the curse to you. On a failed save, you become cursed, and the skin surrounding the mask is riddled with blackened veins and sickly gray skin; you take {@damage 5d10} Psychic damage, and you can't remove the mask unless you are targeted by a {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell or similar magic.

While wearing this mask, you can't smell or taste anything, and you automatically fail any Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) check that relies on smell or taste. In addition, whenever you roll a 1 on {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you take {@damage 1d10} Psychic damage. When you remove the mask after being cursed by it, it takes {@dice 1d4} days for the veins and skin on your face to return to normal.
Your voice also changes. Use the table below to determine the new voice.

rows: Your voice deepens and does not echo.
Your voice becomes raspy and you overly enunciate consonant sounds.
Your voice becomes quiet, almost a whisper, but can still be heard as if spoken at a normal volume.
Your voice becomes high-pitched, and your laugh maniacal.
You slur your words, as if your tongue is suddenly too large for your mouth, and your spit releases a faint hiss as it escapes your mouth.
Your voice becomes that of another ally's and changes each time you complete a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Potion of Dragon's Breath
entries: Drinking this potion grants you a brief surge of draconic power. Once within the next minute, you can replace one of your attacks when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action with an exhalation of magical energy, which has the same effects as the Dragonborn's Breath Weapon trait. The damage type for the exhalation is randomly determined from the Draconic Ancestors table in the Dragonborn species description when this potion is created. The amount of damage the exhalation deals and its save DC are determined by the potion's rarity, as shown in the table below.

rows: {@item Potion of Dragon's Breath (Very Rare)|24GriffonsSaddlebag1|Very Rare}

entries: When crafting a Potion of Dragon's Breath, you can use the harvested stomach and gizzard of a fallen dragon in place of the raw materials normally needed to craft a magic item. If you do, the exhalation's damage type is determined by the kind of dragon the materials were sourced from, and the potion's rarity is determined by the dragon's age category: Uncommon (young dragon), Rare (adult dragon), or Very Rare (ancient dragon). Once you start crafting a Potion of Dragon's Breath in this way, if you don't spend at least 2 hours working to craft it on a given day before finishing it, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the harvested materials are destroyed, and the potion spoils.

item.name: Potion of Dragon's Breath (Uncommon)
entries: Drinking this potion grants you a brief surge of draconic power. Once within the next minute, you can replace one of your attacks when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action with an exhalation of magical energy, which has the same effects as the Dragonborn's Breath Weapon trait. The damage type for the exhalation is randomly determined from the Draconic Ancestors table in the Dragonborn species description when this potion is created. The amount of damage the exhalation deals and its save DC are determined by the potion's rarity, as shown in the table below.

rows: {@item Potion of Dragon's Breath (Very Rare)|24GriffonsSaddlebag1|Very Rare}

If you do, the exhalation's damage type is determined by the kind of dragon the materials were sourced from, and the potion's rarity is determined by the dragon's age category: Uncommon (young dragon), Rare (adult dragon), or Very Rare (ancient dragon). Once you start crafting a Potion of Dragon's Breath in this way, if you don't spend at least 2 hours working to craft it on a given day before finishing it, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the harvested materials are destroyed, and the potion spoils.

Once within the next minute, you can replace one of your attacks when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action with an exhalation of magical energy, which has the same effects as the Dragonborn's Breath Weapon trait. The damage type for the exhalation is randomly determined from the Draconic Ancestors table in the Dragonborn species description when this potion is created. The amount of damage the exhalation deals and its save DC are determined by the potion's rarity, as shown in the table below.

rows: {@item Potion of Dragon's Breath (Very Rare)|24GriffonsSaddlebag1|Very Rare}

If you do, the exhalation's damage type is determined by the kind of dragon the materials were sourced from, and the potion's rarity is determined by the dragon's age category: Uncommon (young dragon), Rare (adult dragon), or Very Rare (ancient dragon). Once you start crafting a Potion of Dragon's Breath in this way, if you don't spend at least 2 hours working to craft it on a given day before finishing it, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the harvested materials are destroyed, and the potion spoils.

entries: Drinking this potion grants you a brief surge of draconic power. Once within the next minute, you can replace one of your attacks when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action with an exhalation of magical energy, which has the same effects as the Dragonborn's Breath Weapon trait. The damage type for the exhalation is randomly determined from the Draconic Ancestors table in the Dragonborn species description when this potion is created. The amount of damage the exhalation deals and its save DC are determined by the potion's rarity, as shown in the table below.

rows: {@item Potion of Dragon's Breath (Very Rare)|24GriffonsSaddlebag1|Very Rare}

entries: When crafting a Potion of Dragon's Breath, you can use the harvested stomach and gizzard of a fallen dragon in place of the raw materials normally needed to craft a magic item. If you do, the exhalation's damage type is determined by the kind of dragon the materials were sourced from, and the potion's rarity is determined by the dragon's age category: Uncommon (young dragon), Rare (adult dragon), or Very Rare (ancient dragon). Once you start crafting a Potion of Dragon's Breath in this way, if you don't spend at least 2 hours working to craft it on a given day before finishing it, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the harvested materials are destroyed, and the potion spoils.

item.name: Prismatic Javelin
entries: This dark crystal {@item Javelin|XPHB} has a prismatic head that can refract light into beautiful displays of color. Immediately after you hit a target with a ranged attack roll using the {@item Javelin|XPHB}, it disappears in a small burst of light before reappearing in your open hand.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can throw the {@item Javelin|XPHB} at a point you can see within 120 feet of you and use a command word. The {@item Javelin|XPHB} erupts at that point in a dazzling display of color and light, casting painful {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. Each creature of your choice within the {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} that isn't behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw. For each target, roll {@dice 1d6} to determine which beam of light affects it, consulting the table below. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 2d10} damage of the type corresponding to the beam's color for each beam of light it is struck by. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

rows: The creature is struck by two beams of light. Roll twice, rerolling any 6.

item.name: Ring of Wind
entries: While wearing this ring, you can use it to cast the {@spell Wind Wall|XPHB} spell (save DC 13). Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
When cast from the ring, the wall appears in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you, which is stationary. The wall is 15 feet high and 1 foot thick.

item.name: Ring of Fire
entries: While wearing this ring, you can use it to cast the {@spell Wall of Fire|XPHB} spell (save DC 15). Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
When cast from the ring, the wall appears in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you, which is stationary. The wall is 20 feet high and 1 foot thick.

item.name: Ring of Ice
entries: While wearing this ring, you can use it to cast the {@spell Wall of Ice|XPHB} spell (save DC 16). Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
When cast from the ring, the wall appears in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you, which is stationary. The wall is 10 feet high and 1 foot thick.

item.name: Ring of Stone
entries: While wearing this ring, you can use it to cast the {@spell Wall of Stone|XPHB} spell (save DC 16). Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
When cast from the ring, the wall appears in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you, which is stationary. The wall is 10 feet high and 6 inches thick.

item.name: Scholar's Cap +1
entries: This hat carries with it the cleverness and knowledge of many previous scholars. While wearing it, you gain a +1 bonus to Intelligence checks and Intelligence saving throws. In addition, for every 5 days you spend researching a subject, you gain knowledge as if you had been researching it for 1 additional day.

item.name: Scholar's Cap +2
entries: This hat carries with it the cleverness and knowledge of many previous scholars. While wearing it, you gain a +2 bonus to Intelligence checks and Intelligence saving throws. In addition, for every 5 days you spend researching a subject, you gain knowledge as if you had been researching it for 2 additional days.

item.name: Scholar's Cap +3
entries: This hat carries with it the cleverness and knowledge of many previous scholars. While wearing it, you gain a +3 bonus to Intelligence checks and Intelligence saving throws. In addition, for every 5 days you spend researching a subject, you gain knowledge as if you had been researching it for 3 additional days.

item.name: Singing Stein
entries: While this pewter stein holds at least a sip's worth of potable alcohol, the face adorning it comes alive to sing songs based on the kind of drink it contains (examples are shown in the table below). If there are multiple kinds of alcohol within the stein, it either alternates between song types or does its best to combine their various lyrics. The stein isn't sentient, but it does have a pleasant singing voice. If there are multiple Singing Steins in the same room, they can sing together in harmony if they have the same kind of drink within them.

If the drink inside the stein is {@condition poisoned}, it sings songs slightly out of key, which can be discerned by a creature that takes a {@action Search|XPHB} action to make a successful Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) check. The DC for this check is determined by the GM.

item.name: Snugglebeast (Dragon)
entries: When a creature regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the end of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} by spending one or more of its {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, that creature magically regains extra {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} if it spent the {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} snuggling with one of these enchanted toys. The rare dragon variant of the snugglebeast restores {@dice 3d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

A creature can only benefit from one Snugglebeast at a time. Once this property of the Snugglebeast is used by a creature, that creature can't benefit from the same Snugglebeast's magic again until it finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Snugglebeast (Owlbear)
entries: When a creature regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the end of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} by spending one or more of its {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, that creature magically regains extra {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} if it spent the {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} snuggling with one of these enchanted toys. The common owlbear variant of the snugglebeast restores {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

A creature can only benefit from one Snugglebeast at a time. Once this property of the Snugglebeast is used by a creature, that creature can't benefit from the same Snugglebeast's magic again until it finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Snugglebeast (Tarrasque)
entries: When a creature regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the end of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} by spending one or more of its {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, that creature magically regains extra {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} if it spent the {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} snuggling with one of these enchanted toys. The very rare tarrasque variant of the snugglebeast restores {@dice 4d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

A creature can only benefit from one Snugglebeast at a time. Once this property of the Snugglebeast is used by a creature, that creature can't benefit from the same Snugglebeast's magic again until it finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Snugglebeast (Unicorn)
entries: When a creature regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the end of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} by spending one or more of its {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, that creature magically regains extra {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} if it spent the {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} snuggling with one of these enchanted toys. The uncommon unicorn variant of the snugglebeast restores {@dice 2d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

A creature can only benefit from one Snugglebeast at a time. Once this property of the Snugglebeast is used by a creature, that creature can't benefit from the same Snugglebeast's magic again until it finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

item.name: Snugglebeasts
entries: Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

items: {@item Snugglebeast (Owlbear)|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}
{@item Snugglebeast (Unicorn)|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}
{@item Snugglebeast (Dragon)|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}
{@item Snugglebeast (Tarrasque)|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}

item.name: Staff of the Four Seasons
item.reqAttune: by a Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, or Wizard
entries: This staff's form changes with the seasons. It has 10 charges and can be wielded as a magic {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} that grants a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with it. As long as the staff has at least 1 charge, your melee attacks with it deal an extra {@damage 1d8} damage on a hit. This extra damage's type is based on the staff's season: spring (Radiant), summer (Fire), autumn (Necrotic), or winter (Cold).
Over the course of 10 minutes, you can focus on the staff to change its form to a different season. It remains in this form for 8 hours before reverting to the current season again.

While holding the staff, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC and spell attack modifier. The spells you can cast from the staff in this way are determined by its season, as shown in the table below.

rows: {@spell Guiding Bolt|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Moonbeam|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Plant Growth|XPHB} (3 charges)
{@spell Burning Hands|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Spike Growth|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Fireball|XPHB} (3 charges)
{@spell Inflict Wounds|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Pass Without Trace|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Vampiric Touch|XPHB} (3 charges)
{@spell Color Spray|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Darkvision|XPHB} (2 charges), {@spell Sleet Storm|XPHB} (3 charges)

entries: The staff regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the staff loses all foliage and vitality, becoming a gnarled, nonmagical {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}.

item.name: Tote of Tricky Treat Sugarbombs
entries: This canvas bag holds {@dice 5d8 + 10} clove and pumpkin candies inside. A small illusory face appears over the sewn-on pumpkin at night. Each candy has a minor magical effect that lasts for 1 minute unless otherwise specified. Some effects are pleasant, whereas others are mischievous. You can eat a piece of candy as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. When you do, roll on the table below to determine the candy's effect.

rows: The candy seems to have spoiled, forcing you to succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or spend your action on your next turn retching and reeling.
Your face appears to melt, revealing the skull beneath it. The effect is harmless and illusory.
Any food you eat or water you drink taste like ash or blood, respectively.
Other creatures' eyes appear to be hollowed out and blackened to you.
Your ears turn a different, vibrant color at the start of each of your turns.
Your hair falls out and regrows over the next minute.
Your laughter becomes maniacal, and you have trouble stopping once you've started.
You hear a fly near your ear, but you can neither see it nor swat it away.
Your skin turns orange, and light spills forth from your eyes and mouth, shedding {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet.
Confetti bursts from the top of your head, and small children can be heard laughing.
You sprout tiny, flightless bat wings.
You know the location of the nearest pumpkin within 1 mile.
Nearby crows and ravens land on your shoulders or outstretched arm.
A friendly, floppy wizard hat appears on your head. After 1 minute, it disappears in a cloud of stars.
Any alcohol in your stomach or that you drink becomes nonalcoholic.

Your voice becomes smooth and golden, allowing you to add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to Charisma ({@skill Performance}) checks made to sing. If you already have proficiency in this skill, you have {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in it instead.
Any food you eat or water you drink tastes delicious and reminds you of your favorite pastimes.
You gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, its range increases by 30 feet.
You can magically sense if a creature is possessed, and you can see creatures that have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition.

item.name: Tub of Churning and Fermentation
entries: This squat, wooden and ceramic tub has a screw-on lid that fits snuggly around its base. The tub can hold up to 2 gallons of liquid or 1 cubic foot of material within it. You can leave a liquid or other perishable in the tub and secure the lid before saying the name of the end product you'd like the contents to turn into. If you say the name of a possible result, the tub clicks lightly before locking its lid shut.

After 10 minutes, the tub gives a second, louder click as the lid unlocks. The contents of the tub will have been stirred, processed, or otherwise fermented in such a way to generate the named end product. Adding extra ingredients into the tub can impact the end product's flavor.
Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Astral Bracelet of Energy
entries: An Astral Bracelet is a powerful magical bangle that allows its wearer to see, commune, or otherwise manifest things using powers beyond the Material Plane. The metal and glyphs of each bracelet are related to the kind of power that it holds. You can pull the bracelet off and hold it in the palm of your hand as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. When you do, the bracelet unfurls to reveal the four inner rings collapsed within it, transforming the bracelet into an astronomical sphere. While the bracelet is transformed in this way, its inner rings slowly spin on their own.

The bracelet has 10 charges, which are only usable in its sphere form. It regains {@dice 2d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend its last charge, the sphere's spinning rings magically fold up again into the wearable bracelet. You can fold the rings back into place early as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action.

entries.name: Spells
entries: Each kind of Astral Bracelet has a different list of spells that can be cast from it. If a spell cast in this way normally requires a Material component with a cost of 100+ GP, that component is still required when casting the spell. While holding the bracelet in its sphere form, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the corresponding spells from it: {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} (3 charges), {@spell Tenser's Floating Disk|XPHB} (1 charge), {@spell Globe of Invulnerability|XPHB} (6 charges), {@spell Hallucinatory Terrain|XPHB} (4 charges), {@spell Hypnotic Pattern|XPHB} (3 charges), {@spell Protection from Energy|XPHB} (3 charges), or {@spell Otiluke's Resilient Sphere|XPHB} (4 charges).

item.name: Astral Bracelet of Heavens

item.name: Astral Bracelet
entries: Multiple variations of this item exist, as listed below:

items: {@item Astral Bracelet of Energy|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}
{@item Astral Bracelet of Heavens|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}
{@item Astral Bracelet of Hells|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}
{@item Astral Bracelet of Knowledge|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}
{@item Astral Bracelet of Omniscience|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}
{@item Astral Bracelet of Planebending|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}

entries: An Astral Bracelet is a powerful magical bangle that allows its wearer to see, commune, or otherwise manifest things using powers beyond the Material Plane. The metal and glyphs of each bracelet are related to the kind of power that it holds. You can pull the bracelet off and hold it in the palm of your hand as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. When you do, the bracelet unfurls to reveal the four inner rings collapsed within it, transforming the bracelet into an astronomical sphere. While the bracelet is transformed in this way, its inner rings slowly spin on their own.

The bracelet has 10 charges, which are only usable in its sphere form. It regains {@dice 2d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend its last charge, the sphere's spinning rings magically fold up again into the wearable bracelet. You can fold the rings back into place early as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action.

entries.name: Spells
entries: Each kind of Astral Bracelet has a different list of spells that can be cast from it. If a spell cast in this way normally requires a Material component with a cost of 100+ GP, that component is still required when casting the spell. While holding the bracelet in its sphere form, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the corresponding spells from it, as shown below, using your spellcasting ability modifier and spell save DC.

item.name: Ionbreaker, the Tempest's Reach
entries: This bow was crafted by a powerful djinni in the Stormheart: a perpetual maelstrom of wind and electricity in the Elemental Plane of Air. Lost to time, it has remained in the storm for untold centuries absorbing the volatile magic that runs rampant there. You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: A Storm Is Coming
entries: Whenever you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} while the bow is on your person, you can roll {@dice 1d10} and add the number rolled to the total.

entries.name: Fast as Lightning
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can make one attack with the bow as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entries.name: Discharge
entries: Immediately after you hit a creature with a ranged attack using the bow on your turn, the target and other creatures within 5 feet of it each take {@damage 1d8} Lightning damage. Once a creature takes Lighting damage from this property, it can't take this damage again until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Chained Conduits
entries: When you hit a target with a ranged attack using the bow, that target becomes a conduit for electricity for 1 minute. You can use a command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to cause all such conduits within 60 feet of you to magically electrify. When you do, 5-foot-wide Lines of electricity arc between all electrified conduits that are within 30 feet of each other. Each conduit takes {@damage 2d8} Lightning damage, and all other creatures that are in at least one of the Lines must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d8} Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Once a conduit has been electrified in this way, it is no longer a conduit. Once a creature or object takes Lighting damage from this property, it can't take this damage again until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Maelstrom Marksman
entries: Once on each of your turns when you miss a target with a ranged attack using the bow, you can reroll the attack roll, making the attack against a different target you can see within 30 feet of the first one. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this attack roll.

entries.name: Zephyr Step
entries: While holding the bow, you have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and can hover as long as you are within 20 feet of the ground.

entries.name: Embrace the Storm
entries: While you're attuned to the bow, you're subjected to the chaotic magic of the Stormheart whenever you take Lightning damage. When you do, roll {@dice 1d8}, using the table below to determine the resulting effect.

rows: You have {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Lightning damage until the end of your next turn.
You are thrown 20 feet in a random direction and have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
You have the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition for 1 hour.
You can't take {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} for 1 minute.
You cast {@spell Fog Cloud|XPHB} centered on a point within 5 feet of you. The spell doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB} and you can't end it early, instead lasting for {@dice 1d4} rounds.
Up to three random creatures within 10 feet of you take {@damage 1d8} Lightning damage.
Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is doubled until the end of your next turn.
You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Lightning damage until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Random Properties
entries: Ionbreaker, the Tempest's Reach has the following random properties:

items: 1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Beneficial Properties|XDMG|minor beneficial} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Major Beneficial Properties|XDMG|major beneficial} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Minor Detrimental Properties|XDMG|minor detrimental} property
1 {@table Artifact Properties; Major Detrimental Properties|XDMG|major detrimental} property

entries.name: Destroying the Bow
entries: The bow can be destroyed if it's struck by lightning in the Stormheart at the same time as it's hit by a hammer made of pure adamantine against a similarly adamantine anvil by a creature attuned to the bow. When it's destroyed, each creature within 60 feet of the bow takes {@damage 10d10} Lightning damage and {@damage 10d10} Force damage. If a creature is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} by this damage, it's immediately turned to ash and can only be revived by a {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell.

itemGroup.name: Bird of a Feather
items: Bird of a Feather (Common)|24GriffonsSaddlebag1
Bird of a Feather (Uncommon)|24GriffonsSaddlebag1

itemGroup.name: Scholar's Cap
entries: This hat carries with it the cleverness and knowledge of many previous scholars. While wearing it, you gain a bonus to Intelligence checks and Intelligence saving throws. This bonus is determined by the hat's rarity. In addition, for every 5 days you spend researching a subject, you gain knowledge as if you had been researching it for an additional number of days based on the hat's rarity: 1 day (Uncommon), 2 days (Rare), or 3 days (Very Rare).
items: Scholar's Cap +1|24GriffonsSaddlebag1
Scholar's Cap +2|24GriffonsSaddlebag1
Scholar's Cap +3|24GriffonsSaddlebag1

itemGroup.name: Dragon Edge Weapon (Generic)
items: Dragon Edge Weapon|24GriffonsSaddlebag1
+1 Dragon Edge Weapon|24GriffonsSaddlebag1
+2 Dragon Edge Weapon|24GriffonsSaddlebag1

itemGroup.name: Dragonkin Weapon
items: +1 Dragonkin Weapon|24GriffonsSaddlebag1
+2 Dragonkin Weapon|24GriffonsSaddlebag1
+3 Dragonkin Weapon|24GriffonsSaddlebag1

entries: This ammunition is covered in an alchemical concoction that ignites upon impact, casting {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in the space of the target it hits and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} in a 5-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the target for 1 minute. A creature hit by the ammunition takes {@damage 1d4} Acid damage at the start of each of its turns and has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks while the ammunition remains lit. The ammunition remains lodged in a target it hits until a creature takes a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action to remove and snuff out the ammunition. The target can't benefit from the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition while the ammunition is lodged within it. The ammunition is destroyed by the lit solution, preventing it from being recovered once fired.

This magic bow is enchanted with passive Abjuration protections. When you hit a creature with a ranged attack using this bow, the bow creates a thin barrier of protective magic that stays between you and that creature until the start of your next turn. If you hit a different creature while protected by this barrier, the barrier moves to defend you against the new target instead. While the barrier is protecting you against a creature in this way, you have {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} against that creature's ranged attacks and effects originating from it.

inherits.reqAttune: by a Creature of Good Alignment
entries: This vibrant blade carries with it some of the properties of the couatl who either forged it or the whose scales it was crafted from. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, when you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll made with this weapon, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} Poison damage and must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition and a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0 until it takes any damage or someone else uses an action to shake the target out of its stupor.

entries.name: Flight of the Couatls
entries: While you're attuned to three items with this property, you gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet. If you already have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}, it increases by 30 feet instead.

inherits.reqAttune: by a Creature of Good Alignment
entries: This wooden bow is carved to resemble a mighty couatl. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The bow has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d3 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. When you make a ranged attack against a Large or smaller creature with the bow, you can choose a limb to target and expend 1 of the bow's charges as part of the attack. On a hit, the attack deals Radiant damage instead of its normal type, and the target must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the chosen limb is pinned by radiant tethers of magic, holding it to the target's body or an adjacent surface (if any) for up to 1 minute.

Depending on the pinned limb, the target may gain the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, be unable to move, or make attack rolls with {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} (GM's choice) while the limb remains pinned in this way. To escape, the target or a creature within 5 feet of it must take an action to make a DC 15 Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check, freeing the target on a success.

entries.name: Flight of the Couatls
entries: While you're attuned to three items with this property, you gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet. If you already have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}, it increases by 30 feet instead.

These weapons are made of a scaly metal that radiates draconic elements. You have a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and it deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the same type as your Dragonborn Breath Weapon trait on a hit. In addition, while attuned to the weapon, you have a +1 bonus to your Breath Weapon's save DC.
The color of the weapon's metal changes to reflect your own natural colors as a Dragonborn once you attune to it.

These weapons are made of a scaly metal that radiates draconic elements. You have a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and it deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the same type as your Dragonborn Breath Weapon trait on a hit. In addition, while attuned to the weapon, you have a +2 bonus to your Breath Weapon's save DC.
The color of the weapon's metal changes to reflect your own natural colors as a Dragonborn once you attune to it.

These weapons are made of a scaly metal that radiates draconic elements. You have a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and it deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the same type as your Dragonborn Breath Weapon trait on a hit. In addition, while attuned to the weapon, you have a +3 bonus to your Breath Weapon's save DC.
The color of the weapon's metal changes to reflect your own natural colors as a Dragonborn once you attune to it.

A favorite among tricksters, this ammunition is intended to distract rather than wound. When you make a ranged attack roll using this ammunition and hit, the target takes no damage, and the ammunition vanishes and is destroyed on impact. In its place, an instantaneous noise of your choice erupts from the point of impact, such as the sound of books falling, a dropped coin, or the breaking of glass. You decide the noise and its volume when you fire the ammunition. The sound can be as quiet as a whisper or as loud as a scream. A creature that hears the sound can determine that it is an illusion (no action required) with a successful DC 10 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check.
This festerwood hammer has amber pustules on its end that have enriched the wood with its corrosive properties. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

In addition, this hammer has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dusk. When you hit a target with the hammer, you can expend 1 charge to magically release some of the corrosive acid onto it. If the target is Large or smaller, it takes a cumulative -1 penalty to AC, which lasts for 1 minute. If the target is Huge or Gargantuan, it takes this penalty only after you release the acid onto it for a second or third time, respectively, within 1 minute. When an affected target is hit by the hammer, it takes an extra {@damage 1d4} Acid damage for each -1 penalty to AC it has in this way.
This monumental hammer requires a Strength of 17 or higher to wield. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to use the command words, "light the forge," and cause flames to magically curl up and heat the hammer's anvil head. These flames shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. While the hammer is lit, it deals an extra {@damage 1d8} Fire damage on a hit and ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage. The flames last until you take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to use the command word, "quench," or until you drop or stow the hammer.

entries.name: Smith's Blessing
entries: You can use the hammer to cast either the Creation (level 6 version) or {@spell Fabricate|XPHB} spell as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action instead of its normal casting time. When you cast {@spell Creation|XPHB} in this way, you can use its conjured materials as raw materials for the {@spell Fabricate|XPHB} spell, although the materials still have a limited duration. Once the hammer has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn.

In addition, you know the {@spell Mending|XPHB} cantrip while you're attuned to the hammer. When you cast it to repair metal, the break or tear can be up to 5 feet in any dimension instead of 1 foot. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks using {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB} to craft or repair an object when you use the hammer's head as the anvil.

entries.name: Iron Raze
entries: The hammer deals an extra {@damage 2d8} Bludgeoning damage to Constructs and structures on a hit.

These magic weapons have been tempered to glide through the wind, doubling the normal and long ranges of their {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} property. Ranged attack rolls made with these weapons ignore air-based hindrances, such as fog and high winds, allowing them to be made without the normal {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} or not automatically miss as a result of those obstructions.

This magic ammunition is made by the elder Clerics of nature and hunting deities. {@action Attack|XPHB} rolls made with this ammunition deal no damage on a hit. When you hit a creature other than yourself with the ammunition, the target regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d4} plus your Dexterity and Wisdom modifiers (minimum +1). If the target is an ally, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll. Once it hits a creature, the ammunition dissolves into pure healing magic before it's destroyed.

This Arrow or Bolt is fletched with phoenix feathers and is magically bound to a small {@item Quiver|XPHB} or {@item Crossbow Bolt Case|XPHB}, respectively. You have a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. Once fired, the ammunition bursts into flames upon impact, dealing Fire damage on a hit instead of Piercing. At the start of your next turn, the ammunition leaves behind a small pile of ash and reappears in its container.

You can use a command word as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} before firing the ammunition to magically set it ablaze. If you fire it on that same turn, the ammunition deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Fire damage on a hit and immediately turns to ash; it turns to ash before reaching its target if fired more than 30 feet away. The ammunition reappears in its container immediately after turning to ash.

You can store up to ten other Arrows or Bolts in the small {@item Quiver|XPHB} or {@item Crossbow Bolt Case|XPHB}, respectively, but can only have one piece of Phoenix Ammunition bound to it at a time. If the ammunition is ever destroyed, it reappears in its container after 24 hours unless it was destroyed while rendered nonmagical, such as by an {@spell Antimagic Field|XPHB}.

This ammunition is etched with runes that are magically discordant to spellcasters. You have a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this piece of magic ammunition. When a creature that is {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} is hit by a piece of Runic Ammunition, that creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw it makes to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}.
Immediately after the ammunition hits a target, it becomes nonmagical.

These silvered weapons are of elven make. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with these magic weapons, which deal {@damage 2d6} damage on a hit instead of their normal damage dice. Scoring a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} with the weapon doesn't allow you to roll the attack's damage dice twice. Instead, whenever you hit a target and roll the same number on both of the weapon's damage dice, you can immediately roll {@dice 1d6} and add it to the weapon's damage dice. You continue rolling additional damage dice until you roll a number that doesn't match the previous rolls.

This silver axe can temporarily bind a creature to its corporeal form. When you hit a creature that has the Incorporeal Movement trait using the axe, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} Force damage, and it can't benefit from its Incorporeal Movement trait again until the end of its next turn.

This rose has a stem large enough to hold like the hilt of a weapon. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding it, you can cause the rose to magically grow one of the following weapons from its center: {@item Dagger|XPHB}, {@item Shortsword|XPHB}, {@item Rapier|XPHB}, or {@item Whip|XPHB}. If the hilt already has a weapon grown from its center, the existing weapon withers away and disintegrates, allowing the new one to take its place.

You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Piercing damage on a hit. While attuned to Thornpiercer, you are proficient with the weapons it grows. Each weapon option has a unique property, as detailed below.

entries.name: Dagger
entries: When you throw this weapon, only the blade is thrown from the rose hilt. Hit or miss, the blade erupts into a burst of thorns after reaching its target. The target and up to three other creatures of your choice within 10 feet of it must succeed on a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d4 + 2} Piercing damage.

entries.name: Rapier
entries: When you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll with this weapon, the blade's thorns eviscerate the inside of the target, dealing an extra 10 Piercing damage to it. If the target was a Large or smaller creature, it must also make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by the amount of damage it took from the attack. On a successful save, its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by half that amount instead. This reduction to the target's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum returns to normal when it finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Shortsword
entries: The first creature hit by this weapon on each of your turns must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 1d6} Poison damage and has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Whip
entries: When you hit a Medium or smaller creature with this weapon, you can attempt to grapple it with the {@item Whip|XPHB}. When you do, the target must succeed on a DC 17 Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition. The {@condition Grappled|XPHB} target takes {@damage 1d4} Piercing damage from the {@item Whip|XPHB}'s thorns at the start of each of its turns until it's freed.
