To escape, the target or a creature within 5 feet of it must take an action to make a DC 17 Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check, freeing the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} creature on a success. The {@item Whip|XPHB} breaks off from the hilt when you successfully grapple a target in this way, allowing you to move freely while the target remains {@condition Grappled|XPHB}.

This unique ammunition's spiraling design allows it to travel unimpaired through water. When firing the Arrow or Bolt underwater, you ignore the normal drawbacks of underwater combat. If you fire it while not underwater, the normal and long ranges for the attack are halved.

This pitch-dark piece of ammunition is composed of densely packed shadows. Attacks with it deal Psychic damage on a hit instead of Piercing. The ammunition makes no sound when it's fired or strikes a surface, leaves no discernible wounds, and vanishes immediately after hitting or missing its target.

This split metal sword arcs with electricity between its two halves. This magic weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Lightning damage on a hit. While holding the sword, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to unleash a concentrated beam of lightning from the blade in a {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} that is 60 feet long and 5 feet wide. Each creature in the {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

When you reduce a Small or larger creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} against a target with this weapon, you can create a harmless sensory effect or illusion, as if by the {@spell Prestidigitation|XPHB} spell, as part of that attack. The effect can appear on the target of the attack or in the area immediately surrounding it.

This deep red weapon was made by a vengeful craftsman. You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. If the attack is made against a creature who dealt damage to you since the end of your last turn, this bonus increases to +2 instead.

This weapon is made from the harvested remains of a fallen dragon. With a properly reclaimed claw or tooth, the resource can be forged into a simple Piercing or Slashing weapon whose latent draconic powers can appear in battle.

The first time you hit a creature on each of your turns with an attack roll you had {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on using this weapon, its draconic magic comes forth to deal an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of a type corresponding to the original dragon's breath weapon. Weapons made from the claws or teeth of dragons change in rarity and power depending on the original dragon's age.

Young dragons' bones aren't as powerful as older ones and create Uncommon weapons. Adult dragons can produce Rare weapons, and ancient dragons can create Very Rare weapons, each granting a bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls determined by the weapon's rarity.

This weapon is made from the harvested remains of a fallen dragon. With a properly reclaimed claw or tooth, the resource can be forged into a simple Piercing or Slashing weapon whose latent draconic powers can appear in battle.

The first time you hit a creature on each of your turns with an attack roll you had {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on using this weapon, its draconic magic comes forth to deal an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of a type corresponding to the original dragon's breath weapon. Weapons made from the claws or teeth of dragons change in rarity and power depending on the original dragon's age.

Young dragons' bones aren't as powerful as older ones and create Uncommon weapons. Adult dragons can produce Rare weapons, and ancient dragons can create Very Rare weapons, each granting a bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls determined by the weapon's rarity.

This weapon is made from the harvested remains of a fallen dragon. With a properly reclaimed claw or tooth, the resource can be forged into a simple Piercing or Slashing weapon whose latent draconic powers can appear in battle.

The first time you hit a creature on each of your turns with an attack roll you had {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on using this weapon, its draconic magic comes forth to deal an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of a type corresponding to the original dragon's breath weapon. Weapons made from the claws or teeth of dragons change in rarity and power depending on the original dragon's age.

Young dragons' bones aren't as powerful as older ones and create Uncommon weapons. Adult dragons can produce Rare weapons, and ancient dragons can create Very Rare weapons, each granting a bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls determined by the weapon's rarity.

This armor absorbs poison and allows you to traverse webbed areas more easily. While you're wearing the armor, you can't be caught in webs of any sort and can move through webs as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. In addition, when you take Poison damage while wearing this armor, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reduce the damage by an amount equal to {@dice 1d8} plus your Constitution modifier.

This weapon was forged using the preserved remains of an ancient mimic. You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can use a command word to magically reshape it into any kind of Melee weapon with which you're proficient that doesn't have the Heavy or Reach property.

When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can attempt to grapple the target with it (no action required), channeling the weapon's natural pseudopod form. The target must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition. Until the grapple ends, you can't attack or grapple another creature with the weapon. Releasing the weapon ends the condition on the target.

entries.name: Pseuperpod
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can make a melee attack roll against a creature within 30 feet of you using the weapon. The weapon stretches toward the target as a pseudopod, ignoring Half and {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover}, and deals {@damage 6d8} Bludgeoning damage on a hit. After the attack, the weapon retracts back into its previous form. If you attempt to grapple the target with the attack, the save DC for the Strength saving throw increases to 15. On a failed save, the target is pulled up to 15 feet closer to you before being released from the weapon's grasp. If the target failed the save by 5 or more, it is instead pulled up to 30 feet closer to you and remains {@condition Grappled|XPHB}. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until 12 hours have passed.

entries.name: Limb Replacement
entries: If you're missing an arm or forearm, you can graft the weapon to yourself in its place. If you do, the weapon changes and reforms to match the missing limb. You can add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to any Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check you make using the limb; your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} is instead doubled for such checks if you already have proficiency in the {@skill Athletics|XPHB} skill. You can alter the limb's form to turn it into a weapon or revert it to match the missing limb as needed (no action required).

trait.name: Blizzard Stalker
entries: Inclement weather (such as fog, snow or wind) doesn't impede the wraith's vision, and it has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks it makes in such conditions.

trait.name: Incorporeal Movement
entries: The wraith can move through other creatures and objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. It takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

action.name: Life Drain
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6} (with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} during the first round of each combat), reach 5 ft. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d8 + 3}) Necrotic damage plus 16 ({@damage 3d10}) Cold damage. If the target is a creature, its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum decreases by an amount equal to the Necrotic damage taken.

action.name: Freezing Gale {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 14}, each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 28 ({@damage 8d6}) Cold damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

action.name: Create Walker (2/Day)
entries: The wraith targets a Humanoid corpse within 10 feet of itself that died from either Cold damage or exposure to extreme cold. The target's remains animate as either a Skeleton or a Zombie (depending on the state of the corpse, as determined by the GM) that has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Cold and Necrotic damage and shares the wraith's Blizzard Stalker trait. The animated creature is under the wraith's control. The wraith can have no more than ten animated creatures under its control at a time.

trait.name: Ice Walk
entries: The bulette can move across and climb icy surfaces without needing to make an ability check. Additionally, {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} composed of ice or snow doesn't cost it extra movement.

trait.name: Steam Aura
entries: At the end of each of the bulette's turns, each creature of the bulette's choice in a 5-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the bulette takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Fire damage unless the bulette has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

trait.name: Tunneler
entries: The bulette can burrow through solid ice and leaves a slick, 10-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The bulette makes two Bite attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, reach 5 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d12 + 4}) Piercing damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Fire damage.

action.name: Steam Eruption {@recharge 5}
entries: If the bulette is burrowed no more than 20 feet deep in solid ice, it spends 5 feet of movement to burrow directly upward, emerging from the ice and jumping to a space within 5 feet of where it emerged. {@actSave dex} {@dc 15}, each creature in the space where the bulette emerged or its destination space. {@actSaveFail} 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Fire damage, and the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} The target is pushed to the nearest unoccupied space within 5 feet of where the bulette emerged or its destination space.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The gargoyle doesn't provoke an Opportunity Attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gargoyle makes two attacks, using Claw or Ice Dart in any combination.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d10 + 2}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Ice Dart
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 4}, range 30/60 ft. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d4 + 2}) Piercing damage.

trait.name: Adapted
entries: The weremammoth can tolerate temperatures of 0 degrees Fahrenheit or lower without being subjected to the effects of extreme cold.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The weremammoth makes three attacks, using Spear or Trample in any combination. It can replace one attack with a Gore attack.

action.name: Gore (Mammoth or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 5 ft. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d8 + 7}) Piercing damage. If the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave con} {@dc 12}. {@actSaveFail} The target is cursed. If the cursed target drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it instead becomes a Weremammoth under the GM's control and has 10 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to this weremammoth's curse for 24 hours.

action.name: Spear (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 10}, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d6 + 7}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Trample (Mammoth or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 10}, reach 5 ft. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d10 + 7}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature and the weremammoth moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

bonus.name: Shape-Shift
entries: The werewolf shape-shifts into a Large mammoth-humanoid hybrid or a Huge mammoth, or it returns to its true humanoid form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

monster.name: Risen Sporehusk
languages: understands Common plus one other language it knew but can't speak

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The sporehusk regains 5 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of its turn. If the sporehusk takes Fire or Radiant damage, this trait doesn't function on the sporehusk's next turn. The sporehusk dies only if it starts its turn with 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and doesn't regenerate.

trait.name: Sunlight Weakness
entries: While in sunlight, the sporehusk has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@dice D20} Tests.

action.name: Grab
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 11}).

bonus.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. (must target a creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the sporehusk). {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) Piercing damage.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: The sporehusk regains 5 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of each of its turns. If it takes Fire or Radiant damage, this trait doesn't function on the sporehusk's next turn. The sporehusk dies only if it starts its turn with 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and doesn't regenerate.

trait.name: Sunlight Weakness
entries: While in sunlight, the sporehusk has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@dice D20} Tests.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The scout makes two attacks, using Shortsword and Longbow in any combination.

action.name: Grab
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 ft. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d6 + 1}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 11}).

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 ft. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d6 + 1}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 3}, range 150/600 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d8 + 1}) Piercing damage.

bonus.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 ft. (must target a creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the sporehusk). {@h}5 ({@damage 1d8 + 1}) Piercing damage.

trait.name: Death Flash
entries: The invisiboar emits a bright flash of light when it dies. {@actSave con} {@dc 13}, each creature in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the invisiboar. {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

trait.name: Invisible
entries: The invisiboar always has the Invisible condition unless it's in the area of an {@spell Antimagic Field|XPHB} spell or similar effect.

action.name: Gore
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) Slashing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature and the boar moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Piercing damage and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

action.name: Beak
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 8}, reach 15 ft. {@h}21 ({@damage 3d10 + 5}) Slashing damage. If the swordbeak moved 30+ feet straight toward the target immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The winged wretch makes two Rend attacks.

action.name: Rend
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d8 + 1}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The winged wretch makes three Rend attacks.

action.name: Rend
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 6}, reach 5 ft. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Thunderous Shriek {@recharge 5}
entries: The winged wretch emits a cacophonous wave of energy. {@actSave con} {@dc 12}, each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 17 ({@damage 5d6}) Thunder damage, and the target has the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Dendallen casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 17}):

spellcasting.name: Protective Magic (3/Day)
headerEntries: Dendallen casts {@spell Counterspell|XPHB} or {@spell Shield|XPHB} in response to the spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Dendallen makes four attacks, using Staff or Chilling Ray in any combination. He can (A) replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting to cast {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB} or (B) replace two attacks with a use of Spellcasting to cast {@spell Blight|XPHB} (level 5 version).

action.name: Staff
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 9}, reach 5 ft. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d6 + 5}) Force damage. If the target is a creature, it is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave con} {@dc 17}. {@actSaveFail} 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Necrotic damage, and the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on any ability check or saving throw that uses Strength or Constitution. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

action.name: Chilling Ray
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 9}, range 120 ft. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d8}) Cold damage.

monster.name: Spirit of Dendallen
languages: Common plus three other languages; telepathy 120 ft.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The spirit casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 21}):

spellcasting.name: Protective Magic
headerEntries: The spirit casts {@spell Counterspell|XPHB} or {@spell Shield|XPHB} in response to the spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

trait.name: Attuned Thrall
entries: {@actSave cha} {@dc 21}, the creature that attuned to three Remnants of Dendallen (if the creature has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition or can't be possessed, it can still be affected, but it makes its saves against this effect with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}). {@actSaveFail} The target has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition, becoming the host to the Spirit of Dendallen. On the spirit's turn, it can issue verbal or telepathic commands to the target (no action required). The target does its best to obey on its turn. If it completes an order and doesn't receive further direction from the spirit, it acts and moves as it likes, seemingly unaware of or unbothered by the presence of the spirit. The spirit can also command the target to take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} but must take its own {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to do so.

The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect and dismissing the spirit on a success. The DC for this save decreases when the spirit drops to certain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} thresholds, reducing by 1 at 150 HP ({@dc 20}), 100 HP ({@dc 19}), and 50 HP ({@dc 18}).

trait.name: Immutable Form
entries: The spirit can't shape-shift.

trait.name: Incorporeal Tether
entries: The spirit can't move on its own. Instead, as the spirit's host moves, the spirit is moved with it (remaining centered on the host), even through other creatures and objects. The spirit can occupy another creature's space and vice versa.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the spirit fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The spirit has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Persistent Tendrils
entries: If the spirit doesn't have any tendrils at the end of its turn, one Tendril of Dendallen is created, as if by the Create Tendrils action.

trait.name: Unstable Conduit
entries: If the spirit's host dies, the spirit is destroyed in an explosion of necromantic energy, and the host is disintegrated into gray dust, leaving behind any Remnants of Dendallen it was wearing or carrying. The host can be revived only by a {@spell True Resurrection|XPHB} or a {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell. {@actSave dex} {@dc 16}, each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the host. {@actSaveFail} 33 ({@damage 6d10}) Necrotic damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The spirit makes two attacks, using Slam or Decaying Ray in any combination. It can (A) replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting to cast {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB} (level 2 version) or (B) replace two attacks with a use of Spellcasting to cast {@spell Blight|XPHB} (level 9 version).

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 13}, reach 10 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) Force damage plus 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Acid damage.

action.name: Decaying Ray
entries: {@atkr r} {@hit 13}, range 120 ft. {@h}19 ({@damage 3d8 + 6}) Necrotic damage. If the target is a creature, it is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave con} {@dc 21}. {@actSaveFail} The target gains 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, taking 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Necrotic damage on a failure or losing all {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels caused by this effect on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

action.name: Create Tendrils {@recharge 5}
entries: The spirit creates {@dice 1d4 + 2} Tendrils of Dendallen, each appearing in unoccupied spaces the spirit can see on solid surfaces within 150 feet of itself. The spirit can have no more than six tendrils at a time. The tendrils take their turns immediately after the spirit on the same {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, and they obey the spirit. A tendril remains for 1 minute or until it's reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

legendary.name: Accursed Magic
entries: The spirit uses Spellcasting to cast {@spell Bestow Curse|XPHB}, {@spell False Life|XPHB} (level 3 version), or {@spell Hold Person|XPHB} (level 3 version). The spirit can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

legendary.name: Command Tendrils
entries: Up to two Tendrils of Dendallen the spirit can see each either make a Grasp attack or use {@spell Teleport|XPHB}. The spirit can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

legendary.name: Sweep
entries: The spirit sweeps out with its massive arm. {@actSave dex} {@dc 19}, each creature in a 20-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 23 ({@damage 3d12 + 4}) Force damage, and the target is pushed up to 15 feet away from the spirit and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

action.name: Immovable
entries: The tendril can't move or be moved except via teleportation or interplanar travel.

action.name: Immutable Form
entries: The tendril can't shape-shift.

action.name: Necromantic Demise
entries: The tendril disappears if the Spirit of Dendallen is destroyed or dismissed.

action.name: Will of Dendallen
entries: The tendril has a telepathic link with the spirit and the spirit's host while it's on the same plane of existence as them. The tendril doesn't provide cover to enemies against attacks and effects that pass through its space.

action.name: Grasp
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7}, reach 10 ft. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d10 + 5}) Force damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Acid damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 15}) and is pulled up to 5 feet toward the tendril. The tendril can have only one target {@condition Grappled|XPHB} in this way at a time.

action.name: Fling
entries: The tendril throws a Medium or smaller creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by it to a space it can see within 60 feet of itself that isn't in the air. {@actSave dex} {@dc 15}, the creature thrown and each creature in the destination space. {@actSaveFail} 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

action.name: Teleport
entries: The tendril teleports up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space it can see on a solid surface.

monsterFluff.name: Invisiboar
entries: Native to the jungles of Elsath, invisiboars are literally invisible boars. It's largely believed that these strange pigs owe their existence to the wild magic that flows through the jungles of the world, and the fact that so many of them are found near sites of permanent or programmed illusions lends further credence to that theory.

Invisiboars are consummate hunters who use their innate invisibility to stalk their prey before viciously charging it. When pitted against tougher combatants, they utilize hit and run tactics that let them charge the same target repeatedly.
When invisiboars are killed, the magic that conceals them "bursts," blinding creatures who witness the burst.

monsterFluff.name: Nicrone Dendallen
entries: Nicrone Dendallen is a human wizard whose passion for self-improvement has become an obsession, focused on removing the inherent limits of his own mortality. His focus on gaining power through sacrifice at any cost attracts dedicated sycophants. Their beliefs make them eager to recklessly endanger their lives for the chance to earn powerful rewards from his hand.
Dedicated and tenacious followers of Dendallen are given tokens of his appreciation: terrifying items made by Dendallen himself, each enchanted with a grotesque balance of strength and consequence.

While Dendallen is a master of the necromantic arts, his frustration with the limits of his mortal body makes him scoff at those who pursue longevity through undeath's tenuous hold on existence. Only under extreme circumstances—or the promise of extreme rewards—is he willing to work with undead creatures or the magic that enables them.

entries.name: Teachings of Dendallen
entries: Dendallen's belief that self-sacrifice leads to self-improvement is a fervent mantra held by his followers. In general, those that subscribe to Dendallen's teachings believe that in order to truly become greater, you must be willing to let go of the mortal trappings that bind you. The major tenets of Dendallen are as follows:

entries.name: Embrace Self-Sacrifice
entries: To forget the familiar as a price for greatness is admirable.

entries.name: Conquer your Limits
entries: To become more powerful is to embrace the purpose of life.

entries.name: Enforce your Will
entries: To conquer all obstacles in your path is to ensure success.

entries.name: Followers of Dendallen
entries: Use the {@creature Cultist|XPHB} and {@creature Cult Fanatic|XPHB} statistics for followers of Dendallen. Dendallen's more powerful followers can use other humanoid stat blocks that fit your setting and party level. These leaders are groomed to be more formidable than those deemed undeserving, unproven, or mediocre.

Typically, these trusted followers of Dendallen will have overt visual ailments or symptoms of disease, but will be notably more powerful because of it. Elite followers such as this will occasionally wield a {@area Remnant of Dendallen|item-remnant|x}, bestowed unto them after they complete a dangerous or otherwise notable feat of faith, loyalty and self-sacrifice. Upon defeating such a creature, your party can take the {@area Remnant|item-remnant|x} for themselves. The particular {@area Remnant|item-remnant|x} each follower wields is up to you, but should be scaled relative to the creature's challenge rating.

entries.name: Slaying Nicrone Dendallen
entries: Killing Nicrone allows for his spirit to return at a later time. His final mortal words should capture his beliefs and foreshadow his return, such as "Giving up your weaknesses grants power beyond compare!"

entries.name: Attuning to the Remnants
entries: Attuning to three Remnants of Dendallen is a gift as well as a curse. While a creature is attuned to three of these items, it can freely attune to other Remnants of Dendallen. When it does, its attunement to another Remnant ends, as well as its curse.

If Dendallen is dead, being attuned to these items activates a secondary effect once you reach level 15 (or 14 if there are 5 players in your group, etc.). When this happens, the attuned creature's cursed body becomes strong enough to handle the intensity of channeling the {@creature Spirit of Dendallen|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}. The event is involuntary, and happens either at the end of a long rest or at your discretion.

entries.name: The Remnants of Dendallen
entries: Items created by {@creature Nicrone Dendallen|24GriffonsSaddlebag1|Dendallen} are called Remnants of Dendallen and double as vessels to store a portion of his spirit. At the time of his death, these Remnants can be scattered around the planes of existence or collected by his remaining devout followers: whatever makes the most sense for your campaign. Any item that bears Dendallen's name in its title is considered to be a Remnant.

Remnants are laced with necromancy magic that forces creatures to sacrifice a part of themselves in order to use them. The sacrifices, while potentially reversible, are more than just skin-or-bone-deep. As curses take hold, they begin to gradually provide strength and vitality to the lingering spirit of Dendallen trapped inside them.

If a creature attuned to at least one Remnant is in the presence of another Remnant that isn't attuned to by someone else, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 20} Wisdom saving throw or become obsessed with it and do anything within its power to take and attune to that Remnant before another creature is able to. The obsession lasts until the creature is unable to see or touch the Remnant for at least 1 hour or until it's targeted by either a {@spell greater restoration} or {@spell remove curse} spell.

entries.name: Dendallen's Return
entries: When the {@creature Spirit of Dendallen|24GriffonsSaddlebag1} is channeled by a level 15 creature attuned to 3 of his Remnants, they become momentarily frozen in place. While unmoving, the creature's body begins to overflow in a torrent of green arcane and necrotic energy that spills forth from their back. The energy coalesces above them to form the towering, spectral avatar of Nicrone Dendallen's returned form: the {@creature Spirit of Dendallen|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}. Its hulking, necromantically-charged form looms over the hosted creature and drips with ooze-like green necrotic ectoplasm.

The scraps of Nicrone Dendallen's spirit that were anchored in his Remnants combine to form the new spirit. These once-weak shards have been empowered as the attuned creature has sacrificed aspects of themselves through the items' various curses. Removing the worn Remnants from the host (even forcefully) fails to destroy or dispel the {@creature Spirit of Dendallen|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}.

The spirit's appearance is that of a huge, muscular lich made of spectral miasma. Depending on the Remnants that the hosted creature has attuned to, the spirit's appearance may change to reflect the host's Remnants. Regardless of appearance, the spirit's abilities and power are unchanged.

Dendallen was an egotistical and jealous wizard in life, and death has not changed that. He despises other spellcasters that refuse to submit to him, especially those who deal significant damage to his spirit or to its host. However, Dendallen is also paranoid and desperate to retain his tenuous foothold on this plane, so his spirit will hoard its higher-level spell slots unless it is near defeat.

entries.name: Resilient Thrall
entries: Once a creature attunes to three Remnants of Dendallen, nothing short of a {@spell wish} spell can break their attunement to the items. However, once the {@creature Spirit of Dendallen|24GriffonsSaddlebag1} has been summoned, targeting the host with greater restoration, remove curse, the {@i break enchantment} effect of {@spell dispel evil and good} or other applicable spells of 5th level or greater grants the host advantage on their next saving throw to resist Dendallen's thrall. Likewise, any game effects that end possession or the {@condition charmed} condition don't end the spirit's thrall over its host, but do grant the host advantage on their next saving throw against the spirit's control.

entries.name: Personal Connections
entries: Characters who share a meaningful personal connection with Dendallen's host can attempt to assist in their struggle against the thrall of Dendallen by reminding them of shared memories, such as telling inspiring stories or making personal pleas. At their discretion, you can choose to reward these attempts by granting the host inspiration to use on their saves against Dendallen's control.

entries.name: Psychic Roots
entries: After 1 minute of the initial emergence of the {@creature Spirit of Dendallen|24GriffonsSaddlebag1}, the hosted creature suffers disadvantage on all saving throws it makes to resist the spirit's Attuned Thrall ability. Once an additional minute has passed, the host becomes possessed by Dendallen, as described in the {@i Victory or Defeat} section below.

entries.name: Undead Nature
entries: The spirit of Dendallen doesn't require air, food, drink, or sleep.

entries.name: Magical Tethers
entries: When the spirit of Dendallen is destroyed, all Remnants of Dendallen currently on that plane of existence decay into a gray goo and are destroyed, and any curse afflicting a creature from a Remnant is ended.

entries.name: Victory or Defeat
entries: If the spirit of Dendallen is defeated, the host creature gains four levels of exhaustion. In addition, all Remnants of Dendallen on the current plane of existence, even those not attuned to by the host, turn to mush and are destroyed as the magic that anchored Dendallen's spirit is dispelled. All of the Remnants' effects and curses that were affecting the creature end.

If the spirit is victorious in combat and maintains its control over the host, it can spend 1 minute solidifying its ownership and possess the host. Once the creature is fully possessed in this way, the spirit of Dendallen can stay within the creature's body and command it indefinitely. The host creature shows no outward signs of the possessing spirit. While possessed in this way, the host automatically fails all saving throws against the spirit's thrall.

While Dendallen is proud of his necromantic achievements, his step into undeath is a shameful setback. He will do whatever it takes to remain within the possessed creature and maintain his sense of life unless absolutely necessary. The spirit can reemerge from the possessed creature as an action. When this happens, the possessed creature can resume attempting saving throws to maintain its free will, but they suffer disadvantage on each of these attempts. Use the spirit of Dendallen's stat block and the possessed creature's statistics for these saving throws.

monsterFluff.name: Sporehusk
entries: The creatures that fall victim to the Lightspore's influence become sporehusks: empty shells of their former selves whose only purpose is to nourish the great Lightspore at the center of the Festerwood.

Sporehusks will not attack unless provoked. Their natural preference is to shamble their way toward the Lightspore, but they will defend themselves if attacked or otherwise prevented from reaching their destination.

When a creature dies while it has at least one Spore Counter, it rises as a sporehusk after 24 hours if it wasn't one already. Such a creature either uses the default {@creature Risen Sporehusk|24GriffonsSaddlebag1} stat block or has the Sporehusk Template applied to its statistics (GM's discretion).

entries.name: Sporehusk Template
entries: Any creature made from living tissue can become a sporehusk, including Undead risen from such creatures. When a creature becomes a sporehusk and has this template applied to it, it retains all its statistics except as noted below.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: The sporehusk is an Undead.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: The sporehusk's alignment is Neutral Evil. The sporehusk loses all motivations, bonds, and loyalties it had in its previous life.

entries.name: Armor Class
entries: The sporehusk's {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} becomes 12 plus its Dexterity modifier unless it was already higher.

entries.name: Hit Points
entries: The sporehusk gains 2 Hit Dice, increasing its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum accordingly.

entries.name: Ability Scores
entries: The sporehusk's Strength and Constitution scores increase by 2, and its Dexterity, Wisdom, and Charisma are reduced by 2 (to a minimum of 1). Its Intelligence score becomes 6 unless it is already lower.

entries.name: Skills
entries: The sporehusk loses any skill proficiencies it has.

entries.name: Vulnerabilities
entries: The sporehusk has {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Fire and Radiant damage.

entries.name: Resistances
entries: The sporehusk has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage.

entries.name: Immunities
entries: The sporehusk has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} conditions.

entries.name: Senses
entries: The sporehusk has {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 15 feet and {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet, unless it has a superior {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} or {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} range, respectively.

entries.name: Languages
entries: The sporehusk understands Common plus one other language it knew (if any) but can't speak.

entries.name: New Trait:
entries: Regeneration. The sporehusk regains 5 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of each of its turns. If the sporehusk takes Fire or Radiant damage, this trait doesn't function on the sporehusk's next turn. The sporehusk dies only if it starts its turn with 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and doesn't regenerate.

entries.name: New Trait:
entries: Sunlight Weakness. While in sunlight, the sporehusk has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests}.

entries.name: New Action:
entries: Grab. When the sporehusk uses its arms to deal damage with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, the attack deals Bludgeoning damage equal to {@dice 1d6} plus the sporehusk's Strength modifier. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape DC 10 plus the sporehusk's Strength modifier).

entries.name: New Bonus Action:
entries: Bite. While grappling a creature, the sporehusk can use its teeth to deal damage with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} against the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} target. On a hit, the attack deals Piercing damage equal to {@dice 1d8} plus the sporehusk's Strength modifier.

entries.name: REMINDER: CHANGING ABILITY SCORES
entries: When you change a creature's ability scores, remember to adjust any bonuses for attack and damage rolls, as well as its AC, {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, saving throws, and skills.

entries.name: Example Sporehusk
entries: As an example, the Sporehusk Template has been applied to a {@creature Scout|XMM}, as shown in the {@creature Scout Sporehusk|24GriffonsSaddlebag1} stat block.

entries.name: Saving Sporehusks
entries: A sporehusk subjected to 8 or more hours of sunlight each day for 5 consecutive days is cured of its affliction and becomes a nonmagical corpse. If it is later revived, it has no memory of its time spent as a sporehusk.
If a sporehusk is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and later revived, the creature returns to its normal faculties with 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level and two Spore Counters.

monsterFluff.name: Swordbeak
entries: As the name suggests, swordbeaks are huge birds with 10-foot-long razor-sharp beaks. These beaks are capable of punching through stone and steel. Voracious carnivores, they often attack large creatures, and even humanoids, in grasslands, hillsides, coasts, and other open areas.

Swordbeak nests are usually littered with gold, gems, and other valuables that their humanoid prey were carrying at their time of death. Appreciative of shiny objects, swordbeaks defend their collected treasures almost as fiercely as they do a clutch of eggs.

monsterFluff.name: Winged Wretch
entries: Winged wretches are swarming bat-humanoid hybrids native to subterranean realms and tropical jungles. Although they resemble certain shapechangers and vampires, they are not blood-drinkers. Regardless, they are cruel creatures who delight in the torture of weaker prey.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Glacier
entries: {@i Overpower Foes with the Might of a Blizzard and Armor of Ice}

Born from the frigid and daunting Everglacier, barbarians who follow Path of the Glacier have learned to harbor the resolute stillness, patience, and unwavering determination and destruction of the icy landscape. These warriors transform their bodies into powerful moving bunkers that are as difficult to take down as they are to escape from.

Barbarians that follow this path are often trained hunters who enjoy the patient stalking of prey. They thrive on the peril they impose on their fleeing targets, and take special pride in their slow-and-steady approach.

subclassFeature.name: Frostbite
entries: You can unleash the unforgiving cold inside of you with your attacks. Once on each of your turns while your Rage is active, you can choose to release a burst of sheer cold when you hit a target with a Strength-based attack. When you do, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} Cold damage from the attack. This extra damage increases by {@dice 1d6} when you reach Barbarian levels 9 ({@dice 2d6}) and 16 ({@dice 3d6}).
When a creature takes Cold damage in this way, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Permafrost
entries: Your skin becomes icy to the touch, outwardly manifesting the cold and patient well of power within you. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Frosted Flesh
entries: While you aren't wearing any armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

entries.name: Rage Extension
entries: Whenever your Rage would end while you don't have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition, you can choose to extend it (no action required). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Resistance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage.

subclassFeature.name: Cold Fortress
entries: You learn to further channel the icy resilience within you. When you enter your Rage and aren't wearing any armor, your skin becomes shielded by a protective layer of frost. This frost grants you a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d12} plus your Constitution modifier.

You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your following turns while your Rage is active to expend a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} and gain these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} again.

subclassFeature.name: Deep Sleep
entries: You can choose to enter a deep state of hibernation when you take a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. While resting in this way, you're cold to the touch and appear dead to all outward inspection and to spells or other magical effects used to determine your status. Noise and other distractions fail to wake you.

You awaken from this rest after 6 hours, when you take damage, or when a creature uses its action to slap you awake. If you finish a continuous 6 hours of sleep in this way, you gain the benefits of finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, and you gain a special pool of {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, which lasts until the end of your next {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} and can be used as normal. This pool has a number of Barbarian {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} in it equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1 {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die}).

subclassFeature.name: Avalanche Stomp
entries: You become as mighty as an unstoppable glacier. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can stomp the ground to send out a tremor. Each creature of your choice in a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, a creature takes Bludgeoning damage equal to {@dice 3d6} plus your Strength modifier, and it has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

subclassFeature.name: Runetagger
entries: {@i Use Powerful Runes to Weaken Enemies and Escape from Harm}

You concentrate your efforts on perfecting your art, developing special marks to claim and cripple your targets. Rebel leaders, artists, spies, and other members of the political underworld might belong to this archetype. Originally a tactic for subterfuge and assassination for denizens of the Festerwood, those who employ these powerful works of art are famously difficult to pin down. The magical marks left behind become a calling card for any Runetagger, and as their strength and notoriety grow from the deeds that they commit (both good and evil), so too does the renown for their art.

subclassFeature.name: Impressionist
entries: You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} with {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB} and {@item Painter's Supplies|XPHB}, and you're never without your brushes. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can create either {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB} or {@item Painter's Supplies|XPHB} in one of your free hands. When you do, you can also create either {@item Ink|XPHB} (if you created {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB}) or a {@item Vial|XPHB} of paint (if you created {@item Painter's Supplies|XPHB}); the ink or paint can be any color of your choice. Items created in this way immediately vanish if they are ever not on your person or if you take this action again.

In addition, whenever you finish making a mark or rune with ink or paint, you can imbue it with the effect of the {@spell Message|XPHB} spell. When you do, the mark is visibly magical and sheds {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} in a 5-foot radius. A creature that touches the glowing mark telepathically hears the message you imbued in it. Once the message is heard, the mark loses these properties and becomes nonmagical.

subclassFeature.name: Runes
entries: You have 4 Rune Points, which fuel various magical effects from this subclass. When you hit a creature with a melee attack using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can spend 1 Rune Point to mark that creature with a magical rune in a flourish of paint and weaponry. These runes remain on a marked creature for up to 1 minute. A rune vanishes early if its magic fades (as described below) or if a creature takes an action to wipe it off. You regain all spent Rune Points when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
When you mark a creature with this feature, you can choose to leave one of the following runes.

entries.name: Cryos
entries: The marked creature can't take {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} until the start of your next turn, at which point the rune's magic fades.

entries.name: Hexxus
entries: When the marked creature succeeds on an ability check or attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to activate the rune; the marked creature must be within 60 feet of you and you must be able to see it. When you activate the rune, the creature subtracts {@dice 1d6} from its roll, potentially turning the success into a failure. Once the rune has been activated, its magic fades.

entries.name: Locus
entries: The next attack roll made against the marked creature has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}, and if that attack hits, it deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Acid damage. The rune's magic fades after the next attack roll against the creature hits or misses.

subclassFeature.name: Escape Artist
entries: You become slippery and evasive, capable of worming out of even the tightest holds. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks and saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions. When you have one of those conditions, you can attempt to escape as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} if doing so would normally require an action.

In addition, while you're within 5 feet of a solid surface (such as the ground or a wall), you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and spend 1 Rune Point to magically create a rune on that surface. When you do, you have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition for 10 minutes. The condition ends early immediately after you make an attack roll, deal damage, cast a spell, or leave the space where you created the rune.

subclassFeature.name: Resourceful
entries: Your mastery over the arts is lauded by friends and feared by foes. Whenever you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you regain spent Rune Points until you have two if you have fewer than that. In addition, whenever you deal Sneak Attack damage and add one of your Cunning Strike effects, you can spend 1 or more Rune Points to reduce the number of Sneak Attack damage dice you must forgo by one for each Rune Point spent.

subclassFeature.name: Lead Paint
entries: You're able to apply more ink or paint whenever you leave a rune, using it to expose weak points in a target's defense and guide your strikes. Whenever you hit a creature marked with one of your runes or spend a Rune Point to mark it with one of your runes as part of that attack, you don't need {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll or an ally within 5 feet of the target to use your Sneak Attack against it; you still can't use your Sneak Attack if you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll. When you do, your Sneak Attack damage increases by {@damage 2d6} damage for the attack.

subclassFeature.name: Frost Sorcery
entries: {@i Summon Storms and Sheets of Slick Ice to Control the Battlefield}

Your magic is created by pieces of the Everheart, the center and driving force behind the expanding Everglacier wastelands. This power within you might be passed down through ancestors who protected the glacier's magical core, or it might have been forced upon you through a chance encounter with the enchanted ice itself. Regardless of the source of your power, you are a creature of cold incarnate.

subclassFeature.name: Frost Spells
entries: When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Frost Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Frost Spells
rows: {@spell Blindness/Deafness|XPHB}, {@spell Ice Knife|XPHB}, {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}, {@spell Sleep|XPHB}
{@spell Cone of Cold|XPHB}, {@spell Conjure Elemental|XPHB} (Cold damage only)

subclassFeature.name: Create Ice
entries: While touching a solid surface (such as the ground or a wall), you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to magically create icy terrain on up to five 5-foot squares, starting with the surface you're touching. Each square covers a solid surface and must be contiguous with at least one other square. The ice-covered squares are {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} and last until the end of your next turn.
You can spend 1 or more Sorcery Points when you take this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to freeze an additional five contiguous squares for each Sorcery Point spent.

subclassFeature.name: Frozen Body
entries: Your skin takes on a faintly ice-like, crystalline glow. Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by 3, and it increases by 1 whenever you gain another Sorcerer level.
In addition, {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} composed of ice or snow doesn't cost you extra movement, and when you walk on ice, you only spend 1 foot of movement for every 2 feet you move.

subclassFeature.name: Cold-Hearted
entries: Your icy core manifests outwardly, granting you the following benefits.

entries.name: Cryomancy
entries: When you deal Cold damage to a Large or smaller creature with a spell, you can spend 1 or more Sorcery Points to attempt to freeze that creature in place. When you do, that creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 15 feet for each Sorcery Point you spend; this reduction lasts until the end of the creature's next turn. If you reduce a creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} to 0 in this way, it takes {@damage 2d6} Cold damage.

entries.name: Freeze Water
entries: You can now use your Create Ice feature to turn water into icy terrain, in addition to solid surfaces. When you do, the ice extends 5 feet deep, freezing up to a 5-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}. These {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cubes} can support Large or smaller creatures; if a Huge or larger creature moves onto one of these {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cubes}, it reverts to its water form early.

entries.name: Resistance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage.

subclassFeature.name: Flash Freeze
entries: You're most confident on the ice. Your movement doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} while you're moving on icy terrain.

In addition, when a creature you can see within 5 feet of yourself hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to deal Cold damage to the attacker equal to your Charisma modifier plus half your Sorcerer level (round down). When you do, you can use your Create Ice feature as part of that {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Frozen Soul
entries: You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Cold damage and {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage.

In addition, you always have the {@spell Wall of Ice|XPHB} spell prepared, and you can cast it without a Material component. You can also cast it once without a spell slot; if you choose for its form to be flat when cast in this way, the panels of ice don't need to be contiguous with one another. Once you use this feature to cast the spell without a spell slot, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Oath of the Hearth
entries: {@i Protect Your Friends with Your Warmth and Incinerate Those Who Would Harm Them}

The Oath of the Hearth originates from the city of the same name, whose tragic downfall has been all but lost to history over centuries. These Paladins swore an oath to protect this tropical, sunlit city in the name of their celestial guardians. Until the end, they fulfilled their oaths. You may swear this oath because you have an age-old connection to the city through your bloodline, or you may have established a connection to the city's principles through stories or interaction with a related celestial being. Regardless of your reasons for taking it, those who swear this oath adorn their armor with symbols of community, fire, and the sun to reflect their commitment to the city and the lives that were led there.
These Paladins share the following tenets:

items: Light the way to progress and salvation.
Offer guidance and respite to those that need it.
Turn those that would douse the peoples' flames to ash.

subclassFeature.name: Oath of the Hearth Spells
entries: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of the Hearth Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Burning weapon
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to ignite one Melee weapon that you're holding with fiery energy. For 10 minutes or until you use this feature again, you deal extra Fire damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) whenever you hit a target with that weapon, and each time you hit with it, you can cause it to deal its normal damage type or Fire damage.
The weapon also emits {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet.
You can end this effect early (no action required). This effect also ends if you aren't carrying the weapon.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of Warmth
entries: You and your allies have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold and Fire damage while in your Aura of Protection.

subclassFeature.name: Insulating Ward
entries: Whenever you cast a level 1+ Paladin spell with a spell slot or cast a level 1+ spell without a spell slot using a Paladin feature, you and allies within your Aura of Protection each gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the spell's level plus your Charisma modifier (minimum of 2 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}).

subclassFeature.name: Burning Spirit
entries: You can magically ignite yourself to embody your fiery soul. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you gain the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required).

entries.name: Firelight
entries: You shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} that fills your Aura of Protection and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 30 feet.

entries.name: Fleet of Foot
entries: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet. In addition, you can pass through and end a move in a space occupied by another creature as if that space were unoccupied, and ending a turn in a space with another creature doesn't cause you or that creature to have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Vengeful Flame
entries: At the end of each of your turns, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you takes Fire damage equal to twice your Charisma modifier (minimum of 2 Fire damage).

subclassFeature.name: Winter Trapper
entries: {@i Employ Icy Snares to Render Your Quarry Frozen and Helpless}

Winter Trappers are masters of control and keep both their adversaries and quarries helpless and at bay. Originating from the Everglacier, these trained hunters are experts at staying upright when others would surely fall, and have learned how to use magic and acrobatics to keep both themselves and their allies out of harm's reach.

subclassFeature.name: Pin Down
entries: You learn how to keep your prey exactly where you want them. Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can cause it to take an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the attack's normal damage type and reduce the creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
When you reach Ranger level 11, this extra damage increases to {@dice 1d8}, and the target also can't make {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Winter Trapper Spells
entries: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the Winter Trapper Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Arctic Predator
entries: Your archetype's wintry history grants you the following benefits.

entries.name: Helpless Prey
entries: When you make an attack against a target that has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition while you're more than 5 feet away from it, you don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll as a result of that condition.

entries.name: Ice Walker
entries: {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} composed of ice or snow doesn't cost you extra movement, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks while in icy or snowy terrain.

entries.name: Sure-Footed
entries: You can't have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition unless you also have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

subclassFeature.name: Magic Snare
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can imperceptibly create a magical trap in the ground at your feet. The first Large or smaller creature other than you that enters that space in the next minute springs the trap and must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC; the trap can't be sprung by you or creatures you designate when you create it. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 2d8} Piercing damage, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.

A creature that can see {@condition Invisible|XPHB} objects (such as one benefiting from {@spell See Invisibility|XPHB} or {@sense Truesight|XPHB}) can see these traps. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Tripped Defenses
entries: You have learned to use a creature's mistakes against them. When a creature you can see within 5 feet of you misses you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to attempt to throw it off balance. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition; otherwise, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved until the end of the current turn unless it has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. Immediately after you do this, you can either make one attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} against that creature or move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} from that creature.

subclassFeature.name: College of Choreography
entries: {@i Inspire Your Allies and Confound Enemies with Deft Movement}

Bards of the College of Choreography tell stories and perform music without making a sound, choosing to instead move with Fey-like grace and magic to evade harm and inspire others. These dances originate from the Festerwood, whose radiant spores and light made for miraculous performances and deadly hazards. These Bards can be found anywhere from the local tavern to the highest levels of theater, moving crowds to tears as quickly as rebellion. At every turn, they inspire others to be brave, move, and join in.

subclassFeature.name: Fast Movement
entries: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet. It increases by another 5 feet when you reach Bard levels 6 (total increase of 15 feet) and 14 (total increase of 20 feet).

subclassFeature.name: Inspirational Dance
entries: You learn how to use the universal language of dance. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to dance and reinvigorate another creature of your choice who can see you.

When you do, roll your Bardic Inspiration die; that creature gains a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the number rolled plus your Charisma modifier (minimum of 2 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}). When a creature gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} in this way, it can immediately take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} or take the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action.

subclassFeature.name: Entrancing Movement
entries: Your movements become so graceful that even the most cold-hearted enemies are filled with remorse for having stopped your dance. Whenever a creature hits you with an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} or hits you with an attack roll while you're benefiting from the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action, that creature takes Psychic damage equal to your Charisma modifier plus half your Bard level (round down).

In addition, you always have the {@spell Charm Person|XPHB} spell prepared, and you can cast it without a Verbal component. With this feature, you can cast it without expending a spell slot; when you do, it is cast as a level 3 spell, and the targets don't make their saving throws with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} as a result of you or your allies fighting them. Once you cast the spell with this feature, you can't do so in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Endless Dance
entries: Your dance has become so invigorating that it can inspire an ally to attack. When a creature would gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} from your Inspirational Dance feature, it can instead choose to take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one attack using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

In addition, your endless dancing allows you to more easily avoid being hit. You can take the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, and whenever you take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to expend a Bardic Inspiration die, you can take the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action as part of that {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Astral Domain
entries: {@i Control the Flow of Space and Planar Magic}

Gods of the Astral Plane are as lost to time and space as the realm they reign over. The Astral Plane fills the gaps between the planes of existence and is an important balancing force in the cosmic ecosystem of the multiverse. Practitioners of this domain see the absence of anything as something and consider the Astral Plane as the ultimate destination of all things. These acolytes follow the ultimate path to their destination, and help shepherd others along their way in a grand mission of entropy. Clerics of the Astral Domain are chaotic by nature, but typically choose to destroy evil where they find it and hasten its inevitable journey to the Astral Plane.

subclassFeature.name: Astral Domain Spells
entries: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Astral Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Astral Domain Spells
rows: {@spell Blur|XPHB}, {@spell Guiding Bolt|XPHB}, {@spell Invisibility|XPHB}, {@spell Longstrider|XPHB}, {@spell Starry Wisp|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Planar Reach
entries: You can create and reach through brief holes in the fabric of reality. When you cast a spell that has a range of Touch, you can make the spell's range 30 feet instead. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Create Void
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a use of your Channel Divinity to open a planar tear at a point you can see within 60 feet of you, creating a powerful vacuum in a 15-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on that point. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes Force damage equal to {@dice 1d8} plus your Cleric level and is pulled up to 15 feet toward the point. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much Force damage only. The tear then vanishes.

subclassFeature.name: Spatial Exchange
entries: You always have the {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it by expending a use of your Channel Divinity rather than a spell slot. When you cast it in this way, you can choose a space within 30 feet of yourself that is occupied by a creature. If that creature is willing, you both teleport, swapping places; this effect fails if there isn't enough room for you or the creature to teleport there.

subclassFeature.name: Supreme Switching
entries: Your Spatial Exchange feature improves in the following ways:

items: Whenever you cast {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}, you can swap places with a willing creature, even if you cast it using a spell slot instead of your Channel Divinity.

When you cast {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} using your Channel Divinity, you can now choose a space within 30 feet of yourself that is occupied by an unwilling creature, swapping places with it as normal. If the creature succeeds on a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC, this effect fails and that use of your Channel Divinity is wasted.

Whenever you successfully swap places with another creature using your Channel Divinity, you can cast a level 0-5 spell with a range of Touch as part of that {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. The spell must have a casting time of an action or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, and it must target the creature you're swapping places with. The spell takes effect as you and the other creature appear in your new spaces.

subclassFeature.name: Circle of the Unbroken
entries: {@i Use the Strength of Nature Itself to Empower Your Martial Prowess}

Circle of the Unbroken is an order of Druids who have abandoned the patient teachings of their predecessors, deciding instead to take up arms in defense of the wilderness. These combative Druids form militias and harness the fury of nature itself to forcefully remove any encroaching evil that threatens their sacred lands.

While the chaotic bend of nature is found within these Druids, their bodies and impulses are tamed through training and discipline. Originally from the unforgiving Festerwood, this circle's teachings are as rigorous as the forest, blending a mixture of offense and defense to stand up to all of the world's challenges.

subclassFeature.name: Unbroken Circle Spells
entries: When you reach a Druid level specified in the Circle of the Unbroken Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Circle of the Unbroken Spells
rows: {@spell Ensnaring Strike|XPHB}, {@spell Shillelagh|XPHB}, {@spell Shining Smite|XPHB}, {@spell True Strike|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Improved Shillelagh
entries: When you cast {@spell Shillelagh|XPHB}, you can cause any Melee weapon you are holding to be imbued with nature's power; you can choose for the weapon to retain its normal damage die instead of becoming a {@dice d8}. In addition, you can use any weapon with which you have proficiency as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Druid spells.

subclassFeature.name: Wild Recovery
entries: You gain the ability to recover using the wild, bestial magic that courses through you. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape to regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d6} plus your Druid level. This healing increases by {@dice 1d6} when you reach Druid levels 10 ({@dice 3d6} plus your Druid level) and 14 ({@dice 4d6} plus your Druid level).

subclassFeature.name: Shillelagh Mastery
entries: While you're wielding a weapon under the effect of Shillelagh, you can attack twice with the weapon instead of once whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn. In addition, you gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with that weapon; this bonus increases to +2 when you reach Druid level 10 and +3 when you reach Druid level 14. If the weapon already granted a bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls, you must choose whether to use that bonus or the one granted by this feature when attacking with it.

subclassFeature.name: War Druid
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can replace one of the attacks with a casting of one of your Druid cantrips that has a casting time of an action.

subclassFeature.name: Nature Armor
entries: Your connection to nature helps to shield you from harm. You're always under the effect of the {@spell Barkskin|XPHB} spell.

In addition, at the start of each of your turns, you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half your Druid level (round down). When you assume a Wild Shape form, you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Druid level plus half your Druid level (round down).

subclassFeature.name: Materializer
entries: {@i Easily Create and Destroy Matter as a Master Artisan and Arcanist}

Some Wizards abandon the limitations set by magic schools and instead focus on the binding force that holds everything together. These mages spend their efforts weaving and recreating matter from thin air to create art as well as utility. They value the craftsmanship taught by artisans around the world and consider their skills and creations to be a form of magic all its own.

Some arcanists focus on altering the living, whereas Materializers focus on creating and enhancing the inanimate. These mages see beauty in the possibility presented by raw materials and are found as readily studying ancient lore as they are in the center of a bustling forge. Many followers of this tradition take great care and pride in their Component Pouches and find great kinship among Bards, artists, and other craftsmen.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Artisan
entries: You have dedicated your studies to creation and the magic surrounding it. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Bonus Cantrip
entries: You know the {@spell Mending|XPHB} cantrip. If you already know it, you learn a different Wizard cantrip of your choice. The cantrip doesn't count against your number of cantrips known.

entries.name: Creation Savant
entries: You gain proficiency with three {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace your proficiency with one type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} with a different type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice.

entries.name: Mage Craft
entries: Your exceptional wit and aptitude allow you to do more with less time. While crafting using a set of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} with which you have proficiency, you can subtract your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1) from the number of hours you must work each day, allowing you to complete a full day's work in 7 hours or less (to a minimum of 2 hours).

subclassFeature.name: Minor Creation
entries: When you cast a level 1+ spell, you can magically create a {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} of inanimate material of your choice that is no larger than 5 feet on a side. The {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} appears in an unoccupied space you can see on the ground within 30 feet of you, or it appears magically connected to another {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} within that range that you created with this feature. Once you reach Wizard level 10, the range increases to 60 feet. A {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} created in this way weighs a number of pounds equal to 5 times its length on a side, regardless of its material. The {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cubes} can support up to 10 times their weight, potentially serving as a bridge, and they can provide cover for creatures behind them.

You can have a total number of these {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cubes} in existence at a time equal to your Wizard level. You can dismiss any number of the {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cubes} of your choice as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Otherwise, a {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} disappears after 1 hour or if it takes any damage. The {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cubes} are objects that have an AC equal to 10 + your Intelligence modifier, 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}, and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Force damage.

entries.name: Cube Detonation
entries: When you take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to dismiss one or more of the {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cubes}, you can choose to detonate any of them instead, destroying them. Each creature within 10 feet of a detonated {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. A creature takes Force damage equal to {@dice 1d6} + half your Wizard level (round down) on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. You can detonate a total number of {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cubes}—all at once or with multiple separate {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB|Bonus Actions}—equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1), and you regain all expended uses of doing so when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore uses of it by expending a level 1+ spell slot (no action required), regaining a number of uses equal to the slot's level.

subclassFeature.name: Material Enhancement
entries: Your attention to both magic and mundane detail gives you a heightened understanding and control over them. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch a weapon, suit of armor, or other object. Until the end of your next {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, the object becomes magical if it isn't already, and gains your choice of up to two of the following benefits:

items: (Weapon Only) The wielder of the weapon gains a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with it; this bonus increases to +2 when you reach Wizard level 10 and +3 when you reach Wizard level 14. If the weapon already granted a bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls, the wielder must choose whether to use that bonus or the one granted by this feature when attacking with it.

(Armor Only) The wearer of the armor gains a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}; this bonus increases to +2 when you reach Wizard level 10 and +3 when you reach Wizard level 14. If the armor already granted a bonus to AC, the wearer must choose whether to use that bonus or the one granted by this feature when calculating its {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}.
(Armor Only) The armor doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks if it normally would.

The object sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, a creature holding the object can cause it to stop or resume shedding this light.

The object's weight is halved. If the object is a suit of armor, it doesn't have a Strength requirement if it normally would. If it's a weapon with the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|Heavy} property, it doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the wielder's attack rolls as a result of that property.
When a creature throws the object, it flies back to the creature's hand immediately after hitting or missing its target.
You change any of the object's colors, smells, and textures.

entries: You can use this feature twice, expending one use for each benefit you give an object. You regain all expended uses of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
