Once you give an object a benefit in this way, you can't give that benefit to an object again until you regain your expended uses of this feature.

subclassFeature.name: Dematerialize
entries: Your spells deal double damage to objects and structures, and whenever you deal damage to a Construct, it takes an extra {@damage 1d8} Force damage.

subclassFeature.name: Rematerialize
entries: When you see a Large or smaller nonmagical object or structure be destroyed within 60 feet of you, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cause its debris to magically vanish. Once within the next 8 hours, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to recreate the item, causing it to reappear intact in an unoccupied space you choose within 60 feet of you.

If the item is recreated in the air, it immediately falls. Any creature below it must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes Bludgeoning damage for every 10 feet the item fell before reaching it. The amount of damage dealt is based on the item's size: Small or smaller ({@dice 1d6}), Medium ({@dice 2d6}), or Large ({@dice 3d6}). On a successful save, a creature takes half as much Bludgeoning damage. Unless the object is at least one size smaller than a creature it falls on, that creature is pushed to the nearest unoccupied space.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Astral Griffon Patron
entries: {@i Control Your Own Extradimensional Space}

You have made a pact with a powerful demigod of the Astral Plane named Criir: a griffon whose control over space, creation, and pocket dimensions extends throughout the multiverse. They aim to expand their eclectic collection of treasures, both magical and mundane. These treasures may be worth very little in the average shop, but to the Astral Griffon, they hold a special place in history or the fabric of existence. Your binding may lead you far and wide in search of foreign Artifacts as quickly as it would a nearby pub for a tarnished locket. Regardless of the task, the Griffon's aims are rarely subject to distinctly good or evil motives.

subclassFeature.name: Astral Griffon Spells
entries: The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Warlock level specified in the Astral Griffon Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Astral Griffon Spells
rows: {@spell Identify}, {@spell Locate Object}, {@spell Magic Missile}, {@spell Rope Trick}
{@spell Mordenkainen's Private Sanctum}, {@spell Leomund's Secret Chest}

subclassFeature.name: Astral Missile
entries: You can cast {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB} without expending a spell slot a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. When you cast it in this way, the darts can target a creature you can't see; if the target isn't within range, the dart flies haphazardly before vanishing through an instantaneous planar tear.

subclassFeature.name: Extradimensional Acuity
entries: Your patron grants you the ability to maintain a small pocket dimension. This extradimensional space is always accessible to you and can hold up to 50 pounds of inanimate material, not exceeding a volume of 1 cubic foot. You can access and interact with this space using your hands the same way you would with a normal container. Your hands disappear within the space while they're accessing it.

Placing a {@item Bag of Holding|XDMG}, {@item Heward's Handy Haversack|XDMG}, {@item Portable Hole|XDMG}, or similar item inside your pocket dimension instantly destroys that item, and the contents of the two extradimensional spaces are scattered in the Astral Plane. When this happens, you are unable to access your pocket dimension again until 7 days have passed.
In addition, you can sense whether extradimensional spaces (other than the one given to you by your patron) are present within 60 feet of you. This feature doesn't reveal their location or number.

subclassFeature.name: Planar Escape
entries: You can hide yourself in a temporary extradimensional space to avoid harm. When you take damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to halve that damage against you and disappear into a harmless demiplane, which overlaps with your current plane. You can perceive the plane you left and move normally while in this demiplane, but you are imperceptible to creatures on the overlapped plane. You remain in this demiplane until the end of your next turn or until you choose to return to the plane you left (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Extradimensional Mastery
entries: The fabric of existence is clear to you. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Astral Clarity
entries: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to enhance your perception of reality. For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks that rely on sight, and you have {@sense Truesight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Magical Snoop
entries: While you're within 10 feet of a magic item that contains an extradimensional space, you can sense what objects are stored within it.

entries.name: Resistance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Force damage.

subclassFeature.name: Pocketeer
entries: You've learned how to mold extradimensional spaces beyond their normal limits. Your pocket dimension can now hold up to 250 pounds of inanimate material, not exceeding a volume of 10 cubic feet.

In addition, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to target an object you can see within 30 feet of yourself that isn't being worn, shunting it into your pocket dimension; if there isn't enough space in your pocket dimension for the object, this effect fails and your action is wasted. If the object is being carried by a creature, that creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC to avoid this effect. The object remains in your pocket dimension until you retrieve it. If you don't retrieve the object before the end of your next turn, it reappears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you or in your hands if you have any free (your choice each time). Once you use this effect to target an object that is being carried by a creature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Warrior of the Celestial
entries: {@i Divine Answers, Support Allies, and Smite Foes with Holy Energy}

Monks of the Way of the Celestial are peacekeepers and protectors of their chosen kin. These monks meditate to achieve a greater understanding of a divine being, granting them the ability to channel celestial energies into their own. These martial defenders are pensive, patient, and understanding, but they offer little mercy when evil intentions are made clear.

The Way of the Celestial originated from the lost city of Hearth as a tribute to the city's guardian couatl protectors. While these teachings are far from common knowledge, there's no telling what small, protected monasteries may still exist after the city's untimely destruction. Regardless of how you come upon this tradition, the wisdom of the celestials guides its every principle.

subclassFeature.name: Celestial Connection
entries: You can speak telepathically to any creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. You don't need to share a language with the creature for it to understand your telepathic communications, but the creature must be able to understand at least one language. Your communication doesn't give the creature the ability to respond to you telepathically.

subclassFeature.name: Soul-Searching Strike
entries: When you touch a creature or hit it with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend 1 Focus Point (no action required) to peer into its soul. Until the end of your next turn, or for 1 minute if you aren't in combat, you are aware of the target's emotions and its most obvious desire. At the GM's option, you may also discern its current number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or one piece of the target's history. In addition, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next attack roll you make against the target before the end of your turn, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) checks made to interact with the target until this effect ends.
Whenever you hit a creature with an attack granted by your Flurry of Blows, you can use this feature without expending a Focus Point.

subclassFeature.name: Stabilizing Focus
entries: You can harness and channel healing energy. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch a {@status Bloodied|XPHB} creature and expend 1 or more Focus Points. For each Focus Point you expend, the creature regains 5 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. This feature can restore a creature to no more than half its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum.

In addition, you can expend 2 Focus Points to cast one of the following spells without a spell slot or Material component: {@spell Enhance Ability|XPHB}, {@spell Lesser Restoration|XPHB}, or {@spell Protection from Evil and Good|XPHB}. Your spellcasting ability for these spells is Wisdom.

subclassFeature.name: Celestial Emissary
entries: Your connection to Celestials can be used to expand your mind's reach. You always have the {@spell Dream|XPHB} and {@spell Scrying|XPHB} spells prepared. Your spellcasting ability for them is Wisdom, and with this feature, you can cast them without expending a spell slot or needing Material components. Whenever you use this feature to cast {@spell Dream|XPHB} without expending a spell slot, only you can act as the dream messenger. Once you cast a spell with this feature, you can't cast that spell in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

In addition, when you make an attack roll with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} and hit, you can cause the target to take extra Radiant damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die. You can deal this extra damage a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Heavenborne
entries: Your energy is perfectly intertwined with both harmful and healing Celestial magic. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Stabilizing Focus
entries: You can use your Stabilizing Focus as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. In addition, when you use your Stabilizing Focus, you can target a {@status Bloodied|XPHB} creature up to 30 feet away from you instead of one you touch.

entries.name: Limited Flight
entries: You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. This benefit only works in short bursts; you fall if you end your turn in the air and nothing else is holding you aloft.

entries.name: Soul Strike
entries: Your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes} deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Radiant damage on a hit.

subclassFeature.name: Couatl Herald
entries: {@i Show Mercy to Enemies and Support Allies as a Paragon of Civility and Restraint}

The Couatl Heralds were, or are, martial warriors whose intention was to neutralize threats without always resorting to deadly force. These combatants were a beloved symbol of peace in the once great city of Hearth, whose terrible destruction has been all but entirely lost to history. Perhaps you come from a long line of sequestered heralds who had escaped the city before its collapse, or maybe you found the age-old journals of one of the city's training officers. Regardless of how you come across this knowledge, training, and virtue, a true Couatl Herald defends the lives of all, regardless of their transgressions.

subclassFeature.name: Agent of Mercy
entries: You harbor a wellspring of positive magic within yourself. It is represented by your Mercy Dice, which fuel features you have from this subclass. The Mercy Dice table shows the die size and number of these dice you have when you reach certain Fighter levels.
A Mercy Die is expended when you use it. You regain all expended Mercy Dice when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Benevolent Presence
entries: When you make a Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) check or Charisma ({@skill Performance|XPHB} or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check, you can expend one or more Mercy Dice and add those dice to the roll.

entries.name: Merciless Strike
entries: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with an attack roll using a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can expend a Mercy Die to deal extra Radiant damage to the target equal to the Mercy Die roll.

entries.name: Peaceful Ward
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a Mercy Die to gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Mercy Die roll plus your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB|Temporary Hit Point}).

subclassFeature.name: One of the People
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Insight|XPHB} and {@skill Persuasion|XPHB} skills. In addition, when you would reduce a creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} with a melee or ranged attack, you can choose to knock it out, instead reducing the creature to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}. The creature then has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition and starts a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}. The creature remains {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} until it regains any {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or until someone uses an action to administer first aid to it, which requires a successful DC 10 Wisdom ({@skill Medicine|XPHB}) check.

subclassFeature.name: Peacebringer
entries: Whenever you deal damage with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, it can deal your choice of Radiant damage or its normal damage type.

In addition, you always have the {@spell Calm Emotions|XPHB} and {@spell Sanctuary|XPHB} spells prepared. Your spellcasting ability for them is Charisma, and you can cast them without needing Somatic or Material components. With this feature, you can cast each of these spells without expending a spell slot. Once you cast a spell with this feature, you can't cast that spell in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Calm Mind
entries: Whenever you successfully persuade a creature to stand down or deescalate an otherwise violent situation, you regain one expended Mercy Die. If multiple creatures are persuaded in this way, you regain one expended Mercy Die for each affected creature.
In addition, you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions.

subclassFeature.name: Paragon
entries: You've learned how to lead your allies using magical words of empowerment. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can command a creature you can see within 30 feet of you and expend a Mercy Die. If the creature can hear you, it gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Mercy Die roll plus your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB|Temporary Hit Point}), and it can immediately take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} and make one attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
If damage dealt by an attack made in this way would reduce a creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, that creature is magically knocked out instead.

subclassFeature.name: Heraldic Enforcer
entries: Your training has taught you to strike with the magic and force of a mighty couatl. You can use your Merciless Strike once on each of your turns without expending a Mercy Die. If you do so, you can use your Merciless Strike again this turn, expending a Mercy Die as normal.

In addition, if you choose to knock a creature out instead of killing it when you would reduce it to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the target magically remains {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} for 8 hours or until you or an ally takes an action to shake it awake, even if the target regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or someone uses an action to administer first aid to it.
